note: Eponymous school founder; single sūtra text, no verse/commentary conflation. Companion to Āśvalāyana-Śrautasūtra.

title: Kauśika-Sūtra
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Vedic Literature. Samhitas and Brahmanas_Jan Gonda, 1975.txt
note: Atharvaveda (Śaunaka) Kalpasūtra; traditional ascription to Kauśika, historically uncertain. No multi-author structure in the edition.

title: Vārāha-Gṛhyasūtra
note: School-affiliated, genuinely anonymous; traditional label 'Vārāha' is a śākhā name, not a personal ascription.

title: Śāṅkhāyana-Śrautasūtra
note: Eponymous reputed author; doctrine traced to Kauṣītaki teacher-lineage. Single śrauta sūtra, no verse/commentary conflation.

title: Kauthuma-Gṛhyasūtra
note: Anonymous, Kauthuma-śākhā label only; not to be conflated with Gobhila-Gṛhyasūtra (the main Kauthuma gṛhya manual). Single text, no commentary layer.

title: Āpastamba-Śulbasūtra
note: Eponymous ascription to Āpastamba (geometry/altar-construction sūtra). GRETIL etext is the bare sūtra extracted from a commented version; the commentary (e.g. Kapardisvāmin/Sundararāja) is NOT included, so no conflation in this record.

title: Śāṅkhāyana-Gṛhyasūtra
note: Eponymous/legendary teacher, not a securely individual historical author; school text of the Kauṣītaki/Śāṅkhāyana Ṛgvedins. Closely parallel to the Kauṣītaki-Gṛhyasūtra.

title: Vaitāna-Śrautasūtra [Vaitānasūtra]
primary_author: anonymous (Atharvaveda Śaunaka Śrautasūtra); commentary Ākṣepānuvidhi by Somāditya
note: Edition/file conflates two distinct authorial layers: anonymous Atharvavedic root Śrautasūtra + Somāditya's commentary Ākṣepānuvidhi. Root not attributable to a real person (eponymic/first-word name).

title: Āśvalāyana-Śrautasūtra
primary_author: Āśvalāyana (traditional eponym, Ṛgveda; pupil of Śaunaka)
note: Eponymous teacher of the Āśvalāyana Ṛgvedic school; traditional ascription, not a documented individual author. No commentary bundled in this file.

title: Aṣṭādhyāyī
note: Editorial provenance: sūtra-pāṭha lifted from the Kāśikā commentary, not a directly transmitted Pāṇini MS; this is a text-constitution note, not a change of authorship. Sūtras securely Pāṇini's. (paninipu = sūtras in text order.)

title: Aṣṭādhyāyī
note: Duplicate of the Aṣṭādhyāyī, alphabetically/index-ordered variant (paniniiu). Sūtras securely Pāṇini's; Kāśikā only the source of the extracted text. Text-constitution note only.

title: Āpastamba-Gṛhyasūtra
note: Single title conflates three distinct authorial layers: traditional/eponymic Āpastamba mūla + Haradatta's Anākulā(vṛtti) + Sudarśanācārya's Gṛhyatātparyadarśana(vyākhyā). Root attribution is traditional, not a documented individual.

title: Kaivalya-Upaniṣad
note: Colophon ascribes to Atharvaveda, no human author. Genuinely anonymous; a late Śaiva Upaniṣad.

title: Jaimini-Gṛhyasūtra
current_author: attributed to the Jaiminīya school (trad. Jaimini)
primary_author: anonymous (Jaiminīya school of the Sāmaveda; traditionally ascribed to Jaimini)
source: secondary:cleaned_Feeding the Dead. Ancestor Worship in Ancient India_Matthew R. Sayers (2013).txt
note: Jaiminīya/Sāmaveda school composition; 'Jaimini' is an eponymous/traditional founder ascription, not a verified author.

title: Śira-Upaniṣad [Atharvaśira-Upaniṣad]
note: Colophon ascribes to Atharvaveda; genuinely anonymous Śaiva-leaning Atharvan Upaniṣad.

title: Kaṭha-Upaniṣad
primary_author: anonymous (Kaṭha/Kāṭhaka school of the Black Yajurveda)
note: Anonymous; associated with the Kaṭha (Kāṭhaka) school. Naciketas is a dramatis persona, not the composer.

title: Īśa-Upaniṣad [Īśāvāsyopaniṣad]
primary_author: anonymous (final adhyāya of the Vājasaneyi-Saṃhitā, Śukla Yajurveda)
note: Mūla is anonymous (= VS 40). Śaṅkara is a later commentator (bhāṣya), distinct from the root text and outside this item's scope; no conflation in the mūla itself.

title: Śvetāśvatara-Upaniṣad
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally ascribed to the sage Śvetāśvatara)
note: Traditional eponymous ascription to sage Śvetāśvatara (self-named at 6.21); a Black Yajurveda theistic Upaniṣad, effectively anonymous in authorship.

title: Śvetāśvatara-Upaniṣad
note: Eponymous sage Śvetāśvatara named in 6.21 is a traditional/legendary ascription; actual composition anonymous. GRETIL file is mūla only, no commentary.

title: Āpastamba-Dharmasūtra
current_author: anonymous/traditional (Āpastamba school of the Kṛṣṇa Yajurveda)
note: Traditional eponymous ascription to Āpastamba (school founder); text is a corporate sūtra of the Āpastamba caraṇa. Mūla only on GRETIL.

title: Īśa-Upaniṣad [Īśāvāsyopaniṣad]
note: Single title conflates anonymous mūla Upaniṣad + Śaṅkara's bhāṣya (commentary). Colophon ascribes bhāṣya to Śaṅkara (traditional but standard ascription). Roles separated.

title: Vaiśeṣikasūtra
primary_author: Kaṇāda (traditional; alias Ulūka, gotra Kaśyapa) — composite redaction
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Traditional/legendary ascription to sage Kaṇāda (Ulūka, atom-eater). Extant text is a composite redaction (c. turn of CE), so single-author attribution is conventional, not historical. GRETIL file is mūla only — Candrānanda's vṛtti from the edition is NOT included.

title: Ṛgvidhāna
note: Traditional ascription to Śaunaka (Vedic school eponym). Metrical manual of Ṛgvedic mantra-application; regarded as later/post-Vedic. No individual historical author securely established.

title: Gautama-Dharmasūtra
note: Traditional eponymous ascription to Gautama; no historical individual author. GRETIL note explicitly: no formal colophon naming him. Mūla only.

title: Baudhāyana-Dharmasūtra
current_author: anonymous/traditional (Baudhāyana school of the Taittirīya Yajurveda)
primary_author: anonymous (Baudhāyana school of the Taittirīya/Kṛṣṇa Yajurveda)
note: Traditionally ascribed to the sage/teacher Baudhāyana (school eponym); composite school redaction. This e-text is mūla only; Govinda Svāmin's Vivaraṇa not included.

title: Arthaśāstra
source: secondary:cleaned_Buddhism in the Shadow of Brahmanism_Johannes Bronkhorst, 2011.txt
note: Traditional single author Kauṭilya disputed; text is a composite stratified work (Trautmann statistical analysis). Conflation: nominal author vs. earlier sourced ārya-teachers vs. later redactor.

title: Gautama-Dharmasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (mūla, ascribed to Gautama); Maskarin (Maskarī) — bhāṣya
note: current_author records only Gautama; this e-text conflates anonymous mūla (ascribed Gautama) + Maskarin's bhāṣya. Colophon names Maskarin. Mislabeled 'Gautama-Gṛhyasūtra' on Sansknet.

title: Dharmaskandha
current_author: anonymous/traditional (ascribed to Śāriputra in Sanskrit/Tibetan sources, to Maudgalyāyana in Chinese sources)
primary_author: traditional: Śāriputra (Sanskrit/Tibetan tradition) / Maudgalyāyana (Chinese tradition); actually anonymous Sarvāstivāda Abhidharma
source: secondary:cleaned_Vasubandhu's Abhidharmakosa and the Commentaries Preserved in the Tanjur_Marek Mejor, 1991_OCR.txt
note: Two divergent traditional ascriptions (Śāriputra vs Maudgalyāyana) for a single anonymous canonical Abhidharma text — competing legendary attributions, not multiple historical authors of distinct parts, so conflation_flag=false.

title: Praśna-Upaniṣad
note: current_author=anonymous omits the Śaṅkara bhāṣya present in THIS file (the 'bu' variant). Conflates anonymous mūla + Śaṅkara commentary.

title: Praśna-Upaniṣad
primary_author: anonymous (mūla Upaniṣad of the Atharvaveda, Pippalāda school)
note: Mūla-only variant (contrast prasupbu which adds Śaṅkara's bhāṣya). Genuinely anonymous; no conflation.

title: Māṇḍūkya-Upaniṣad
source: gretil-header + content; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Title says 'Upaniṣad' but file interleaves the anonymous Upaniṣad with Gauḍapāda's kārikās (29 vv. = Āgama-prakaraṇa). Upaniṣad proper is anonymous Vedic; kārikās are by Gauḍapāda (c. 6th c. CE).

title: Vāsiṣṭha-Dharmasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (Vasiṣṭha school; pseudepigraphically ascribed to sage Vasiṣṭha)
note: Eponymous/pseudepigraphic ascription to sage Vasiṣṭha; actual composition anonymous (Vasiṣṭha dharma school). No colophon naming a person.

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 4. Kiṣkindhākāṇḍa
note: Traditional single-author ascription to Vālmīki; text is composite/redacted. This book (4) belongs to the older core, not the later-added first/last kāṇḍas.

title: Mahābhārata: Udyogaparvan
note: Traditional ascription to (legendary) Vyāsa; actually anonymous/composite epic redacted over centuries.

title: Mahābhārata: Sabhāparvan
note: Same as other parvans: traditional/legendary ascription to Vyāsa, composite anonymous epic.

title: Mahābhārata: Śalyaparvan
note: Traditional/legendary ascription to Vyāsa; composite anonymous epic redacted over centuries.

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 3. Araṇyakāṇḍa
note: Traditional single-author ascription to Vālmīki retained; scholarly view = composite oral-poetic transmission, not a single historical author. Not a conflation of named authors, so conflation_flag=false; redactional layering noted.

title: Mahābhārata: Āraṇyakaparvan
note: Vyāsa is the legendary/traditional author; actual authorship anonymous and composite. Single parvan of one epic — not a conflation of distinct named authors.

title: Mahābhārata: Karṇaparvan
note: Same as other Mbh parvans: legendary author Vyāsa, real authorship anonymous/composite. Not a conflation of distinct named authors.

title: Māṇḍūkya-Upaniṣad
note: Verified the file contains only the anonymous Upaniṣad mula, NOT Gauḍapāda's Mānḍūkyakārikā/Āgamaśāstra — so no conflation here. The full commented work would conflate (anonymous Upaniṣad + Gauḍapāda's kārikās + Śaṅkara's bhāṣya), but the GRETIL text is mula only.

title: Mahābhārata: Ādiparvan
note: Current_author already captures the dual traditional-Vyāsa / anonymous-composite picture. Ādiparvan (Book 1) is the frame book; legendary author Vyāsa, real authorship anonymous. Not a conflation of distinct named authors.

title: Aṣṭasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā [Aṣṭasāhasrikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna sūtra (canonically 'spoken by the Buddha' / pseudo-buddhavacana but treated as anonymous composite). Edition pairs it with Haribhadra's Āloka, but the e-text is the root sūtra alone — no author conflation in the file itself. Restored missing portion from Karashima noted as editorial, not authorial.

title: Mahābhārata: Droṇaparvan
note: Traditional single-author ascription to Vyāsa is legendary; actual text is an anonymous composite. Critical edition redactor: S. K. De (BORI).

title: Patna Dharmapada
source: secondary:cleaned_Almogi_ed._Evolution of scriptures, formation of canons_the Buddhist case.txt
note: No author; canonical anthology. Skilling (1997) ascribes school to Sāṃmatīya; ms. discovered by Rāhula Sāṅkṛtyāyana in Tibet, ~12th-c. Eastern Indian script. Editor Cone; transcriber Tamai.

title: Mahābhārata: Virāṭaparvan
note: Vyāsa ascription legendary; anonymous composite. Critical edition redactor: Raghu Vira / Utgikar (BORI).

title: Mahābhārata: Bhīṣmaparvan
note: Vyāsa ascription legendary; anonymous composite. Bhagavadgītā embedded here (also anonymous, traditionally Vyāsa). Critical edition redactor: S. K. Belvalkar (BORI).

title: Abhisamācārikadharma
note: Anonymous Vinaya text of the Mahāsāṃghika-Lokottaravāda; no individual author. Critical ed. by Taishō Study Group; earlier ed. Jinananda.

title: Brahmasūtra
note: Conflation: e-text is sūtras (Bādarāyaṇa) extracted from Śaṅkara's bhāṣya — two distinct authors in one source file. Sūtras cite Bādarāyaṇa/Jaimini as authorities; Bādarāyaṇa traditionally = Vyāsa. Minority ascription (Sureśvara) rejected by Bronkhorst.

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 3. Araṇyakāṇḍa
note: Traditional single-author ascription to Vālmīki over composite, multiply-redacted material; not a multi-author work in the commentary sense (no bundled commentary). Critical edition is editorial reconstruction (Divanji/Bhatt).

title: Mahābhārata: Sauptikaparvan
note: Traditional intra-narrative ascription to Vyāsa over anonymous composite material. Constituted text = BORI critical edition (editorial, not authorial). No bundled commentary, so no author conflation within the file.

title: Brahmasūtra
primary_author: Bādarāyaṇa (traditional; identified with Vyāsa in tradition)
note: Sūtra text attributed to Bādarāyaṇa; traditional equation with Vyāsa is legendary. The cited edition is the Śāṅkarabhāṣya, but this GRETIL file contains only the bare sūtras (no Śaṅkara commentary) — no kārikā/bhāṣya conflation in the actual e-text.

title: Bhikṣuṇī-Karmavācanā
note: Anonymous Vinaya ordination formulary (bhikṣuṇī upasaṃpadā). Schmidt is modern editor, not author. No traditional personal ascription.

title: Mahābhārata: Strīparvan
note: Same status as Sauptikaparvan: traditional intra-narrative ascription to Vyāsa over anonymous composite material; editorial critical text. No bundled commentary.

title: Śālistambasūtra
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna sūtra; traditionally buddhavacana. No personal author. A separate commentary is ascribed to Nāgārjuna but is NOT part of this root-sūtra file (no conflation within the e-text).

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 2. Ayodhyākāṇḍa
primary_author: Vālmīki (traditional; part of the unitary core, Books 2-6)
note: Ayodhyākāṇḍa belongs to the original core (Books 2-6); traditional Vālmīki authorship, multi-hand transmission/redaction acknowledged.

title: Brahmabindu-Upaniṣad
source: secondary:Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol.1 - Potter | web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amritabindu_Upanishad
note: Anonymous minor Upaniṣad; = Amṛtabindu Upaniṣad in some anthologies; commentaries (Nārāyaṇa, Śaṃkarānanda) are separate later works, not part of this e-text.

title: Madhyamakaśālistambasūtra
note: Anonymous canonical sūtra; not by Nāgārjuna. Authored commentaries (Kamalaśīla et al.) are distinct texts, not conflated into this root e-text.

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 1. Bālakāṇḍa
current_author: Vālmīki (traditional); Bālakāṇḍa largely a later addition
note: Book 1 is largely a later accretion (with Book 7); traditional Vālmīki ascription, but scholarship treats it as later anonymous composition. Not a multi-author single-title conflation in the colophon sense, so conflation_flag=false.

title: Prātimokṣasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinaya recension; ed. Lokesh Chandra)
note: Anonymous Vinaya recension (Mūlasarvāstivāda Gilgit fragment); Lokesh Chandra is editor of the fragment, not author. No personal author exists for the text itself.

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 5. Sundarakaṇḍa
primary_author: Vālmīki (traditional; part of the unitary core, Books 2-6)
note: Sundarakāṇḍa belongs to the original core (Books 2-6); traditional Vālmīki authorship with acknowledged multi-hand transmission.

title: Nyāyasūtra 5.1
source: gretil-header + secondary:cleaned_DHARMAKIRTI'S PRAMANAVARTTIKA...Tillemans 2000.txt; cleaned_Mind, language, and world...Matilal.txt
note: current_author lists only Gautama; the file actually conflates root sūtras (Akṣapāda Gautama) with Vātsyāyana's Nyāyabhāṣya. Book 5 (jāti/nigrahasthāna) is among the strata some scholars regard as later, but here authorship roles are the salient point.

title: Kāśyapaparivartasūtra [Kāśyapaparivarta]
note: Canonical anonymous Mahāyānasūtra (Ratnakūṭa). No traditional personal author; spoken by/attributed to the Buddha in tradition. Sthiramati's ṭīkā on it is a distinct work.

title: Gurupañcāśikā
current_author: Aśvaghoṣa (traditional attribution; the tantric content postdates the famous 1st-2nd c. poet, so authorship is pseudepigraphic/uncertain)
primary_author: Aśvaghoṣa (tantric; not the kāvya poet — pseudepigraphic)
source: gretil-header(colophon) + secondary:cleaned_Literature of the Madhyamaka_Ruegg_1981.txt; cleaned_Untying the Knots in Buddhism_Wayman_1997.txt
note: Colophon ascribes to Aśvaghoṣa but this is a tantric guru-devotion text; scholarship distinguishes this 'tantric Aśvaghoṣa' from the famous poet — pseudepigraphic. Vāpilladatta wrote a commentary (not part of this verse text).

title: Saundarananda Mahākāvya
source: gretil-header + secondary:cleaned_Eltschinger_Aśvaghoṣa and His Canonical Sources II.txt
note: Securely Aśvaghoṣa (the kāvya poet); attribution supported by the poem's own colophons. Single author, no commentary conflated in this text.

title: Saundarananda
source: gretil-header + secondary:cleaned_Eltschinger_Aśvaghoṣa and His Canonical Sources II.txt
note: Duplicate work of SA_T13 from a different e-text edition; securely the kāvya poet Aśvaghoṣa. Single author.

title: Vajrasūcī
source: gretil-header(colophon) + secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism...Nakamura, 1980.txt; cleaned_Literary History of Sanskrit Buddhism_Nariman_1919.txt
note: Genuinely disputed: Sanskrit colophon = Aśvaghoṣa; Chinese version = Dharmakīrti. Even the Aśvaghoṣa ascription is likely not the 1st-2nd c. poet given later citations. Not a within-text conflation (single composition), so conflation_flag=false.

title: Saundarananda
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Single-author mahākāvya. Aśvaghoṣa's two kāvyas (Saundarananda, Buddhacarita) are the earliest extant Sanskrit kāvya. No multi-author structure.

title: Buddhacarita
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literary History of Sanskrit Buddhism_Nariman G K_1919.txt
note: This e-text = genuine Aśvaghoṣa Sanskrit, cantos 1-14 only. The spurious Sanskrit cantos 15-28 (Amṛtānanda's medieval completion) are NOT present here, so no conflation. The full Buddhacarita transmission DOES conflate Aśvaghoṣa + Amṛtānanda, but not this redaction.

title: Dramatic Fragments (buddhist)
note: Single title conflates >=2 distinct authors: only the Śāriputraprakaraṇa is colophon-attributed to Aśvaghoṣa; the other two dramatic fragments are anonymous/unidentified. Current_author already hedges, but record now separates roles explicitly.

title: Buddhacarita
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literary History of Sanskrit Buddhism_Nariman G K_1919.txt
note: Duplicate Buddhacarita encoding (cantos 1-14, genuine Aśvaghoṣa). Amṛtānanda's later completion (cantos 15-28) NOT in this file -> no conflation flag for this redaction.

title: Kāśyapaparivartasūtra [Kāśyapaparivarta]
note: Anonymous early Mahāyāna sūtra (Ratnakūṭa no. 43 in collection). Buddhavacana, no traditional named human author. Sanskrit commentary (ascribed to Vasubandhu/Sthiramati) exists separately but is NOT part of this sūtra e-text.

title: Garbha-Upaniṣad
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Hinduism and Buddhism. An Historical Sketch - Volume III_Charles Eliot, 1921.txt
note: Anonymous minor (Atharvaveda/sāmānya) Upaniṣad. Internal colophon links it to the Paippalāda recension as a 'mokṣaśāstra'; no individual or ṛṣi authorship. Sometimes traditionally grouped with Pippalāda but no secure ascription.

title: Carakasaṃhitā
current_author: Agniveśa (redacted by Caraka; later supplemented by Dṛḍhabala)
primary_author: Agniveśa (mūla compilation); Caraka (redactor, 79 chs); Dṛḍhabala (restorer/supplementer, 41 chs); Cakrapāṇidatta (Āyurvedadīpikā commentary)
note: Multi-stratum: Agniveśa (orig.) / Caraka (redaction of 79 chs) / Dṛḍhabala (supplemented 41 chs incl. last part of Cikitsāsthāna + Kalpa/Siddhi). GRETIL e-text also carries Cakrapāṇidatta's Āyurvedadīpikā commentary, a separate author. Current_author omitted Cakrapāṇidatta and did not flag conflation.

title: Nyāyasūtra
note: Traditional ascription to Akṣapāda Gautama. The sūtra text itself is layered (later additions debated by scholars), but no distinct named co-author; Vātsyāyana's Bhāṣya is NOT included in this e-text, so no conflation here.

title: Rāmāyaṇa: 2. Ayodhyākāṇḍa
note: Traditional ascription to Vālmīki. This is the critically edited text (Baroda); the Bālakāṇḍa and Uttarakāṇḍa are widely held to be later additions, but Ayodhyākāṇḍa is part of the older core. No co-author/commentary in this e-text.

title: Buddhist dramatic fragments (Turfan): Śāriputraprakaraṇa and other dramas
note: Colophon names Aśvaghoṣa son of Suvarṇākṣī for the Śāriputraprakaraṇa. The two other fragmentary dramas in the same Turfan find are anonymous (sometimes conjecturally linked to Aśvaghoṣa but not by colophon). Workitem had empty title/author; resolved.

title: Bhagavadgītā 11
primary_author: Vyāsa (traditional, mūla); with commentaries by Śrīdhara, Madhusūdana Sarasvatī, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa
note: E-text conflates the mūla (traditionally Vyāsa; really anonymous/composite epic interpolation) with four distinct historical commentators. Current_author noted Vyāsa/anonymous but did not list the commentators or flag conflation.

title: Bhagavadgītā 10
primary_author: Vyāsa (traditional, mūla); with commentaries by Śrīdhara, Madhusūdana Sarasvatī, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa
note: Same structure as BhG 11: mūla (traditionally Vyāsa; really anonymous/composite) plus four distinct commentators. Current_author did not list commentators or flag conflation.

title: Bhagavadgītā 1
primary_author: Vyāsa (traditional, mūla); commentaries by Śrīdhara, Madhusūdana, Viśvanātha, Baladeva
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Studies in honour of Walpola Rahula_Balasooriya et. al., ed (1980) (A).txt
note: Title file is a multi-commentary edition: root Gītā (trad. Vyāsa, scholarly anonymous/composite) plus four distinct commentators (Śrīdhara, Madhusūdana, Viśvanātha, Baladeva). These distinct authors are conflated under one title.

title: Bhagavadgītā 12
primary_author: Vyāsa (traditional, mūla); commentaries by Śrīdhara, Madhusūdana, Viśvanātha, Baladeva
note: Same structure as Bhagavadgītā 1: root (trad. Vyāsa / scholarly composite) conflated with four distinct commentators. Chapter 12 (Bhakti-yoga) of the same edition.

title: Prātimokṣasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (Sarvāstivāda canonical Prātimokṣa); ed. Georg von Simson
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Schlussteil des Pratimoksasutra der Sarvastivadins_Klaus T. Schmidt, 1989.txt
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya text. CORRECTION: this von Simson/Turfan edition is the Sarvāstivāda recension, NOT Mūlasarvāstivāda as the current record states. von Simson is editor (recorded as translator-role for digital edition).

title: Prātimokṣasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (Mūlasarvāstivāda Prātimokṣa); ed. Anukul Chandra Banerjee
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya text, Mūlasarvāstivāda recension (Banerjee ed.). Distinct from the von Simson Sarvāstivāda edition (prmosu_u). Banerjee recorded as editor.

title: Vimalakīrtinirdeśa [Vimalakīrtinirdeśasūtra]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna sūtra. Sanskrit edition from Potala MS (Study Group on Buddhist Sanskrit Literature). No traditional single-author ascription.

title: Vimalakīrtinirdeśa (Vimalakīrtinirdeśasūtra)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Duplicate digital encoding (TEI/corpustei) of the same anonymous Vimalakīrtinirdeśa. No named author.

title: Yogācārabhūmi: Śrāvakabhūmi
primary_author: Asaṅga (traditional, as compiler/author of the Yogācārabhūmi)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism.txt
note: Traditional Asaṅga attribution retained but flagged: Yogācārabhūmi is composite (Maitreya ascription in Chinese canon; Asaṅga as compiler/redactor; layered strata per Schmithausen). Edition is a modern Study Group reconstruction, no medieval colophon.

title: Yogācārabhūmi: Śrāvakabhūmi
primary_author: Asaṅga (traditional, as compiler/author of the Yogācārabhūmi)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Duplicate Śrāvakabhūmi (Shukla ed.). Edition title names Asaṅga, but composite/multi-stratum status and rival Maitreya ascription mean attribution is disputed, not secure.

title: Pāṃśupradāna-avadāna
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Deeg_Legend and cult.txt; secondary:cleaned_Translation and Study of the Purnavadana_Tatelman, 1988.txt
note: Anonymous; opening 'Avadāna of the Donation of Dirt' of the Aśoka-cycle within the Divyāvadāna / Mūlasarvāstivāda corpus. No personal author.

title: Bhaiṣajyavastu
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Survey of Vinaya Litterature_Prebish, 1994.txt; secondary:Bones, Stones and Buddhist Monks_Schopen, 1997.txt
note: Anonymous; a vastu of the Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinaya, compiled corporately within the saṅgha, no individual author.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 2 (with Śaṅkara's Śārīrakamīmāṃsābhāṣya)
note: Two distinct authors: root sūtra Bādarāyaṇa (traditional Vedānta-sūtra author), commentary Śaṅkara. Bādarāyaṇa's authorship is itself traditional; Śaṅkara's bhāṣya authorship secure. Correctly conflated.

title: Meghasūtra
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna 'Great Cloud' sūtra; Buddhavacana by tradition, no personal author. GRETIL URL returned 404.

title: Vinayaviniścaya Upāliparipṛcchā [Vinayaviniścaya upāliparipṛcchā sūtra]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Bodhisattvapitaka.  Its doctrines, practices and their position in Mahayana literature_Ulrich Pagel, 1992.txt
note: Buddhavacana sūtra; no human author. Nalinaksha Dutt is the modern editor, not author.

title: Bhagavadgītā
primary_author: anonymous/composite (traditionally ascribed to Vyāsa as part of the Mahābhārata)
note: Vyāsa is a traditional/legendary ascription for the whole Mahābhārata; the Gītā itself is anonymous composite verse. Belvalkar/Smith are editors only.

title: Prajñāpāramitāstotra
note: Attribution to Rāhulabhadra secure in the textual tradition; only his identity/dates (= Saraha?) and his floruit are debated, not authorship of this stotra.

title: Nyāyasūtra + Bhāṣya
note: One title conflates root-sūtra author (Gautama/Akṣapāda, traditional) and commentator (Vātsyāyana). current_author already separates the roles; flagged conflation. Gautama himself is a traditional/legendary attribution.

title: Pratītyasamutpādahṛdayavyākhyāna
primary_author: attributed to Nāgārjuna (disputed; probable author Śuddhamati)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 8  Buddhist Philosophy 100 to 350 A.D_Karl H. Potter (1999).txt; secondary:cleaned_Philosophical investigation of the Ch’eng wei-shih lun ..._Dan Lusthaus, 1989.txt
note: Colophon ascribes to Nāgārjuna, but attribution is controverted; Dragonetti/Lusthaus propose Śuddhamati. Treated as disputed/pseudepigraphic rather than secure. Kārikā+vyākhyāna are one author's work (auto-commentary), so not a multi-author conflation.

title: Catuṣpariṣatsūtra
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Earliest Vinaya and the Beginnings of Buddhist Literature_Erich Frauwallner_1956.txt
note: Canonical sūtra, part of Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinaya; no human author. Waldschmidt is the modern critical editor/reconstructor from Turfan manuscripts, not author.

title: Svastyayanagāthā
note: Anonymous canonical benedictory verse-text (svastyayana = rite of welfare/blessing); transmitted in Tibetan canon without authorial ascription. DSBC source_url was only the project homepage; direct page 404.

title: Bhavasaṅkrāntisūtra
note: The SŪTRA itself is anonymous canonical scripture. NB the cited edition (Aiyaswami Shastri 1938) bundles the anonymous sūtra WITH Nāgārjuna's separate Bhavasaṅkrānti-śāstra — that edition-title conflates two distinct authorial entities, but this work-item is the sūtra (anonymous), so no work-internal conflation.

title: Bhikṣuṇīvinaya
source: secondary:cleaned_Regles de Discipline des Nonnes Bouddhistes_Edith Nolot, 1991.txt
note: Anonymous composite Vinaya of the Mahāsāṃghika-Lokottaravāda; no individual author — collective/redactional school text. School ascription confirmed; not single-authored.

title: Daśasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā
note: Anonymous canonical Prajñāpāramitā sūtra (10,000-line version); Konow's text is a scholarly restoration of chs. 1-2, no authorial ascription.

title: Śatasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā II-4 [Śatasāhasrikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Anonymous canonical Prajñāpāramitā sūtra; 'II-4' is Kimura's editorial volume division, not an authorship distinction.

title: Śatasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā II-3 [Śatasāhasrikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Anonymous canonical Prajñāpāramitā sūtra; 'II-3' is an editorial volume division (Kimura), not an authorial part.

title: Mahābhārata: Anuśāsanaparvan
source: secondary:cleaned_Buddhism in the Shadow of Brahmanism_Johannes Bronkhorst, 2011.txt; gretil-header
note: Anonymous oral-composite epic book; late stratum of the MBh. Vyāsa ascription is traditional/legendary only.

title: Saṅghabhedavastu 2
source: secondary:cleaned_Bones, Stones and Buddhist Monks..._Gregory Schopen, 1997.txt; gretil-header
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya section; Gnoli is editor, not author.

title: Vaiśeṣikasūtra
source: secondary:cleaned_Philosophy in Classical India_Jonardon Ganeri, 2001.txt; gretil-header
note: Authorship by 'Kaṇāda' is a traditional sage-ascription (no independent biography). NB: GRETIL e-text contains only the sūtras, not Candrānanda's commentary cited in the edition string.

title: Yogācārabhūmi: Laukikamārga
primary_author: Asaṅga (traditional redactor); composite work of multiple authors
source: secondary:cleaned_three natures and three phases theory.txt; gretil-header
note: Composite/stratified text: traditional Asaṅga ascription overstates single authorship. Multiple anonymous Yogācāra hands over time; Asaṅga as final redactor; legendary Maitreya ascription for parts. Flagged as conflated.

title: Vajracchedikā Prajñāpāramitā [Vajracchedikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Root sūtra is anonymous. Do not conflate with the (separately authored) Vajracchedikā commentary kārikās ascribed to Maitreya/Asaṅga with Vasubandhu's bhāṣya — those are distinct texts.

title: Bhikṣuṇīvinaya
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism..._Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Regles de Discipline des Nonnes Bouddhistes_Edith Nolot, 1991.txt; gretil-header
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya of a specific school recension; Gustav Roth is the editor, not author.

title: Pañcaviṃśatisāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā I [Pañcaviṃśatisāhasrikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Anonymous Prajñāpāramitā sūtra; traditionally buddhavacana. Modern critical ed. Kimura 2007/2009. No individual author; no conflation (single sūtra text, not root+commentary).

title: Bhikṣukarmavākya
note: Anonymous Buddhist monastic ordination ritual text (karmavācanā); no composer named. Ed. Banerjee. No conflation.

title: Śatasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā II-2 [Śatasāhasrikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Anonymous Prajñāpāramitā sūtra; traditionally buddhavacana. No individual author; no conflation.

title: Mahāvadānasūtra
primary_author: anonymous ((Mūla)Sarvāstivāda Dīrghāgama sūtra; buddhavacana by tradition)
note: Anonymous canonical sūtra (the 'Great Discourse on the Lineage', on the seven past buddhas), Sarvāstivāda Dīrghāgama. Critical reconstruction by Fukita. No individual author; no conflation.

title: Suvikrāntavikrāmiparipṛcchā [Suvikrāntavikrāmiparipṛcchāprajñāpāramitāsūtra, Sārdhadvisāhasrikā prajñāpāramitā]
primary_author: anonymous (Mahāyāna Prajñāpāramitā sūtra; buddhavacana by tradition)
note: Anonymous Prajñāpāramitā sūtra (a.k.a. Sārdhadvisāhasrikā); traditionally buddhavacana. No individual author; no conflation.

title: Sarvajñatākāradhāraṇī [Karuṇāpuṇḍarīka(sūtra)]
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna sūtra; traditionally buddhavacana. The Sarvajñatākāradhāraṇī is an embedded dhāraṇī section within the Karuṇāpuṇḍarīka. No individual author; no conflation.

title: Śatasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā II-1 [Śatasāhasrikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra]
note: Sūtra, not an authored work; presented as buddhavacana. Editor Kimura is editorial role only, not author.

title: Mahāvadānasūtra
note: Reconstructed from Turfan manuscripts; complex anonymous transmission, no single author. Fukita is editor only.

title: Bhadracaryādeśanā
primary_author: anonymous; traditionally ascribed to (the bodhisattva) Samantabhadra
note: Traditional in-text speaker is Samantabhadra (legendary, not historical authorship). Effectively anonymous. Title also = Bhadracarīpraṇidhāna; concludes Gaṇḍavyūha-sūtra.

title: Dūtavākya
note: Current_author 'Bhāsa' stated flatly; status is in fact disputed/traditional (Trivandrum-plays authorship problem). Single attributed author, so no conflation.

title: Svapnavāsavadatta
note: Best-known of the disputed Trivandrum plays; attribution traditional/disputed rather than secure. Single author, no conflation.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 4 (with Vācaspati Miśra's Bhāmatī)
current_author: Bādarāyaṇa (mūla); Vācaspati Miśra (Bhāmatī sub-commentary); Śaṅkara (underlying bhāṣya)
primary_author: Vācaspati Miśra (Bhāmatī ṭīkā); Śaṅkara (bhāṣya); Bādarāyaṇa (mūla sūtra)
note: Genuine multi-author conflation (cf. Mahāyānasūtrālaṅkāra pattern): mūla Bādarāyaṇa, bhāṣya Śaṅkara, Bhāmatī Vācaspati Miśra. Current_author already lists the three roles; matches.

title: Satyasiddhiśāstra [Tattvasiddhiśāstra]
primary_author: Harivarman (original author); N. Aiyaswami Sastri (Sanskrit reconstruction from Chinese)
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt | web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tattvasiddhi | gretil-header
note: Original composition by Harivarman (c. 250-350 CE) is secure as authorship; but the extant/edited SANSKRIT is a modern reconstruction by N. Aiyaswami Sastri from Kumārajīva's Chinese (original Sanskrit lost). Roles separated: author (Harivarman) vs translator (Kumārajīva) vs reconstructor/editor (Aiyaswami Sastri).

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 3 (with Vācaspati Miśra's Bhāmatī)
current_author: Bādarāyaṇa (mūla); Vācaspati Miśra (Bhāmatī sub-commentary); Śaṅkara (underlying bhāṣya)
note: Three distinct authors across textual layers. Bādarāyaṇa's authorship of the sūtras is itself traditional/legendary; Śaṅkara and Vācaspati Miśra attributions are secure. The Bhāmatī is the Adhyāya-3 layer foregrounded here.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 4 (Śaṅkara's bhāṣya with Govindānanda's Ratnaprabhā)
note: Three distinct authors across layers: sūtra (Bādarāyaṇa, traditional), bhāṣya (Śaṅkara), sub-commentary Ratnaprabhā (Govindānanda, c. 16th c.). Sister-volume to item 253 (Bhāmatī) from same printed edition.

title: Pravrajyāvastu
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya text, collectively transmitted/redacted within the Mūlasarvāstivāda school. Editors Vogel/Wille and Dutt are modern philologists (Sanskrit-fragment reconstruction), not authors. No traditional individual ascription.

title: Nāṭyaśāstra
note: Traditional ascription to the sage Bharata; widely regarded as a composite work without a single historical author. Kept as traditional (not conflated): the layering is anonymous accretion, not distinct named authors playing different roles.

title: Mahāyānaviṃśikā [Mahāyānaviṃśati]
note: Colophon ascribes to ārya-Nāgārjuna, but attribution to the historical Madhyamaka Nāgārjuna is disputed by modern scholarship; one of the many spurious/uncertain 'Nāgārjuna' minor texts. Retained as disputed rather than pseudepigraphic since no specific alternative author is established.

title: Suśrutasaṃhitā
primary_author: Suśruta (legendary core); Nāgārjuna (redactor/pratisaṃskartā); + Uttaratantra by a later hand; Ḍalhaṇa (Nibandhasaṅgraha commentary)
source: secondary:cleaned_Buddhism in Kerala_P. C. Alexander, 1949.txt | web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sushruta_Samhita | edition-metadata
note: Classic composite/conflation: legendary surgeon Suśruta (Sūtra-Sthāna etc.) + redaction ascribed to Nāgārjuna (pratisaṃskartā, per Ḍalhaṇa) + later-added Uttaratantra; this edition bundles Ḍalhaṇa's Nibandhasaṅgraha commentary (12th c.). The 'Nāgārjuna' here is the medical/alchemical Nāgārjuna, distinct from the Madhyamaka founder.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 3 (Śaṅkara's bhāṣya with Govindānanda's Ratnaprabhā)
note: Three distinct authors across textual layers. Sūtra traditionally ascribed to Bādarāyaṇa (= Vyāsa in tradition); bhāṣya securely Śaṅkara; Ratnaprabhā by Govindānanda (16th c.). Bhāmatī/Nyāyanirṇaya named in the print edition are NOT in this digital file.

title: Bhagavadgītā 16
primary_author: Bhagavadgītā (mūla, trad. Vyāsa); commentaries by Śrīdhara Svāmin, Madhusūdana Sarasvatī, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa
note: Single title bundles 4 distinct commentators on the Gītā verses; mūla anonymous (traditional Vyāsa ascription). Commentators: Śrīdhara (14th c.), Madhusūdana (16th c.), Viśvanātha & Baladeva (Gauḍīya, 17th-18th c.).

title: Bhagavadgītā 14
primary_author: Bhagavadgītā (mūla, trad. Vyāsa); commentaries by Śrīdhara Svāmin, Madhusūdana Sarasvatī, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa
note: Same structure as other Gītā-chapter files: mūla (anonymous/Vyāsa-ascribed) plus 4 distinct named commentators conflated under one title.

title: Bhagavadgītā 15
primary_author: Bhagavadgītā (mūla, trad. Vyāsa); commentaries by Śrīdhara Svāmin, Madhusūdana Sarasvatī, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa
note: Mūla (anonymous/Vyāsa-ascribed) plus 4 distinct named commentators conflated under one title.

title: Bhagavadgītā 17
primary_author: Bhagavadgītā (mūla, trad. Vyāsa); commentaries by Śrīdhara Svāmin, Madhusūdana Sarasvatī, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa
note: Mūla (anonymous/Vyāsa-ascribed) plus 4 distinct named commentators conflated under one title.

title: Mahākarmavibhaṅga
primary_author: anonymous (Buddhavacana; traditionally a discourse of the Buddha)
note: Canonical sūtra-genre text, no named author; traditional ascription is Buddhavacana. Edited/translated by Sylvain Lévi (editor, not author).

title: Pratītyasamutpādādivibhaṅganirdeśasūtra
note: Canonical sūtra; traditional ascription Buddhavacana. Vaidya is editor only.

title: Pravāraṇāvastu
note: Canonical Vinaya section; Jin-il Chung is editor/reconstructor, not author.

title: Upasaṃpadājñapti
note: Canonical Vinaya ordination formulary; editors (Jinananda; Jin-il Chung) are not authors.

title: Śayanāsanavastu
note: Canonical Vinaya section; Gnoli is editor, not author.

title: Mahāyānasūtrālaṅkāra
source: secondary:cleaned_Universal Vehicle Discourse Literature (Mahayanasutralamkara) by Maitreyanatha-Aryasanga.. (Bhasya) by Vasubandhu_Robert Thurman, 2004.txt
note: Multi-author: root kārikās attributed to Maitreya(nātha) (Asaṅga in some traditions / as redactor-transmitter), prose bhāṣya by Vasubandhu. GRETIL header alone names Asaṅga for the kārikās; the Maitreya ascription and the verse/commentary split are the key conflation. Current_author already captures the three roles.

title: Upasampadājñapti
current_author: anonymous/traditional (Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinaya tradition; edited by B. Jinananda)
primary_author: anonymous (Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinaya tradition); ed. Jin-il Chung / B. Jinananda
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya ordination formulary; modern editors Chung and Jinananda are editors, not authors.

title: Śayanāsanavastu
note: Anonymous canonical Vinaya section (vastu); Gnoli is the modern editor.

title: Nagaropamasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (Saṃyuktāgama sūtra); ed. Bongard-Levin, Boucher, Fukita, Wille
note: Anonymous canonical āgama sūtra; the four named scholars are editors/reconstructors.

title: Parāśaradharmasaṃhitā [Parāśarasmṛti]: Kāṇḍas 1 & 2
note: Title conflates two distinct authors: legendary sage Parāśara (mūla, traditional/pseudepigraphic) vs historical 14th-c. Mādhava=Vidyāraṇya (ṭīkā). Separated as roles.

title: Bhagavadgītā 18
primary_author: Vyāsa (traditional, mūla); Śrīdhara Svāmin, Viśvanātha Cakravartin, Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa (commentaries)
note: One file conflates the mūla (Vyāsa/anonymous) and three separate commentators from different centuries; separated by role.

title: Divyāvadāna
primary_author: anonymous (compiled; draws on Mūlasarvāstivāda-vinaya); ed. P. L. Vaidya
note: Anonymous compilation; an unnamed compiler assembled pre-existing (largely Mūlasarvāstivāda-vinaya) narratives. Not a single named author.

title: Sabhikasaugataśāsanapravrajyāvratacaraṇaparivarta
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Kingdom of Bamiyan...Klimburg-Salter, 1989.txt
note: Chapter of the Mahāvastu; no individual author. Traditional ascription is collective to the Lokottaravādins/Mahāsāṃghika; compiled over centuries in multiple strata. current_author 'anonymous/traditional' is accurate.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 1 (Śaṅkara's bhāṣya with Govindānanda's Ratnaprabhā)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Realism_Jadunath Sinha, 1938.txt; secondary:cleaned_World Philology...Pollock 2015.txt
note: Three distinct authors across three centuries: sūtra (Bādarāyaṇa), bhāṣya (Śaṅkara, ~8th c.), Ratnaprabhā sub-commentary (Govindānanda Sarasvatī, later medieval, Bengal). current_author already separates the roles correctly.

title: Saddharmasmṛtyupasthānasūtra
primary_author: anonymous (heterogeneous Buddhist compilation, Greater Gandhāra milieu)
source: secondary:cleaned_Stuart_Yogācāra Substrata? ...Third-Century Yoga Practitioners in Greater Gandhāra.txt
note: Editor Stuart classes it as 'authorless' and heterogeneous. Anonymous canonical sūtra. current_author 'anonymous/traditional' accurate.

title: Āryasaṃghāṭasūtra
source: secondary:cleaned_BHAISAJYAGURU-SUTRA AND THE BUDDHISM OF GILGIT_Gregory Schopen, 1978.txt; edition (von Hinüber & Kudo)
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna sūtra; framed as buddhavacana, no individual author. current_author 'anonymous/traditional' accurate.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 4 (with Govindānanda's Ratnaprabhā)
source: gretil-header (parallel brssgo1u); secondary:cleaned_World Philology...Pollock 2015.txt; secondary:cleaned_Indian Realism_Jadunath Sinha, 1938.txt
note: Three distinct authors (sūtra/bhāṣya/sub-commentary). current_author already lists all three roles; Śaṅkara's bhāṣya is the primary commentarial layer, Govindānanda glosses it.

title: Karmavibhaṅgopadeśa
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey...Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; gretil-header; web:https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/journal-of-the-royal-asiatic-society/article/abs/...Levi-1932
note: Anonymous upadeśa (commentary) on the Mahākarmavibhaṅga. The colophon name 'Bhikṣu Śrutasoma' appears in the e-text subscription but is best read as a scribal/devotional formula, not a secure authorial attribution; not in any role recognized by Lévi/Nakamura. Kept anonymous; traditional ascription noted.

title: Dharmasamuccaya [Saddharmasmṛtyupasthānakārikā]
primary_author: Avalokitasiṃha (compiler of the verses); anonymous (underlying Saddharmasmṛtyupasthānasūtra)
note: Colophon/opening: 'bhikṣuṇā avalokitasiṃhena samudbhāvitaḥ'. Two distinct roles: anonymous source sūtra (Saddharmasmṛtyupasthāna) vs Avalokitasiṃha the compiler of the verse extracts. Flagged as conflation rather than single author.

title: Tantrākhyāyika 2
source: secondary:cleaned_Pancatantra. The Book of India's Folk Wisdom_Patrick Olivelle, 1999.txt | gretil-header
note: Traditional ascription to Viṣṇuśarman is from the text's own frame-prelude; scholars (Edgerton) regard it as a literary/fictitious attribution; competing name Vasubhāga in S. recensions. Effectively anonymous; recorded as pseudepigraphic.

title: Tantrākhyāyika 1
source: secondary:cleaned_Pancatantra. The Book of India's Folk Wisdom_Patrick Olivelle, 1999.txt | gretil-header
note: Pseudepigraphic frame-attribution to Viṣṇuśarman; genuinely anonymous folk-narrative compilation. Same status as Tantrākhyāyika 2.

title: Brahmasūtra: Adhyāya 2 (with Vācaspati Miśra's Bhāmatī)
current_author: Bādarāyaṇa (mūla); Vācaspati Miśra (Bhāmatī sub-commentary); Śaṅkara (underlying bhāṣya)
primary_author: Vācaspati Miśra (Bhāmatī ṭīkā); Śaṅkara (bhāṣya); Bādarāyaṇa (mūla sūtra)
note: Genuine multi-author stratification: Bādarāyaṇa (root sūtra), Śaṅkara (bhāṣya), Vācaspati Miśra (Bhāmatī ṭīkā). Display lead = Vācaspati Miśra (the actual composing author of this Bhāmatī text). Conflation flagged; matches current attribution.

title: Bodhisattvabhūmi
primary_author: Asaṅga (traditional; as compiler/redactor of the Yogācārabhūmi corpus)
note: Sole attribution 'Asaṅga' is the traditional Indo-Tibetan ascription; East Asian tradition ascribes the YBh to Maitreya, and modern scholarship treats the YBh as a composite/compilation, so Asaṅga's role is compiler/redactor of pre-existing materials. Status downgraded to disputed; single (composite) section so no conflation flag.

title: Śarīrārthagāthā
current_author: anonymous/traditional (compiled within the Yogācārabhūmi, ascribed to Asaṅga)
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt | web:https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/yogacara/
note: Two roles conflated: anonymous canonical (āgama) verses vs the compiler (YBh-redactor, traditionally Asaṅga, low confidence) who anthologized them. Not an original composition. Conflation flagged.

title: Yogācārabhūmi: Samāhitā Bhūmi
primary_author: Asaṅga (traditional, Tibetan); Maitreya (traditional, Chinese) — composite compilation
source: web:https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/yogacara/; web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yogacarabhumi-sastra; secondary:cleaned_Silk Road Journey with Xuanzang_Sally Wriggins, 2004.txt
note: Composite text with chronological layers; traditional Asaṅga (Tib) vs Maitreya (Chin). Not securely single-authored; flagged as conflated redactional layers rather than one named author.

title: Abhisamayālaṅkāranāmaprajñāpāramitopadeśaśāstra
current_author: attributed to Maitreyanātha (Tibetan tradition: revealed by Maitreya to Asaṅga)

title: Madhyāntavibhāgaṭīkā Chapter 1
note: Three-layer Yogācāra text like the Mahāyānasūtrālaṅkāra: root kārikās = Maitreya(nātha), bhāṣya = Vasubandhu, ṭīkā (this work) = Sthiramati. Current 'Sthiramati' names only the sub-commentator; flagged as conflation and roles separated.

title: Madhyāntavibhāgaṭīkā (Yamaguchi)
note: ṭīkā is Sthiramati's, but the work as transmitted embeds Maitreya's kārikā and Vasubandhu's bhāṣya (root + bhāṣya by different authors); the ṭīkā itself is securely Sthiramati's.

title: Madhyāntavibhāgaṭīkā
source: secondary:cleaned_Jewels of the Middle Way_James B Apple, 2019.txt (Pandeya 1999 ref)

title: Avadānaśataka
note: No human author claimed; traditional ascription is to the Buddha (Sugatabhāṣita). Colophon names ācārya Thandīśvara as one who 'brought it to appearance' (prakāśitam) — possibly redactor/promulgator, not original author; insufficient to assert authorship.

title: Daśabalasūtra
note: Canonical sūtra (Buddhavacana); anonymous. Waldschmidt is modern critical editor, not author. Source_url returns wrong text (Abhidharmakośa).

title: Skanda-Purāṇa

title: Āryabhaṭīya

title: Māṇḍūkya-Upaniṣadkārikā [Gauḍapādakārikā, Māṇḍūkyakārikā, Āgamaśāstra]

Distinct from the Māṇḍūkya Upaniṣad itself (śruti).

title: Kālottara
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva Siddhānta scripture; traditionally spoken by Śiva)

title: Daśakumāracarita: Ucchvāsas 3, 5 & 6
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Ajanta. History and Development. Vol, II, Arguments About Ajanta_Walter M. Spink, 2006.txt

title: Nyāyāvatāra

title: Kāmikāgama, Uttarabhāga
note: Revealed Śaiva āgama, no named human author. Uttarabhāga is a later, separately accumulated layer than the Pūrvabhāga — a redactional stratification rather than two named authors, so not flagged as multi-author conflation.

title: Kāvyādarśa
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Companion to World Philosophies_Eliot Deutsch, Ron Bontekoe, 1999.txt

title: Tantravārttika (on the Mīmāṃsāsūtra / Śābarabhāṣya)
source: secondary:cleaned_Understanding Mantras_Harvey P. Alper, 1989.txt; secondary:cleaned_Buddhism in the Shadow of Brahmanism_Johannes Bronkhorst, 2011.txt
note: Vārttika layer securely Kumārila Bhaṭṭa; but the editions stack Jaimini's sūtra + Śabara's bhāṣya + Kumārila's vārttika (the Tantravārttika covers MS 1.2–3.; Ślokavārttika covers 1.1, Ṭupṭīkā the rest). Conflation_flag set for the multi-author layered structure.

title: Vajravidāraṇā-dhāraṇī
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna dhāraṇī (buddhavacana). Samten & Pandey are editors, not authors.

title: Pratisarāstotra
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Anonymous Buddhist protective stotra tied to the Mahāpratisarā/Pañcarakṣā cult; edition gives editor (Pandey), no author.

title: Brahmāṇḍa-Purāṇa 3
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally ascribed to Vyāsa as compiler)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Mysticism and Sacred Scripture_Steven T. Katz, 2000.txt; tradition
note: Anonymous composite Purāṇa text; Vyāsa is the standard legendary compiler-ascription for all Purāṇas, not a historical author.

title: Kaṭhināvadāna
source: secondary:cleaned_Hunter_Life of Brian Houghton Hodgson_British resident at the court of Nepal.txt; dsbc-header
note: Anonymous avadāna (story of the kaṭhina robe-offering); no traditional personal ascription found.

title: Kāvyālaṃkāra
note: Secure single authorship by Bhāmaha; internal colophon self-naming. (Note: the related Kāvyālaṃkāra-sūtra-vṛtti is a different work by Vāmana — not this text.)

title: Kāmaratnatantra
source: secondary:cleaned_Mayer_Scriptural Revelation in India and Tibet.txt; secondary:cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue of Sanskrit, Pali, and Prakrit books in the British museum.txt
note: Anonymous magical/tantric manual (kāmaratna 'sorcery' genre); no reliable personal ascription; sometimes loosely grouped with Nāgārjuna-attributed magical texts but not securely so.

title: Vajrayoginīpraṇāmaikaviṃśikā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_English_Vajrayogini_Her Visualization, Rituals, and Forms.txt
note: Anonymous 21-verse Vajrayoginī praise-stotra (GSS42); contrast neighbouring GSS43 which is by Vibhūticandra. No author named in edition or study.

title: Maṅgalāṣṭaka
note: Generic 8-verse benediction; no single named author. Title is reused across many distinct anonymous hymns (Buddhist liturgical here).

title: Mahāvākya Upaniṣad
note: Root Upaniṣad anonymous (Atharvaveda, Muktikā 108). Commentary distinctly by Upaniṣad-brahmayogin per Adyar ed.; root and commentary are different authors -> conflation.

title: Liṅga-Purāṇa 1
note: Conventional ascription to Vyāsa; genuinely an anonymous, redacted/composite Purāṇic compilation. Traditional, not secure.

title: Balimālikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue... | secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Insight Wayman | secondary:cleaned_Tibetan Studies PIATS5
note: Anonymous Buddhist tantric bali ritual (52 sections, Vajrasattva). Tibetan rendering by Zhalu Lotsāva + Dīpaṃkara (translators, not authors). NOT a grammatical/Śaiva work despite GRETIL folder path.

title: Kālānalatantra
note: Anonymous Tantra (āgama/revelation framing). No named human author; no critical edition. Tradition would frame it as spoken by Śiva.

title: Paribhāṣāvṛtti
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Tradition and Argument...Bronkhorst 2003 | secondary:cleaned_Language and Reality Bronkhorst 2011
note: Anonymous tantric paribhāṣā-vṛtti. Explicitly NOT the grammatical Paribhāṣāvṛtti of Vyāḍi/Puruṣottamadeva/Sīradeva (those are separate, named works). current_author was empty -> resolved to anonymous.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Paramokṣanirāsakārikā
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Root kārikās by Sadyojyoti (Śaiva Siddhānta); commentary (vṛtti) by Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha. Current_author lists only Sadyojyoti, omitting the distinct commentator.

title: Āryabuddhabalādhānaprātihāryavikurvāṇanirdeśanāma Mahāyānasūtra
note: Anonymous canonical Mahāyāna sūtra preserved in Gilgit mss; traditionally buddhavacana, not authored by an individual.

title: Nityakarmapūjāvidhi
note: Anonymous daily-worship ritual manual transmitted within Vajrayāna lineage; no compiler named in edition.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Mokṣakārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt | secondary:cleaned_skt-en-mono.txt
note: Root kārikās by Sadyojyoti; vṛtti by Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha (Moksakarikavrtti). Current_author omits the commentator; multi-author work.

title: mukuṭāgama
note: Anonymous revealed Śaiva āgama; traditionally ascribed to Śiva, no historical author. No published critical edition.

title: Amoghapāśakalparāja Part VII
note: Anonymous canonical Mahāyāna/kriyā tantra; divine speakers (Avalokiteśvara, Vajrapāṇi) framed as source, not a human author. 'Part VII' is a modern editorial division of the serial Taishō Univ. transcription.

title: Sūryasiddhānta
current_author: anonymous/traditional (ascribed to a revelation by Sūrya, the Sun-god, to the asura Maya)
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally revealed by Sūrya to Maya); Raṅganātha (commentary Gūḍhārthaprakāśaka)
note: Root verses anonymous (legendary divine revelation Sūrya->Maya); this edition bundles Raṅganātha's commentary Gūḍhārthaprakāśaka (AD 1603) — root vs commentary are distinct authors, hence conflated.

title: Mṛtyuñjayakalpa
note: Anonymous ritual kalpa. Traditional ascriptions for the related mantra (Vasiṣṭha; Mārkaṇḍeya) belong to the mantra, not this kalpa manual; no author for the kalpa itself.

title: Āryābuddhabalādhānaprātihāryavikurvaṇanirdeśanāma mahāyānasūtra
source: secondary:reference-literature/cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; gretil-header
note: Canonical Mahāyāna sūtra (Toh. 186), anonymous Buddhavacana; ed. Vaidya, studied by Schopen. source_url file is mislabeled (contains Daśabhūmika).

title: Vajradevīstotra
note: Buddhist hymn to the goddess Vajradevī; no author named in the stotra-saṃgraha edition or secondary literature. Anonymous.

title: Dattātreyabodha
note: Ascribed to the legendary/deified sage Dattātreya — a pseudepigraphic traditional attribution, not a historical author; actual composer anonymous.

title: Buddhastotra
primary_author: Ārya Nāgārjuna (per DSBC node); framed as Buddhavacana in colophon
note: source_url resolves to Sattvārādhanagāthā (DSBC ascribes to Ārya Nāgārjuna; colophon frames as Buddha-spoken) — title mismatch with 'Buddhastotra'; ascription to Nāgārjuna is traditional/uncertain. Two competing framings (Nāgārjuna vs Buddhavacana) but a single nominal author, so not flagged as multi-author conflation.

title: Vasudhārādhāraṇī 3 [Vasudhārādhāraṇīsūtra]
note: Manuscript colophon names scribe/patron (saṃvat 1695), not a composer. Traditionally Buddhavacana; no human author. Editor = Jaini (modern).

title: Kaularahasya
note: Colophons name only the text, no composer. Dyczkowski = digitizer/editor, not author. Revealed-scripture genre.

title: Kāvyālaṃkāra-sūtra-vṛtti
current_author: Vāmana (NOT Bhāmaha; metadata author hint appears incorrect)
note: Vāmana wrote both kārikā-sūtras and his own vṛtti (svopajña), so not a multi-author conflation. Metadata hint correct: Vāmana, not Bhāmaha.

title: Avalokiteśvarahayagrīvadhāraṇī
note: Genre = canonical dhāraṇī, no composer; traditionally Buddhavacana. No colophon author available (DSBC, no printed edition).

title: Rūpastava
note: No author named in the stotra collection; remains anonymous. Editor = Pāṇḍeya (modern, not composer).

title: Vātulottarāgama (Vātulottara)
note: Revealed Śaiva āgama; no human composer. Traditional ascription = divine revelation (Sadāśiva), recorded separately not as authorship.

title: Mahāmāyāsādhanopāyikā
current_author: anonymous/traditional (several sādhanas attributed to various siddhas)
primary_author: various siddhas (Kukkuripa; Ratnākaraśānti; et al.) — compiled in Sādhanamālā
note: Multiple distinct sādhanas under one title; one ascribed to Kukkuripa, another tradition to Ratnākaraśānti; gathered by Sādhanamālā compiler. Roles separated rather than left flatly 'anonymous'.

title: Āryatriratnānusmṛtisūtra
primary_author: anonymous (buddhavacana; traditionally word of the Buddha)
note: Anonymous canonical sūtra; traditional ascription to the Buddha (buddhavacana). Distinct from modern same-named sādhana.

title: Pīṭhastava
note: Buddhist Pīṭhastava unattributed in its edition; no human author recoverable. Beware homonymous Śaiva Pīṭhastava (Śrīnātha) — not the same work.

title: Kāvyādarśa
note: Secure single authorship; colophons name ācārya Daṇḍin. (This GRETIL file covers Paricchedas 1-2 only.)

title: Pratītyasamutpādamahāyānasūtra
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna sūtra; traditional ascription to the Buddha (buddhavacana).

title: Paratantra (Parātantra)
note: Śākta tantric scripture (āgama); anonymous/revealed, no human author. 'Paratantra' matches in secondary lit refer to the Yogācāra concept, not this text.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Tattvatrayanirṇaya
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Root prakaraṇa by Sadyojyoti (early Śaiva Siddhānta exegete, c. 7th-8th c.); the edition conflates it with Aghoraśiva's (12th c.) vṛtti, a distinct later author. Roles must be separated.

title: Mahogratārāstuti
note: No author in colophon or header; hymn to Mahogratārā. Genuinely anonymous; no traditional ascription recovered.

title: Narakoddhārastotra
source: secondary:cleaned_Nagarjuna and the Philosophy of Openness_Nancy McCagney 1997.txt
note: Traditionally ascribed to Nāgārjuna in the stotra/stava corpus but judged spurious by modern scholarship (Lindtner's non-genuine list); the Sanskrit Bauddhastotrasaṃgraha text carries no internal author. Pseudepigraphic Nāgārjuna ascription, not secure.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Tattvasaṃgraha
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Root text by Sadyojyoti; the printed Aṣṭaprakaraṇa conflates it with Aghoraśiva's later commentary. Separate the two roles.

title: Sadyojyoti: Nareśvaraparīkṣā
note: Treatise by Sadyojyoti (c. 7th-8th c.); commentary (-prakāśa) by Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha II (c. 950-1000), a distinct later author bundled in the edition. Roles separated.

title: Tārāstuti [Āryatārāstuti]
current_author: anonymous/traditional (several Tārā hymns; cf. Sarvajñamitra's Sragdharāstotra)
note: Secure ascription to Ācārya Candradāsa. The current_author note wrongly conflates this with Sarvajñamitra's Sragdharāstotra (a different work, by a different Kashmiri author); they are distinct texts. Author resolved, not anonymous.

title: Brahmasiddhi
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 3 - Potter 1981.txt
note: Root text only in this e-text; no conflation. Commentary by Śaṅkhapāṇi exists but is not part of this file.

title: Vibhramaviveka
note: Short verse treatise on the theory of error; securely Maṇḍana's.

title: Brahmasiddhi
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 3 - Potter 1981.txt
note: Duplicate edition of the Brahmasiddhi root text; root text only, no commentary conflation.

title: Santānāntarasiddhiṭīkā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Buddhist proof for omniscience. ’Sarvajnasiddhi’ of Ratnakirti_Steven B. Goodman, 1989.txt; secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Logic. vol 2_Theodore Stcherbatsky, 1930, 1984.txt
note: Two distinct authors: root kārikā by Dharmakīrti, ṭīkā by Vinītadeva. current_author omits Dharmakīrti.

title: Liṅga-Purāṇa 2,1–55
note: Anonymous compiled Purāṇa; traditional ascription to Vyāsa noted separately; narrated by Sūta/Mārkaṇḍeya.

title: Yuktidīpikā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Foundations of Dharmakirti's Philosophy_John D. Dunne, 2004_OCR.txt; secondary:cleaned_Alambana-pariksa of Dignaga…_N. Aiyaswami Sastri, 1980.txt
note: Anonymous Sāṃkhya commentary; the verses commented on are Īśvarakṛṣṇa's but those are not part of this commentary text.

title: Āryaśrīgrahamātṛkā nāma dhāraṇī
note: Anonymous Buddhist dhāraṇī; only modern digitizers named. Traditional ascription is Buddhavacana (frame device), not a historical author.

title: Nītiśāstra
source: secondary:cleaned_Taranatha's History of Buddhism in India_Debiprasad Chattopadhyaya, 1990.txt | web:https://encyclopediaofbuddhism.org/wiki/N%C4%ABti%C5%9B%C4%81stra_of_Mas%C5%ABr%C4%81k%E1%B9%A3a
note: Compiler is Masūrākṣa (colophon Masurakṣa-nīti), gathering verses from earlier śāstras. The Nāgārjuna alternative is a conflation with the distinct Nāgārjuna-nīti tradition; demoted to low confidence.

title: Sarvatathāgatatattvasaṅgraha [Sarvatathāgatatattvasaṃgraha]
note: Anonymous root yoga-tantra (compiled ~7th c., Chinese tr. 726 CE). Frame is Buddhavacana; commentators (Śākyamitra, Ānandagarbha) are distinct and not conflated into this root edition.

title: Vasudhārādhāraṇī 2 [Sucandragṛhapatiparipṛcchā/Sucandrāvadāna]
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna dhāraṇī-sūtra known via Gilgit MS; colophon lost ('End of manuscript missing'). Editors/identifiers are modern (Wille, Harrison).

title: Siddhārthacaritrakāvya
current_author: Buddhaghoṣa (Buddhaghoṣa Thera, modern Sri Lankan author)
primary_author: Buddhaghoṣa (medieval South Indian poet; namesake, NOT the Pali commentator nor a modern author)
source: gretil-header | web:https://www.academia.edu/27378019 | secondary:cleaned_Life and Work of Buddhaghosa_Bimala Charan Law,1923.txt
note: Conflates THREE distinct Buddhaghoṣas: (1) medieval Sanskrit poet 'Buddhaghoṣācārya' who actually wrote this Padyacūḍāmaṇi (~9th–10th c., S. India); (2) the 5th-c. Pali commentator (a namesake, NOT the poet); (3) the supposed 'modern Sri Lankan author' in current_author (incorrect). Real author = the medieval Sanskrit poet, otherwise unidentified.

title: Tārābhaṭṭārikānāmāṣṭottaraśatakastotra
primary_author: anonymous (108-names stotra; traditionally Buddha-spoken, revealed via Avalokiteśvara)
note: Anonymous Tārā nāma-stotra; colophon declares it Buddha-spoken (buddhaparibhāṣita), internal speaker Avalokiteśvara. No historical author.

title: Tārāsragdharāstotra
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_In Praise of Tara_Martin Willson 1992.txt
note: Single-author Kāvya hymn (37 verses) to Tārā by the Kashmiri poet Sarvajñamitra; secure traditional+scholarly attribution. The medieval Sanskrit commentary (Vidyabhusana's ed.) is a separate later text not included here.

title: Vaiśeṣikasūtraṭīkā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Philosophy in Classical India_Jonardon Ganeri, 2001.txt; secondary:cleaned_Logic, language, and reality_Matilal, 1990.txt
note: Title labels only the ṭīkā (Candrānanda) but the work conflates two distinct authors: the root Vaiśeṣikasūtra of Kaṇāda and the vṛtti of Candrānanda. Candrānanda is the genuine commentator; Kaṇāda is the legendary/founding sūtrakāra.

title: Candrajñānāgama (Candrajñāna)
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva āgama; traditionally proclaimed by Śiva)
note: Revealed (apauruṣeya in tradition) Śaiva Siddhānta āgama; no human author. Traditional ascription = revelation of Śiva. Note: in some lists 'Candrajñāna' is also cited as a Kāpālika/non-Saiddhāntika śāstra (Encyclopedia of Tantra), but the IFP/Muktabodha text is the Saiddhāntika pratiṣṭhā-āgama.

title: Grahamātṛkānāmadhāraṇī
note: Canonical dhāraṇī treated as the Buddha's word; genuinely anonymous, no human author. Traditional ascription = revelation by Śākyamuni.

title: Yogapīṭhakramodaya
note: No attestation of a named author found in any of the three source streams; treated as anonymous tantric krama/maṇḍala manual. Confidence medium only because the work itself could not be directly located/verified.

title: Śālistambakamahāyānasūtraṭīkā [Āryaśālistambakamahāyānasūtraṭīkā]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Jewels of the Middle Way_James B Apple, 2019.txt; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism_Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Colophon names Nāgārjuna, but the Nāgārjuna ascription is scholarly-disputed (pseudo-Nāgārjuna of the Madhyamaka stotra/commentary corpus); a different Śālistamba commentary is by Kamalaśīla. Surviving only via Tibetan; Sanskrit retranslated. Single ascribed author, so no conflation flag.

title: Ahorātravratacaityasevānuśaṃsāvadāna
note: Anonymous avadāna. 'Vasubandhu' inside the text is a frame-narrator (sage instructing the king), NOT the author; do not attribute to the Yogācāra master Vasubandhu.

title: Ātreyatilaka
note: Obscure title, no edition and no retrievable e-text content; author genuinely unknown. Recorded uncertain (no author invented).

title: Kamalākarasarvatathāgatastotra
note: Reject the speculative reading of 'Kamalākara' as the author's name; it belongs to the stotra's title/imagery. Treat as anonymous Buddhist stotra. (Distinct from the 17th-c. dharmaśāstra writer Kamalākara Bhaṭṭa.)

title: Veṇīsaṃhāra
note: Secure. Author self-named in the prologue. Jagaddhara is a separate later commentator (not part of this root-text record).

title: Mahādaṇḍadhāraṇīśītavatī
note: Anonymous canonical dhāraṇī/sūtra; Buddhavacana frame, no historical author.

title: Bījagaṇita
note: Secure: root Bījagaṇita by Bhāskara II (c. 1150). Commentaries (e.g. Sūryadāsa's Sūryaprakāśa, Kṛṣṇa Daivajña's Bījapallava) are separate works, not part of this record.

title: Kiraṇatantra
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Current record marks only the anonymous scripture; the e-text in fact conflates the anonymous tantra (root) with Rāmakaṇṭha's named vṛtti. Root = revealed/anonymous; commentary = Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha (c. 950-1000 CE), son of Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha.

title: Śiśupālavadha
note: Edition citation mentions Mallinātha's Sarvaṅkaṣā commentary, but the GRETIL e-text contains only Māgha's verses; no commentator conflation in the actual text. Single secure author.

title: Tārānamaskāraikaviṃśatistotra [Āryatārānamaskāraikaviṃśatistotra]
current_author: anonymous/traditional (presented as words of the Buddha; Vairocana/Kriyātantra)
note: No human author; traditional ascription = buddhabhāṣita / Vairocana per colophon. Not anonymous-unknown but traditional scriptural attribution; matches current record.

title: Mīmāṃsāślokavārttika
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Root vārttika = Kumārila Bhaṭṭa (secure). E-text conflates it with Sucaritamiśra's Kāśikā ṭīkā (edition field wrongly says Pārthasārathi's Nyāyaratnākara). Two distinct authors present in the file.

title: Jātakamālā [Ajātaśatrvavadāna]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Tatelman_The Legend of the Buddha's Wife in the Bhadrakalpāvadāna.txt
note: File compiles multiple Gopadatta stories edited by DIFFERENT modern editors (Hahn, Handurukande, Klaus) but all by the single ancient author Gopadatta — multi-editor, not multi-author. No conflation of ancient authorship.

title: Līlāvatī
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_History of Civilization of Central Asia. V. Sixteenth to the mid-nineteenth century.txt
note: Commentators (Gaṇeśa, Mahīdhara, Śaṅkara) are sources of the edition only; root Līlāvatī e-text is single-author Bhāskara II. Secure attribution.

title: Kāmadhenutantra
source: secondary:cleaned_explaining mantras and the efficacy of ritual.txt; web:https://www.wisdomlib.org/definition/kamadhenutantra
note: Anonymous revealed Tantra; no human composer. Traditionally buddha/Śiva-vacana style (uttered by Śiva). Printed Allahabad ed. lacks ch. 22 and most of 23.

title: Niśvāsakārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_The Śaiva Age.txt; secondary:cleaned_Sinister Yogis_David Gordon White, 2009.txt
note: Anonymous early Śaiva tantra; part of Niśvāsatattvasaṃhitā corpus. No author named; revealed (Śiva-uttered) scripture.

title: Amoghapāśakalparāja Part VI
source: gretil-header (dsbc); secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Anonymous Mahāyāna/Kriyā Buddhist scripture, presented as buddhavacana. Chinese transl. by Ratnacinta (639 CE) attests early circulation but Sanskrit text itself is anonymous. Title labeled Part VI; edition is Kimura Takayasu et al. transcription.

title: Harṣacarita
note: Secure single-author kāvya by Bāṇabhaṭṭa (7th c., court of Harṣa). Print edition pairs it with Śaṅkara's 'Saṅketa' commentary (a distinct, later author), but the GRETIL e-text is root text only — no conflation in this e-text.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmāṣṭottaraśatakastotra [Āryamañjuśrīnāmāṣṭottaraśatakastotra]
note: Anonymous Buddhist stotra (108 names of Mañjuśrī). Not to be confused with the Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti. Traditionally devotional, no named human author.

title: Siddhilakṣmī Ārāmika
note: Anonymous Newar Sarvāmnāya (Śākta) text in the Siddhilakṣmī/Kālīkrama tradition. No named author located; some Sarvāmnāya works are linked to figures like Mukundarāja or royal compilers (e.g. Jitāmitramalla, Pratāp Siṅgh Śāh) but none specifically attached to this title.

title: Vibhāṣāprabhāvṛtti
current_author: the anonymous 'Dīpakāra' (the Abhidharmadīpa's author; project hint of 'Vimalamitra' is unverified—proposals also include Vasumitra/Īśvaravarman)
primary_author: anonymous 'Dīpakāra' (author of the Abhidharmadīpa kārikā and its auto-commentary the Vibhāṣāprabhāvṛtti)
note: Kārikā (Abhidharmadīpa) and bhāṣya/vṛtti (Vibhāṣāprabhāvṛtti) are by the SAME anonymous author ('Dīpakāra')—an auto-commentary, NOT a Maitreya/Vasubandhu-type split. 'Vimalamitra' is only Jaini's tentative guess; not the Dzogchen Vimalamitra. Genuinely anonymous; traditional ascription = none secure.

title: Agnikāryapaddhati
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_Hindu tantric chaplains.txt; secondary:cleaned_Essais sur le rituel.txt
note: Anonymous Śaiva fire-sacrifice (agnikārya) paddhati; generic title—no individual author identified in scholarship. Confirms current 'anonymous/traditional'.

title: Aniruddhasaṃhitā
source: web:https://archive.org/details/aniruddhasamhita; secondary:cleaned_Mandalas and Yantras in the Hindu Traditions_Gudrun Buhnemann 2003.txt; web:https://www.wisdomlib.org/definition/aniruddhasamhita
note: Revealed Pāñcarātra Saṃhitā (a Divyasaṃhitā); divine 'speaker' Aniruddha/Bhagavān via Bhṛgu—not a human author. Confirms current. The name 'Aniruddha' is a Vyūha form of Viṣṇu, not the 15th-c. Sāṃkhya commentator Aniruddha.

title: Pratiṣṭhālakṣaṇasārasamuccaya
note: MEANINGFUL CORRECTION: current_author calls it 'anonymous' and a 'Buddhist iconography text'. It is actually a ŚAIVA pratiṣṭhā/liṅga-installation manual, 11th–12th c., traditionally attributed to VAIROCANA, compiled from Śaiva Āgamas (Devyāmata). Not Buddhist; not anonymous. Attribution is traditional and a compilation, hence Vairocana = compiler. (Distinct from the 'Lakṣaṇasamuccaya' that some dictionaries link to Hemādri.)

title: Kūrma-Purāṇa 2
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally ascribed to Vyāsa; framed as revealed by Viṣṇu as Kūrma)
note: Anonymous Purāṇa with the standard legendary ascription to Vyāsa; divine speaker is Viṣṇu/Śiva (Kūrma avatāra). Composite/multi-layered but no NAMED distinct human authors to separate, so not flagged as conflation. Confirms current.

title: Sāṃkhyaparibhāṣā
note: Minor late Sāṃkhya compendium; no author securely identified in available scholarship. Kept anonymous/uncertain (status 'uncertain' since a named author may exist but is unverified). No traditional ascription located.

title: Navaślokī
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Nine-verse (nava-ślokī) versified summary of Aṣṭasāhasrikā Prajñāpāramitā; author also known as Kambālāmbara/Siddhakambala, contemporary of Vinītadeva (8th c.).

title: Bhagavadāśayānusaraṇabhāṣya
note: Bhāskara's Gītā commentary; he is the c. 9th-c. Bhedābheda Vedāntin. Root Gītā verses are anonymous/traditional and not part of this attribution.

title: Sadyojyoti: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Bhogakārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Root kārikā = Sadyojyoti; commentary = Aghoraśiva, separated by ~4-5 centuries. Item conflates verse-author and commentator.

title: Hāhārāvatantra
note: Anonymous revealed Tantra (Guhyakālī/Uttarāmnāya, Śākta-Śaiva); traditionally framed as divine revelation, no human author. No traditional personal ascription found.

title: Jñānārṇavatantra
note: Anonymous Śākta/Śrīvidyā Tantra (Apte 1912 ed.). Beware homonym: Jaina Jñānārṇava is by Śubhacandra — a separate, unrelated text.

title: Mahāsattvamaṇicūḍamahārājabodhisattvāvadāna
current_author: anonymous/traditional (later versified by Kṣemendra in the Avadānakalpalatā tradition)
source: secondary:cleaned_Translation and Study of the Purnavadana_Joel Howard Tatelman, 1988.txt
note: Anonymous prose avadāna; Kṣemendra is a later versifier/redactor of the same story (Avadānakalpalatā), not author of this text. Roles separated to avoid conflation.

title: Prajñāpāramitāhṛdayasūtra
note: Heart Sutra (vistaramātṛkā/longer recension). Canonically framed as Buddha-vacana; no human author/translator named in this Sanskrit witness. Traditional ascription to the Buddha only.

title: mahāguhyakālīvidhānam
note: Anonymous Śākta/tantric vidhāna; revelatory genre framed as Śiva/goddess dialogue. No named author or editor located.

title: Kūrma-Purāṇa 1
note: Traditional ascription to Vyāsa as compiler (legendary); actual composition anonymous and accretional. Sūta is the internal narrator. Not a single historical author.

title: Uḍḍāmareśvaratantra
note: Anonymous tantra of magical rites; nominal ascription to Śiva as Uḍḍāmareśvara/Bhairava (revelatory frame). Compilatory, no single composer.

title: Tattvasamāsasūtravṛtti [Kramadīpikā]
primary_author: anonymous (Kramadīpikā vṛtti); root Tattvasamāsasūtra also anonymous (ascribed to Kapila)
source: gretil-header + secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Two distinct anonymous layers conflated under one title: the root Tattvasamāsasūtra (≤25 sūtras, trad. ascribed to Kapila) and the Kramadīpikā vṛtti commenting on it — different (both anonymous) authors. Many other commentaries on the same root exist.

title: Triṃśikāṭīkā
note: Secure single-author ṭīkā by Vinītadeva (sub-commentary). The underlying Triṃśikā kārikās are Vasubandhu's and Sthiramati wrote the bhāṣya, but THIS work (the ṭīkā) is solely Vinītadeva's — not itself conflated.

title: Vīrāgama
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva Āgama; traditionally revealed by Sadāśiva/Aghora face)
note: Revealed Śaiva Siddhāntāgama; traditional ascription to divine revelation (Sadāśiva), not a human; current attribution accurate.

title: ugracaṇḍāpūjāpaddhati
primary_author: anonymous (ritual paddhati for the worship of Ugracaṇḍā)
note: Anonymous Śākta-Śaiva ritual paddhati; no human author attributed; current attribution accurate.

title: Brahmasūtra (Śaṅkarabhāṣya)
current_author: Śaṅkara (Ādi Śaṅkarācārya); root sūtras attributed to Bādarāyaṇa
note: Conflates distinct authors by role: sūtra (Bādarāyaṇa) vs bhāṣya (Śaṅkara) vs three sub-commentaries (Vācaspati Miśra, Amalānanda, Appayya Dīkṣita). Current author named only sūtra+bhāṣya; the sub-commentators in the edition title are additional distinct authors.

title: ūrdhvāmnāyapūjāvidhi
source: secondary:cleaned_Tantra in Practice_David Gordon White, ed, 2000_a.txt; tradition
note: Anonymous Kaula ritual manual tied to the ūrdhvāmnāya transmission; no human author attributed; current attribution accurate.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: navagrahakṛtam
current_author: anonymous/traditional (framed as spoken by the Nine Planets)
primary_author: anonymous (pseudo-divine framing: composed by the navagraha / Nine Planets)
note: Devotional pseudepigraphy: ascribed to the navagraha (Nine Planets), a divine/celestial fiction rather than a real author; effectively anonymous. Current framing accurate.

title: Śikṣāsamuccayakārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_MORAL THEORY IN SANTIDEVA’S SIKSASAMUCCAYA_Barbra R. Clayton.txt; web:http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/book/268
note: Single author: both the 27 kārikās and the prose compilation/commentary are Śāntideva's; the anthologized sūtra quotations are quoted scripture, not co-authorship, so no conflation. Current attribution correct.

title: Viśvasāratantra
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; secondary:cleaned_Chinnamasta The Aweful Buddhist & Hindu Tantric Goddess_Elisabeth Anne Benard 2010.txt
note: Anonymous revealed tantra (śāstra spoken by Śiva/Devī); no human author. Traditional ascription = divine revelation only.

title: Adhyardhaśatikā Prajñāpāramitā (Adhyardhaśatikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra)
note: Canonical Mahāyāna/tantric sūtra, buddhavacana. E-text is root sūtra only; no commentary conflated.

title: Adhyardhaśatikā Prajñāpāramitā (Adhyardhaśatikāprajñāpāramitāsūtra)
note: Duplicate root sūtra (Vaidya edition). Canonical, buddhavacana; no commentary conflated.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti (per edition; title field blank in workitem)
note: Root text only in this edition: anonymous/traditional (buddhavacana via Māyājāla). Distinct from Vilāsavajra's commentary (separate work, not edited here), so no in-text conflation. Title supplied from edition.

title: Acintyaviśvasādākhyam (Acintyaviśvasādākhya)
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Śaiva Āgama/Upāgama, revealed; anonymous. Scholarship suspects later compilation/redaction from paddhatis, but no individual author is identifiable.

title: Advayaśatikā Prajñāpāramitāsūtra
note: Canonical Mahāyāna Prajñāpāramitā sūtra, anonymous/buddhavacana; no commentary conflated in e-text.

title: Bhaja-Govindam
primary_author: Śaṅkara (Dvādaśamañjarikā + 5 closing verses, traditional); 14 disciples (Caturdaśamañjarikā, one verse each)
note: Single title conflates distinct authors: Śaṅkara (core 12 + 5) and 14 disciples (1 verse each = Caturdaśamañjarikā). Whole-work ascription to Śaṅkara alone is traditional/disputed; disciple verses are traditional-only.

title: Kalaśārcanavidhi of Siddhalakṣmīdevī
current_author: anonymous/traditional (Newar Sarvāmnāya milieu; some such manuals copied/composed under Malla royal patronage)
source: secondary:cleaned_Tantra in Practice_David Gordon White, ed, 2000_a.txt | secondary:cleaned_YOSHIZAKI_Newar Buddhism.txt | tradition
note: 'of Siddhalakṣmīdevī' designates the deity worshipped, not a human author. Anonymous ritual manual; current 'anonymous/traditional' assessment confirmed.

title: Tridaśaḍāmarāpratyaṅgirāviṣayakanānātantra
note: Title's 'nānātantra' indicates a multi-source compilation of Pratyaṅgirā material; anonymous. Not attributable to one person.

title: Saptabuddhastotra
source: gretil-header(dsbc):https://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/113/882 | secondary:cleaned_Literary History of Sanskrit Buddhism_Nariman G K_1919.txt
