note: Anonymous Buddhist stotra; colophon names no author, only the title's completion. Confirmed anonymous.

title: Tattvamīmāṃsā
current_author: uncertain (a Sāṃkhya Tattvamīmāṃsā; not the modern work of Ganapati Muni)
primary_author: anonymous (root Tattvasamāsa-sūtra; traditionally ascribed to Kapila/Pañcaśikha)
source: gretil-header:https://gretil.sub.uni-goettingen.de/gretil/1_sanskr/6_sastra/3_phil/samkhya/tttvsamu.htm | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Title/URL mismatch: the file is the Tattvasamāsa (anonymous Sāṃkhya root sūtras), traditionally ascribed to Kapila/Pañcaśikha. Distinct commentators (Bhāvāgaṇeśa, Sīmānanda, Nārendra) are NOT the root author. Resolves the 'uncertain' to: anonymous root text.

title: Vikramāraccivaibhava
primary_author: Śānti Bhikṣu Śāstrī (Paṇḍita Śāntibhikṣu Śāstrī, Sāhityācārya)
note: Author was empty; colophon names Śānti Bhikṣu Śāstrī (Sāhityācārya, pupil of Dharmānanda Kosambi). Modern (20th-c.) original Sanskrit composition. Secure single authorship.

title: Yogajāgama
note: Anonymous anthology/compilation of Śaiva āgama passages; not a single-author or singly-revealed scripture. Despite āgama-style title, it is a derivative saṅgraha.

title: Buddhagadyaya [Buddhagajjaya]
source: secondary:cleaned_Bauddhavidyasudhakarah. In Honour of Heinz Bechert_ Michael Han, Jens-Uwe Hartmann, Konrad Klaus, 1997_ (A).txt
note: Late (17th-18th c.) anonymous Sanskrit Buddha-stotra; no colophon author found in collection or secondary literature.

title: Kulasāra
note: Anonymous revealed Kaula/Śaiva tantra (Śiva-Devī dialogue form); cited by Sanderson by folio. No traditional human ascription.

title: Āyurvedamahodadhau Annapānavidhi
note: Single-author medical text; colophon names Suṣeṇa as composer. Annapānavidhi = a chapter/section of the larger Āyurvedamahodadhi.

title: Vajrasatvapārājikā [Vajrasattvapārājikā]
primary_author: Anupamavajra (as author of the Ādikarmapradīpa, of which this is a section)
note: Not truly anonymous: the containing work, the Ādikarmapradīpa, is colophon-attributed to ācārya Anupamavajra (Nepalese, MS copied at Śrīyaśodharavarmamahāvihāra). The Vajrasattvapārājikā is one of its sections.

title: Vajramahākālastotra
note: Anonymous tantric praise-hymn to Vajramahākāla within Bauddhastotrasaṃgraha; no colophon author identified.

title: Mugdhopadeśa
note: Single-author satirical kāvya; colophon explicitly self-names the poet Jalhaṇa.

title: Viśālāmalavatī Pramāṇasamuccayaṭīkā
note: ṭīkā on Dignāga's root text (kārikā+svavṛtti by Dignāga); the commentary is solely Jinendrabuddhi's, so the title is not multi-authored, only a layered commentary tradition.

title: Māravijayastotra
note: Anonymous Buddhist stotra; Lokesh Chandra is modern editor only. 'traditional' qualifier dropped since no traditional ascription is attested.

title: Lokeśvarapārājikā
note: Divine/legendary ascription to Lokeśvara; no human author. Pseudepigraphic rather than plain anonymous because the title itself claims the deity as speaker/author.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: surapatikṛtam
current_author: anonymous/traditional (framed as spoken by Indra, lord of the gods)
note: Subtitle surapatikṛtam ascribes composition to Indra (a god); pseudepigraphic divine attribution, no historical author.

title: Ahorātravratakathā 1
note: Anonymous vrata-kathā; Buddha/Vasubandhu appear as in-text characters, not historical authors. Handurukande is modern editor.

title: Catuḥstavasamāsārtha [Catuḥstavasamārtha]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Minor Buddhist Texts. Part I _Giuseppe Tucci, 1956.txt
note: Composite: Amṛtākara is author of the samāsārtha (digest of the four hymns); the base Catuḥstava is traditionally Nāgārjuna's. Colophon names paṇḍita Amṛtākara.

title: Durgārcanasmṛti
note: Unverified beyond the catalog ascription; role likely compiler of a Durgā-pūjā smṛti. Low confidence.

title: Bodhyāpattideśanāvṛtti
source: secondary:cleaned_Nagarjuniana. Studies in the Writings and Philosophy of Nagarjuna_Chr Lindtner, 1987.txt
note: Traditional ascription to Nāgārjuna; explicitly doubted by Lindtner. It is a vṛtti on the Triskandhaka/confession material. Disputed, not secure.

title: Rakṣākāla(kara)stava
note: No author named in header or colophon; remains anonymous (a protective stotra). Current 'anonymous/traditional' retained.

title: Pratyakṣalakṣaṇa
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci’s Collection. Part I_Francesco Sferra, ed, 2008.txt; web:academia.edu/11836650
note: Standalone extract = chapter 2 (Pratyakṣalakṣaṇa) of the Tarkarahasya; author Vidyākaraśānti by tradition. Current author field was blank.

title: Śatagāthā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Hundert Strophen von der Lebensklugheit. Nagarjunas Prajnasataka_Michael Hahn, 1990.txt; web:academia.edu/5590945
note: Title conflates roles: Vararuci (root verses, traditionally), Vinayacandra (translator/redactor into Tibetan), Dharmaprajña (annotator). Hahn calls it 'attributed to Vararuci', hence traditional not secure.

title: Hetubinduṭīkā
primary_author: Arcaṭa (Bhaṭṭa Arcaṭa), ṭīkā on Dharmakīrti's Hetubindu; with Āloka subcommentary by Durveka Miśra
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy. An Annotated Translation of the Tarkabhasa of Moksakaragupta_Kajiyama, Yuichi, 1966.txt
note: The ṭīkā proper is by Arcaṭa (8th c.), but it is a layered work: root Hetubindu by Dharmakīrti and printed/transmitted edition includes the Āloka subcommentary by Durveka Miśra (ca. 970-1030). Distinct authorial roles; current_author captures only Arcaṭa.

title: Hetubinduṭīkā
primary_author: Arcaṭa (Bhaṭṭa Arcaṭa), ṭīkā on Dharmakīrti's Hetubindu; with Āloka subcommentary by Durveka Miśra
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy. An Annotated Translation of the Tarkabhasa of Moksakaragupta_Kajiyama, Yuichi, 1966.txt
note: Duplicate item of the same Hetubinduṭīkā (same source_url). Layered authorship: Dharmakīrti (root HB) / Arcaṭa (ṭīkā) / Durveka Miśra (Āloka subcommentary).

title: Śikṣāsamuccaya
primary_author: Śāntideva (compiler; Śikṣāsamuccaya-kārikā + prose), an anthology of quoted sūtras
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Guide to the Bodhisattva way of life_Santideva_Vesna A. Wallace & B. Alan Wallace, tr , 1997.txt
note: Single author (Śāntideva) but his role is compiler/anthologist: the bulk is verbatim sūtra extracts (many distinct, unnamed scriptures) framed by his kārikās and prose. Not a multi-author conflation in the attribution sense — the quoted sūtras are sources, not co-authors — so conflation_flag=false. Attribution to Śāntideva is secure.

title: Tattvasaṃgraha
primary_author: Śāntarakṣita (kārikā/root verses); commentary Pañjikā by Kamalaśīla (not in this e-text)
note: Root Tattvasaṃgraha verses by Śāntarakṣita; the famous Pañjikā commentary is by his pupil Kamalaśīla. This particular e-text is the root only (so single author for the digital file), but the work/edition is conventionally a verse+Pañjikā pair. Kamalaśīla listed for completeness; conflation_flag=false because this file is root-only.

title: Saptavidhānuttarastotra
note: Anonymous Buddhist devotional stotra tied to the sevenfold-worship liturgy; no original author named, only modern editor Pandey. Already marked anonymous/traditional. No author invented.

title: Avadhānastotra [Vandanāstava]
note: Anonymous Buddhist vandanā-type stotra. Not to be confused with the Aṣṭamahāsthānacaityavandanastava traditionally ascribed to King Harṣa Śīlāditya (a distinct text, per Lamotte) — that ascription is NOT applied here to avoid mis-conflation. Already anonymous/traditional; unchanged.

title: Devīkālottara-Āgama
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva āgama); commentary by Nirañjanasiddha
note: Root verses anonymous revealed āgama; commentary (not in GRETIL mūla file) by Nirañjanasiddha. Roots vs commentary are different hands but the GRETIL text is mūla-only, so no in-text conflation.

title: Rudrayāmalatantra (Uttarakāṇḍa)
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: No named author; pseudo-quotations from 'Rudrayāmala' are common, the work itself is an anonymous, layered revealed Tantra. Editor Jīvānanda Vidyāsāgara is publisher, not author.

title: Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa, Part 2
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Stratified anonymous compilation accreting over centuries; chronologically composite but no distinct NAMED authors, so anonymous rather than conflated. T. Ganapati Sastri = editor.

title: Aṣṭāṅgahṛdayasūtra (Aṣṭāṅgahṛdaya, Sūtrasthāna)
note: AH securely by Vāgbhaṭa (the GS40 file is the mūla Sūtrasthāna). The 'two Vāgbhaṭas' question is about the relation of AH to the Aṣṭāṅgasaṅgraha, not a conflation within this title. Commentators are distinct later hands but not in the mūla text.

title: Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa (DSBC, ed. T. Ganapati Śāstrī)
note: Work-item had empty title/author; identified as the Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa (TSS 70/76/84, Ganapati Śāstrī ed.). Anonymous compilation; Ganapati Śāstrī = editor only, not author.

title: Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa, Part 3
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Same work as parts 2 and the DSBC edition; anonymous composite text, no named author. Editor = T. Ganapati Sastri.

title: Abhisamayālaṃkārāloka [Abhisamayālaṅkārāloka]
primary_author: Haribhadra (vyākhyā); on Maitreya's Abhisamayālaṃkāra kārikā + the Aṣṭasāhasrikā sūtra
source: secondary:cleaned_Snellgrove_Indo-Tibetan Buddhism.txt; secondary:cleaned_NAGARJUNA'S PHILOSOPHY OF SUNYATA AND HIS DIALECTIC_SHOHEI ICHIMURA, 1972.txt
note: Commentary genuinely structured around another author's verses: the kārikā = Abhisamayālaṃkāra (Maitreya, transmission assoc. Asaṅga), the running commentary/ālokā = Haribhadra (8th c.). Haribhadra authorship of the ālokā itself is secure.

title: parākramapūjā
note: Modern editor (Dyczkowski) only; no original author named. Title is a ritual-section heading, not an authored work. Traditional ascription, if any, would be scriptural/revelatory.

title: Tāratantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śākta tantra; framed as Śiva-Devī dialogue)
note: Editor A. K. Maitra (Akshaya Kumar Maitra) is modern; original author anonymous. Traditional ascription = revelation (Śiva to Devī). Not the Buddhist Tārā-mūla-kalpa, a distinct Śākta Tāratantra.

title: Bodhicaryāvatāra [Bodhisattvacaryāvatāra]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_MORAL THEORY IN SANTIDEVA'S SIKSASAMUCCAYA_Barbra R. Clayton.txt
note: Authorship secure. A shorter Dunhuang recension exists (canonical vs Dunhuang versions differ), but both ascribed to Śāntideva; commentary (Pañjikā) by Prajñākaramati is a separate work, not conflated here.

title: Bṛhatkathāślokasaṃgraha
note: Budhasvāmin is author of this saṃgraha. Underlying narrative tradition is Guṇāḍhya's lost Bṛhatkathā (different source-author), but the extant Sanskrit text is Budhasvāmin's; no in-text conflation.

title: Śāstravārtāsamuccaya
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Buddhahood and Philosophy of Mind ... Tomlinson, 2019.txt
note: Jain Haribhadrasūri (8th c.), distinct from the Buddhist Haribhadra of item 1. Self-named in colophon. Sub-commentaries by others not conflated into this root text.

title: Bodhicaryāvatāra [Bodhisattvacaryāvatāra]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Jnanagarbha on the Two Truths_Eckel 1987; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism_Nakamura 1980; secondary:cleaned_MORAL THEORY IN SANTIDEVA'S SIKSASAMUCCAYA_Clayton
note: Root Bodhicaryāvatāra securely by Śāntideva (8th c.); edition adds Prajñākaramati's Pañjikā (10th c.). current_author lists only Śāntideva, omitting the commentator bundled in the edition title.

title: Guhyasamājatantra
current_author: anonymous/traditional (revealed; traditionally ascribed to the Buddha/Vajradhara; sometimes associated with Asaṅga or with the mahāsiddha tradition)
primary_author: anonymous (revealed tantra; traditionally ascribed to the Buddha/Vajradhara)
source: secondary:cleaned_Capriles_Budd philosophical schools and rdzogs chen; secondary:cleaned_PIATS-06-Fagernes-1992-v2; tradition
note: Revealed scripture; traditionally ascribed to the Buddha/Vajradhara. Ārya-tradition figures (Nāgārjuna, Candrakīrti, etc.) are later interpreters, not the author. Remains anonymous historically.

title: Nyāyapraveśapravṛtti
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism_Nakamura 1980; secondary:cleaned_Steinkellner_Buddhist Epistemological Tradition; secondary:cleaned_Tucci_Doctrines of Maitreya; secondary:cleaned_Studies in the Buddhistic Culture of India_Joshi
note: Pravṛtti (commentary) by Haribhadrasūri (the current_author); root Nyāyapraveśa by Śaṅkarasvāmin (formerly misattributed to Dignāga). Title conflates commentator and root author.

title: Mṛgendrāgama [Mṛgendratantra]: 4. Yogapāda
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Yoga Powers_Knut A. Jacobsen 2012; secondary:cleaned_Essais sur le rituel
note: Revealed Śaiva Siddhānta scripture; anonymous. Bhaṭṭa Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha (vṛtti, ca. 10th c.) is associated by tradition/edition but absent from this mūla-only GRETIL file.

title: Mṛgendrāgama [Mṛgendratantra]: 3. Caryāpāda
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Yoga Powers_Knut A. Jacobsen 2012; secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism_Goudriaan ed
note: Revealed Śaiva Siddhānta scripture; anonymous. Bhaṭṭa Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha wrote the vṛtti (associated by edition/tradition); the root Caryāpāda itself has no human author.

title: Bāhyārthasiddhikārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism_Nakamura 1980; secondary:cleaned_ruegg_bka' gnad 8; secondary:cleaned_Logic, language, and reality_Matilal 1990
note: Securely by Śubhagupta (Bhadanta Śubhagupta, ca. 650-750, a Vaibhāṣika/Sautrāntika realist refuted by Śāntarakṣita-Kamalaśīla). Kārikā = root verses; single author.

title: Hitopadeśa
source: secondary:cleaned_Pancatantra. The Book of India's Folk Wisdom_Patrick Olivelle, 1999.txt
note: Nārāyaṇa is the recaster/compiler, not an original author: the bulk derives from the Pañcatantra tradition (its anonymous material/Viṣṇuśarman frame). Title thus conflates Nārāyaṇa's redaction with inherited Pañcatantra authorship; Nārāyaṇa otherwise nearly unknown, patron Dhavalacandra.

title: Tridaśaḍāmaratantre Siddhikālīstotra
note: Tantric stotra/dialogue framed as revelation of Śiva (Mahādeva). No human author; traditional ascription is to Śiva. Stays anonymous/traditional as currently recorded.

title: Pramāṇavārttikaṭīkā
note: Sub-commentary (ṭīkā) on Dharmakīrti's Pramāṇavārttika, comments on Devendrabuddhi's Pañjikā. Author Śākyabuddhi (a.k.a. Śākyamati), 7th-8th c.; attribution secure. Root verses are Dharmakīrti's (not the author of this ṭīkā).

title: Nairātmyaparipṛcchāmahāyānasūtra
note: Mahāyāna sūtra (a 'paripṛcchā' / question-dialogue). No human author; traditional ascription is Buddhavacana. Editor P. L. Vaidya is modern editor only, not author.

title: Niruttaravāda
note: current_author was blank. A tantric 'niruttara' doctrine text framed as Śiva's revelation; no human author. Traditional ascription to Mahādeva/Śiva. Set to anonymous (was empty).

title: Mṛgendrāgama
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva āgama; commentaries by Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha & Rāmakaṇṭha)
note: Śaiva Siddhānta āgama: the mūla is anonymous/revealed (counted as part of the Kāmikāgama upāgama tradition, word of Śiva). The standard editions conflate it with commentaries by TWO different authors — Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha (Vidyā/Yoga) and Rāmakaṇṭha (Kriyā/Caryā). Roles separated; conflation flagged.

title: Śākyasiṃhastotra: viṣṇukṛtam
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt
note: Buddhastotra in praise of the Buddha (Śākyasiṃha). 'viṣṇukṛtam' is a divine-speaker frame (Viṣṇu as eulogist), hence pseudepigraphic; the real composer is anonymous. Tradition-internal ascription to Viṣṇu noted separately.

title: Mālatīmādhava
source: secondary:cleaned_Penguin History of Early India_Romila Thapar, 2001.txt | gretil-header
note: Root prakaraṇa drama securely by Bhavabhūti; the commentary in this edition is by Jagaddhara (distinct person, role separated but not a conflation of the work's authorship).

title: Bauddhatantrakośa I
primary_author: anonymous (modern compilation; input by Milan Shakya)
note: Modern digital compilation (a glossary of tantric terms drawing on many sources). No single classical author; Milan Shakya is the modern editor/inputter, not the author. Anonymous compilation.

title: Adhyātmasāraśataka [Adhyātmasāraśatakasaṭīka]
primary_author: uncertain (śataka verses + ṭīkā, both unattributed in available sources)
source: web:dsbcproject.org (no record) | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Title indicates verses (śataka) plus a ṭīkā (saṭīka), potentially two authorial layers, but no attribution could be triangulated; deliberately not inventing one. Possible homonym with a Jain/Advaita Adhyātmasāraśataka — not confirmed for this item.

title: Madhyapīṭhapūjāvidhi
note: Anonymous ritual prescription (pūjā-vidhi) preserved only in manuscript. No scribal or authorial name recovered; genuinely anonymous.

title: Niśvāsakārikā
source: web:publications.efeo.fr / tantric-studies.uni-hamburg.de | secondary:cleaned_Samdarshi_Sacred Feminine Imagery in Tantric Buddhism.txt
note: Anonymous āgamic/revealed Śaiva scripture (Niśvāsa corpus). Multiple recensional layers but no named authors; attributed by tradition to Śiva, hence treated as anonymous with revelatory framing.

title: Buddhagaṇḍīstava
current_author: attributed (traditionally) to Aśvaghoṣa or Āryadeva; uncertain
note: Single hymn; not multi-author. Traditional/legendary ascription to Aśvaghoṣa, with alternative Āryadeva; genuinely uncertain. Edited in Pandey, Bauddhastotrasaṃgraha 1994.

title: Vādanyāyaṭīkā
source: gretil-header | web:https://encyclopediaofbuddhism.org/wiki/V%C4%81dany%C4%81ya | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: current_author 'Vinītadeva' is WRONG for THIS text: the e-text/edition is Śāntarakṣita's Vipañcitārthā ṭīkā. Two distinct Vādanyāya commentaries exist (Śāntarakṣita's Vipañcitārthā vs Vinītadeva's ṭīkā); title conflates root verses (Dharmakīrti) + commentary (Śāntarakṣita).

title: Tattvasiddhi
source: web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tattvasiddhi | secondary:cleaned_Studies in the Buddhistic Culture of India_Lal Mani Josh.txt | secondary:cleaned_Pramāṇakīrtiḥ-Steinkellner 70th_v2.txt
note: Beware homonyms: distinct from Harivarman's *Tattvasiddhi (Satyasiddhiśāstra) and from Yoginī Cintā's Vyaktabhāvānugata-tattvasiddhi. This tantric Tattvasiddhi's ascription to Śāntarakṣita is debated (Steinkellner contra Lindtner).

title: Cidānandanātha: Svacchandapaddhati
note: Single-author Śaiva ritual paddhati; colophon-confirmed. Editor Sītalāprasād Upādhyāya is modern, not the author.

title: Paścāttāpa
note: No author given on e-text; Russell Webb is the 2003 editor, not the author. Recorded as anonymous (was empty current_author).

title: mahālakṣmīdīpadānavidhiḥ
note: Anonymous ritual vidhi; no traditional personal ascription found. Consistent with current 'anonymous/traditional'.

title: Jñānasāra
current_author: uncertain (multiple works share this title; e.g. Yaśovijaya for the Jain Jñānasāra)
primary_author: Title conflates several distinct works: Yaśovijaya (Jain Jñānasāra); a post-Bhāvaviveka Madhyamaka author (Buddhist Jñānasārasamuccaya); and an anonymous/Śaiva Jñānasāra (Muktabodha)
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism..._Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Buddhism and Jainism_Sarao,Long 2017.txt
note: Bare title 'Jñānasāra' is ambiguous; Jain (Yaśovijaya, 17th c.), Buddhist (Jñānasārasamuccaya), and Śaiva works coexist. Muktabodha (source_url) = Śaiva recension. Disambiguation needed per edition.

title: Saddharmapārājikā
note: Pārājikā = Vinaya category, not a named author's work; transmitted anonymously. No personal attribution; matches current 'anonymous/traditional'.

title: Ṣaṭsahasrakālottarāgama (Ṣaṭsāhasra Kālottara)
note: Revealed Śaiva āgama; the '6000' is a verse-count recension label, not authorship. Anonymous; matches current attribution.

title: Ṣaṭtriṃśatsaṃvarastuti
note: No author named in GRETIL/Bauddhastotrasaṃgraha for this stotra; J.S. Pandey is editor only. Anonymous, matches current.

title: Makuṭāgama (Vīraśaiva recension)
current_author: anonymous/traditional (ascribed to revelation by the Tatpuruṣa face of Śiva)
note: Revealed āgama (Tatpuruṣa face of Śiva); anonymous. Gurukkal/Dyczkowski are editors of the e-text only. Matches current attribution.

title: Bauddha manīṣā - Bauddheṣu Pramāṇamīmāṃsā
primary_author: uncertain (modern Sanskrit treatise on Buddhist epistemology; named modern author not confirmed)
note: Modern Sanskrit work on Buddhist pramāṇa; author name not verifiable from available sources. Left authors=[] pending confirmation; status=uncertain (a modern author exists but is unconfirmed). current_author was empty.

title: Bhāvanākrama
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Mipham Gyatso Rinpoche's 'Makeover' of Hwashang Moheyan (Tucci ed.)
note: Single author Kamalaśīla; colophon also names Tibetan reviewer/translators Prajñāvarman and Jñānasena (transmission roles, not co-authors).

title: Ḍākinījālasaṃvararahasya
note: Was blank; resolved to Anaṅga Yogin (ascribed). This is a tantric commentary/rahasya, distinct from the root Sarvabuddhasamāyoga-ḍākinījālasaṃvara-tantra.

title: Svayaṃbhūstava
note: Buddhist Svayaṃbhū (Nepal) hymn; no author named in edition. NB: not the Jain Svayambhūstotra by Samantabhadra.

title: Nāmāṣṭaśataka
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Gha rung pa...Lamp That Illuminates (cites Prajñāpāramitānāmāṣṭaśataka as scripture, no author)
note: Name-litany (aṣṭaśata) stotra; no author in edition; traditionally anonymous canonical text.

title: Prajñāpāramitāvajracchedikāṭīkā
note: Two distinct layers: root sūtra (anonymous Buddhavacana) vs ṭīkā by Kamalaśīla. Current attribution credits only Kamalaśīla; flagged as conflation of sūtra + commentary.

title: Madhyamakāloka
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems (Keira); cleaned_Kuijp_Dating two ldeu chronicles
note: Single author Kamalaśīla; secure.

title: Buddhabhaṭṭārakastotra
primary_author: anonymous (colophon ascribes composition to Brahmā, legendary)
note: Colophon names Brahmā as composer — pseudepigraphic divine ascription; genuine human author unknown. Differs from bare 'anonymous/traditional' by recording the specific legendary ascription.

title: Samyaksambuddhabhāṣita Pratimālakṣaṇa
primary_author: anonymous (ascribed to the Buddha as buddhavacana; iconometric tradition also linked to Atreya/Mañjuśrī)
note: Pseudepigraphic buddhavacana (Buddha to Śāriputra); no real author. Editors named but not authors. Atreya/Mañjuśrī appear only for the broader iconometry genre, not this text specifically.

title: Kulānandādi
note: Traditional ascription to Matsyendranātha as eponymous founder of the Kaula school; authorship is school-level, not securely personal. No conflation evident in this short treatise.

title: Tattvasamāsa
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally — and per scholarship 'wrongly' — ascribed to Kapila)
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 4 - Potter 1987.txt
note: Anonymous; Kapila ascription explicitly called 'wrongly' attributed by Potter — hence pseudepigraphic to the legendary founder. The kārikā-text is distinct from its later commentaries (Kramadīpikā etc.), which have their own authors.

title: Aparimitāyuḥsūtra
primary_author: anonymous (Mahāyāna sūtra ascribed to the Buddha as buddhavacana)
note: Anonymous buddhavacana sūtra; no individual author. Buddha/Mañjuśrī are internal speakers, not historical authors; recorded as anonymous per instructions (no genuine author known).

title: Laghuyamasmṛti
primary_author: anonymous (Dharmaśāstra metrical smṛti traditionally ascribed to the sage/god Yama)
note: Pseudepigraphic ascription to Yama (legendary lawgiver); a 'Laghu' recension distinct from the larger Yamasmṛti, both anonymous in real authorship. Recorded as pseudepigraphic rather than bare anonymous to capture the Yama ascription.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti
primary_author: anonymous (root tantra; traditionally Buddhavacana, Māyājālatantra cycle)
note: Root text anonymous/traditional Buddhavacana; do not conflate with Vilāsavajra's commentary. Colophon = compiler/editor only (Pandey), not authorship.

title: Sakalāgamasārasaṅgraha
primary_author: uncertain; traditionally ascribed to Nigamajñānadeśika (Śaiva Siddhānta, 16th c.) — unconfirmed for this title
note: Śaiva Siddhānta āgama-digest; compiler not securely identified. Possible Nigamajñānadeśika but low confidence; treat as uncertain rather than firmly anonymous.

title: Āryamañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti (Mukherji 1963 ed.)
note: current_author was empty; root NMS is anonymous/traditional. Mukherji = editor of Skt+Tib parallel texts, not author.

title: Ratnaguṇasaṃcayagāthā
primary_author: anonymous (verse-summary of the Prajñāpāramitā); recension/chapter-division by Haribhadra
note: Anonymous early verse-summary BUT the transmitted (Vaidya/GRETIL) text is the Haribhadra recension with his praśasti — distinct hands: original anonymous composer vs Haribhadra redactor (vs scribe). Flagged as conflated.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti
primary_author: anonymous (root tantra; traditionally Buddhavacana, Māyājālatantra cycle)
note: Duplicate GRETIL encoding of the same Pandey edition as manjn1pu. Root text anonymous; editor/compiler = Pandey only.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti (Minaev 1887 ed.)
note: current_author was empty; root NMS anonymous/traditional. Minaev = 19th-c. editor, not author.

title: Aparimitāyunāmamahāyānasūtra
note: Buddhavacana by convention; Walleser is editor/translator only. No personal author; traditional ascription = words of the Buddha.

title: Kāvyālaṃkārasārasaṃgraha
note: Distinct from Udbhaṭasiddhasvāmin (the Buddhist hymnist of the Viśeṣastava). This GRETIL e-text = kārikās only, extracted from Pratīhāra Indurāja's commented edition; commentary not included here.

title: Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti
note: Workitem title/current_author blank; title is Mañjuśrīnāmasaṃgīti. Root tantra is anonymous (buddhavacana); Davidson = editor/translator. Commentaries by Raviśrījñāna etc. are distinct texts.

title: Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi
primary_author: Ḍombīheruka (mūla, disputed); Dhyānacandra alias Madhyamakarūci (Mahāsukhaprakāśikā ṭīkā, per DSBC source)
source: gretil-header(dsbc-colophon) | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt | web:https://www.academia.edu/37966200
note: Two layers conflated under one title: root (mūla) ascribed to Ḍombīheruka in the aṣṭasiddhi edition vs the ṭīkā Mahāsukhaprakāśikā by Dhyānacandra at the DSBC URL. Beware also a separate Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi by Śūnyasamādhipāda.

title: Āgamakalpalatā
note: Tantric digest (nibandha) ascribed to Yadunātha Cakravartī; consistent with his Mantraratnākara. Confidence medium — limited independent confirmation specifically for Āgamakalpalatā.

title: Āṅgirasasmṛti
primary_author: pseudepigraphic — ascribed to the sage Aṅgiras (actually an anonymous dharmaśāstra compilation)
note: Legendary ascription to the Vedic sage Aṅgiras; genuinely an anonymous/compiled smṛti. No historical author recoverable.

title: Kāvyālaṃkārasārasaṃgraha
primary_author: Udbhaṭa (kārikā/root text); Pratīhārendurāja a.k.a. Indurāja (Laghuvṛtti commentary)
note: Root text secure to Udbhaṭa; the e-text bundles Indurāja's (Pratīhārendurāja) Laghuvṛtti commentary, a distinct later author. current_author lists only the root author and omits the commentator.

title: Cakrasaṃvaratantra Vol. 2
primary_author: Anonymous (scriptural tantra; traditionally revealed via the siddha Lūipa)
note: Genuinely anonymous scripture; traditional ascription to Lūipa noted but not historical. Gray is editor/translator of the cited edition.

title: Kaulāvalīnirṇaya
note: Attribution rests on the colophon/tradition only; author otherwise unknown. Role is best described as compiler of a nibandha rather than original author.

title: Viṣvaksenasaṃhitā
note: Scriptural Āgama; 'authorship' is the divine/legendary speaker Viṣvaksena. Genuinely anonymous as to human composer.

title: Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi with Mahāsukhaprakāśikā
note: Two distinct works fused: anonymous/uncertain root Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi + Dhyānacandra's Mahāsukhaprakāśikā commentary. Edition filename 'Jñānacandra' corrected to Dhyānacandra (the commentator), who is NOT the root author.

title: Cinmayadīkṣāvidhāna
note: Ritual manual (vidhāna) of unknown authorship; no colophon attribution surfaced. Remains anonymous.

title: Mahādānavidhiḥ
note: Ritual paddhati (gift/donation procedure); no human author claimed, anonymous compilation. Muktabodha HK e-text only.

title: Svacchandatantra
current_author: anonymous/traditional (revelation; dialogue of Bhairava and Devī)
source: secondary:cleaned_Essais sur le rituel.txt; secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism.txt
note: Root tantra anonymous revelation; the named human contribution is Kṣemarāja's Uddyota commentary (distinct role, but the work item is the tantra itself, so root stays anonymous).

title: Tattvasaṃgrahapañcikā [Tattvasaṃgrahapañjikā]
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism_Hajime Nakamura.txt; secondary:cleaned_Jnanagarbha on the Two Truths_Eckel.txt
note: Title conflates two authors: mūla verses by Śāntarakṣita, pañjikā commentary by Kamalaśīla. current_author lists only Kamalaśīla (correct for the pañjikā), but the bundled mūla is Śāntarakṣita's.

title: Pheṭakapratiṣṭhācaṇḍikāgnividhi
note: Newar installation/fire ritual paddhati (caṇḍikāgnividhi); no human author, anonymous local compilation. 'pheṭaka' = the shield iconographic attribute.

title: Dvādaśakāranayastotra
note: Traditional ascription to Nāgārjuna; like most stotras under his name, attribution is doubtful (possibly pseudepigraphic) but conventionally retained. Confidence low.

title: Kaṅkālamālinītantra
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; web:https://shivashakti.com/kankala
note: Anonymous revealed tantra; colophons ascribe it to Dakṣiṇāmnāya, no individual author. Relatively late (post-classical) Śākta-Kaula compilation.

title: Avalokiteśvarastotra
source: secondary:cleaned_Dorji Wangchuk_Cross-Cultural transmission of Buddhist texts; secondary:cleaned_Moonbeams of Mahamudra
note: Traditional ascription to the siddha Carpaṭi/Carpaṭipāda; a single-author stotra, no commentary-conflation.

title: Bṛhadyamasmṛti
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally ascribed to the sage Yama)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Traverses on Less Trodden Path of Indian Philosophy and Religion
note: Smṛti attributed to the divine sage Yama (legendary). No real author; traditional ascription noted, status anonymous.

title: Sāṃkhyatattvapradīpikā [Sāṃkhyārthatattvapradīpikā]
note: Self-identified author Bhaṭṭa Keśava in the text; secure single-author work (a Sāṃkhya manual in the Vijñānabhikṣu tradition).

title: Tripurārahasya, Māhātmyakhaṇḍa
primary_author: anonymous (Haritāyana Saṃhitā; traditionally revealed Dattātreya → Paraśurāma → Haritāyana)
note: Traditional/pseudepigraphic frame: revelation chain Dattātreya>Paraśurāma>Haritāyana; Haritāyana the legendary compiler. Effectively anonymous medieval Śākta work.

title: Kṣurikā Upaniṣad
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy; secondary:cleaned_Origin and Development of Early Indian Contemplative Practices
note: Mūla Upaniṣad anonymous; the named Adyar edition bundles the bhāṣya of Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin (distinct commentator) — root vs commentary by different hands, hence conflation_flag.

title: Abhisamayālaṅkāravṛttiḥ sphuṭārthā
note: Haribhadra is securely the author of the vṛtti; but the edited text embeds the root Abhisamayālaṅkāra kārikās of Maitreya — distinct authors (kārikā vs vṛtti), hence conflation_flag.

title: Arthaviniścayasūtra + Nibandhana
current_author: sūtra anonymous; commentary (Nibandhana) by Bhikṣu Vīryaśrīdatta of Nālandā
source: secondary:cleaned_Skilling_Vasubandhu Vyākhyāyukti.txt; secondary:cleaned_Studies in the Buddhistic Culture of India_Lal Mani Josh.txt
note: Two distinct authorial layers: anonymous root sūtra vs. Nibandhana by Vīryaśrīdatta of Nālandā (fl. reign of Dharmapāla, c. late 8th–early 9th c.). Title conflates both.

title: Adhyātmasāraśataka + ṭīkā
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Workitem had blank title/author. Root verses (śataka) by Prabhākaragupta; the published ṭīkā ascribed to Śiromaṇi. Distinct from the 9th-c. Buddhist logician Prabhākaragupta; identity uncertain hence medium confidence.

title: ūrdhvāmnāya damanārohaṇapaddhati
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; tradition
note: Anonymous ūrdhvāmnāya ritual manual (damanārohaṇa / damanaka-flower offering). No personal attribution; traditional Newar/ūrdhvāmnāya transmission only.

title: Avalokiteśvarāṣṭakastotra
note: Anonymous Buddhist aṣṭaka-stotra to Avalokiteśvara; colophon gives only the deity, no author.

title: Hāratīstotra
note: Anonymous; colophon ascribes provenance to 'Śrī Bhavaratna' (likely a source text/anthology, possibly a person), but no clear individual author. Tentative anonymous.

title: Saubhāgyataraṅgiṇī
source: secondary:cleaned_Traverses on Less Trodden Path...Yajneshwara Shastri.txt (Saundaryalaharī commentary tradition); tradition (Muktabodha)
note: Mukunda is the traditional author of this Saundaryalaharī commentary; not independently corroborated in scholarly lit, so traditional/medium. The base text Saundaryalaharī is separately ascribed (traditionally) to Śaṅkara — not part of this commentary's authorship.

title: Devīrahasya (Devīrahasyatantra)
primary_author: anonymous (compiled by an unnamed Kashmiri redactor; traditionally ascribed to the Rudrayāmala)
source: web:https://shivashakti.com/rahasya; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt
note: Scripture framed as Śiva–Pārvatī dialogue; claims to derive from Rudrayāmala. Late Kashmiri compilation. No individual author; traditional ascription to Rudrayāmala noted separately.

title: Śaktisaṃgamatantra, Sundarīkhaṇḍa
primary_author: anonymous (late Tantric compilation; ed. Benoytosh Bhattacharyya 1947)
note: One of four khaṇḍas of the Śaktisaṃgamatantra. Bhattacharyya is editor, not author. Anonymous scriptural compilation.

title: Svacchandatantra [Svacchandabhairavatantra]
primary_author: anonymous (Bhairava/Mantramārga scripture); Kṣemarāja (Svacchandoddyota commentary, not in this mūla e-text)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Essais sur le rituel.txt; secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism.txt
note: Root tantra anonymous (revealed scripture). Kṣemarāja (11th c.) authored the Svacchandoddyota commentary — listed for completeness since the named KSTS edition pairs them, but the e-text is mūla only; not a conflation within this transcript.

title: Sāṃkhyatattvavivecana
primary_author: Ṣimānanda Dīkṣita (= 'Kṣemānanda' in some bibliographies; single author)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Single author; a commentary (vivecana) on the Tattvasamāsa. Name read as Ṣimānanda Dīkṣita in the colophon vs 'Kṣemānanda' in bibliographies; 'Kṣemendra' is a further mis-reading. No conflation of multiple authors — only an orthographic/transmission variant of one name.

title: Siddhalakṣmīpūjāpaddhati
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_Śaivism and the tantric traditions.txt; secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_Hindu tantric chaplains.txt
note: Anonymous worship manual (paddhati) of the goddess Siddhalakṣmī (Siddhilakṣmī in Nepal). No author or compiler named in tradition or scholarship.

title: Mahālakṣmīpūjāvidhiḥ
primary_author: anonymous (ritual manual for the worship of Mahālakṣmī)
note: Anonymous pūjā-vidhi for Mahālakṣmī. Such ritual manuals are typically uncredited liturgical compilations; no author identifiable.

title: Aśeṣakulavallarī
note: Single ascribed author Gaṇananāthabhaṭṭa retained; modern English translation by Mike Magee noted separately as translator role. Attribution not independently verifiable hence 'traditional'.

title: Raghunandanaprasāda Śukla: Dīkṣāprakāśa
note: Author retained as given. Distinct from the celebrated Raghunandana Bhaṭṭācārya of Bengal. Mike Magee = translator. Single author, no conflation.

title: Śrīguhyasamājatantra
primary_author: anonymous (root tantra); traditionally ascribed to the Buddha; exegetical Ārya tradition ascribed to Nāgārjuna (disputed)
note: Root tantra genuinely anonymous; Buddha-ascription is doctrinal framing, Nāgārjuna-ascription pseudepigraphic/legendary (linked to the 'second/tantric Nāgārjuna' problem). Kept anonymous; conflation_flag false (single root-tantra layer, not a multi-author edition).

title: Kāvyālaṅkārasūtra
note: Sūtras AND self-commentary (svavṛtti) both by Vāmana — one author, not a conflation. Modern editor Nārāyaṇarāma noted as redactor of the printed edition only.

title: Gaṇapatihṛdaya-Dhāraṇī
note: No personal author; colophon only marks completion. Buddhavacana framing. Kept anonymous.

title: Mahāsubhāṣitasaṅgraha
current_author: anonymous/traditional (modern critical compilation ed. by L. Sternbach and others)
primary_author: Ludwik Sternbach et al. (modern compiler/editor); verses anonymous from many authors
note: Modern anthology: thousands of subhāṣitas by countless (mostly anonymous/various) authors, compiled by Ludwik Sternbach. Not a single-author original work; verses are an aggregate rather than a conflation of a fixed set of named authors, so conflation_flag=false but compiler role recorded.

title: Parameśvarīmatatantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva/Śākta-Kubjikā scripture, ascribed to Śiva/Bhairava)
note: Anonymous revealed Kubjikā-tradition tantra; traditional ascription to Śiva/Bhairava only. Not to be confused with Pārameśvaratantra (Śaiva Siddhānta).

title: Mālinīvijayottaratantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Trika scripture); ed. Somadeva Vasudeva / Madhusudan Kaul Shastri
note: Editors (Vasudeva, Kaul Shastri) are not authors; recorded with role 'translator/editor'. Mūla is anonymous āgama.

title: Mālinīvijayottaratantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Trika scripture); ed. Madhusūdan Kaul Śāstrī (KSTS 37, 1922)
note: Duplicate title (Muktabodha encoding) of the Mālinīvijayottara; anonymous āgama. Kaul Śāstrī = editor, not author.

title: Nigamasāranirṇaya
note: Carries over the catalogue/traditional ascription to Rāmāramaṇadeva; confidence low, unverified by secondary literature.

title: Kriyākālaguṇottara
note: Anonymous revealed Gāruḍa-tantra of composite/layered origin (multiple anonymous redactors); Slouber is editor/translator, not author. Not flagged conflated as the distinct contributors are not separately named persons.

title: Parātriṃśikā
primary_author: anonymous (verses = final portion of the Rudrayāmala, revealed Trika scripture)
note: Root verses are anonymous scripture (part of Rudrayāmala); do NOT attribute the mūla to Abhinavagupta — he authored only the Vivaraṇa. Conflation risk noted but item = verses only, so flag false.

title: māheśvaratantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva tantra; framed as Śiva–Devī/Kārttikeya dialogue)
source: tradition; secondary:cleaned_Mirror of Beryl final.txt; web:shivashakti.com/bibby
note: Revealed tantra; no human author. Traditional framing only (Śiva as revealer). Not the Buddhist Tibetan 'Maheśvara tantra' class.

title: Bhūtaśuddhitantra
source: tradition; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt
note: Revealed tantra; anonymous. Title also denotes the bhūtaśuddhi ritual; no individual composer attested.

title: Vijñānabhairava
note: Root tantra only in this e-text; anonymous revealed work. Commentary (conflated authorship) is in the separate MB edition record, not here.

title: Yogaśataka
current_author: anonymous; traditionally attributed to Nāgārjuna (Nāgārjunīya school)
primary_author: attributed to Nāgārjuna (also to Vararuci); actually anonymous compiler of the Nāgārjunīya tradition
source: secondary:cleaned_Walser_Nāgārjuna in context.txt; secondary:cleaned_Wallace_Medicine and Astrology...txt; web:ifpindia.org/bookstore/pifi62
note: Disputed Nāgārjuna vs Vararuci; pseudepigraphic use of 'Nāgārjuna' name. Single (unknown) compiler, not multi-author — no conflation.

title: Mahogratārāṣṭakastotra
source: dsbc; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; secondary:cleaned_From Bodhgaya to Lhasa to Beijing...McKeown.txt
note: Anonymous Buddhist stotra; Miroj Shakya is the e-text inputter (not author). No individual ascription found.

title: Vijñānabhairava (Vijñānabhairavatantra)
primary_author: anonymous (root tantra); Kṣemarāja (uddyota, vv. 1–23); Śivopādhyāya (vivṛti, completion)
source: secondary:cleaned_Essais sur le rituel.txt; secondary:cleaned_Indian Epistemology and Metaphysics_Tuske 2017.txt; web (KSTS edition title)
note: MULTI-AUTHOR: anonymous root tantra + Kṣemarāja uddyota (vv.1–23) + Śivopādhyāya completing the vivṛti. Distinct from the bare GRETIL root-only e-text (SA_GSP30_vijnbhau).

title: Jñānasiddhi
source: secondary:cleaned_Snellgrove_Indo-Tibetan Buddhism.txt; secondary:cleaned_Studies in the Buddhistic Culture of India_Lal Mani Josh.txt
note: Traditional ascription to the siddha-king Indrabhūti of Uḍḍiyāna; complicated by the well-known multiplicity of 'Indrabhūti' figures in the lineage tradition, hence 'traditional' rather than 'secure'.

title: Śrīguhyasamājamaṇḍalavidhi
current_author: anonymous/traditional (such maṇḍalavidhis ascribed to authors of the Ārya school, e.g. Dīpaṃkarabhadra)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopaedia of Tantra. Vol 4_Sadhu Santidev 1999.txt
note: Edition is specifically Dīpaṃkarabhadra's Guhyasamājamaṇḍalavidhi; current_author hedged it as anonymous/traditional, but both e-text header and secondary lit secure the named author.

title: Mṛgendrāgama [Mṛgendratantra]: 1. Vidyāpāda
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Śaiva āgama; commentary by Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha)
note: Mūla āgama is anonymous/revealed; the named commentator Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha is a separate role (his commentary accompanies the edition but is not the mūla). No conflation within the mūla itself.

title: Kuṭṭanīmata
note: Single-author kāvya; authorship secure and uncontested.

title: Pramāṇavārttikavṛtti Commentary [Pramāṇavārttikavṛttiṭīkā]
primary_author: Karṇakagomin (ṭīkā); commenting on Dharmakīrti's Pramāṇavārttika (kārikā) + svopajñavṛtti
source: secondary:cleaned_Millennium of Buddhist Logic. Vol1_Alex Wayman.txt; secondary:cleaned_Horin...Buddhist Logic_Talmo Aoyama (ed) (2004)_(A).txt
note: Title names Karṇakagomin (correct for the ṭīkā layer), but the work as edited conflates Dharmakīrti's root kārikās and his own svavṛtti with Karṇakagomin's sub-commentary — three distinct textual roles, two distinct authors.

title: Śrīsahajasiddhi
source: secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Hevajra's Abhisamaya and tshogs bshad.txt; secondary:cleaned_Taking the Result as the Path...Cyrus Stearns (2006).txt
note: Traditional ascription to the siddha Ḍombī Heruka; matches current_author. Distinguish from the Sahajasiddhi attributed to Indrabhūti and from the later Sa-skya redaction of a same-named work.

title: Kāllottara (Kālottara)
note: Anonymous Śaiva Siddhānta scripture; multiple recensions (Sārdhatriśatika/Trayodaśaśatika/Bṛhat-Kālottara). No human author; traditionally Śiva's revelation.

title: Timirodghāṭana
note: Anonymous revealed Śaiva Tantra; S. Vasudeva is the modern transcriber/editor of the unedited Kathmandu MS, not the author.

title: Nirvāṇatantra
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Anonymous Śākta/Kaula revealed Tantra; do not confuse with Mahānirvāṇatantra. No human author.

title: Kāvyālaṅkārasūtra
note: Securely Vāmana; sūtra + auto-vṛtti both his. Gopendra's Kāmadhenu ṭīkā is NOT in this e-text, so no multi-author conflation here.

title: Ādyanātha: Anuttaraprakāśapañcāśikā
current_author: attributed to Ādyanātha (Ādinātha, the primordial Śiva-teacher; traditional/legendary attribution)
primary_author: ascribed to Ādyanātha (Ādinātha, legendary primordial Śiva-teacher); true author unknown
note: Ascribed to Ādyanātha/Ādinātha (legendary primordial guru) — pseudepigraphic; real author unknown. Trika/Kaula short hymn (pañcāśikā = 50 verses).

title: Brahma-Purāṇa
note: Composite anonymous Mahāpurāṇa; Vyāsa ascription is traditional/legendary. Schreiner & Söhnen-Thieme are modern editors, not authors.

title: Harṣacarita: Pariśiṣṭa
primary_author: various poets/anthologists (Bāṇa-praśastayaḥ, appended verses); NOT Bāṇa
note: Pariśiṣṭa = appended praise-verses on Bāṇa by diverse authors (Candradeva, Ruyyaka, etc.), compiled in the edition; not authored by Bāṇa himself. Multi-author conflation under the Harṣacarita title.

title: Tārārahasyavṛttikā
source: secondary:cleaned_LVP_Bouddhisme_études et matériaux.txt; secondary:cleaned_Khecari-Vidya (Hatha Yoga) _James Mallinson, 2003.txt
note: Vṛtti (commentary/gloss) on a Tārā tantra; ascribed to Śaṅkara Āgamācārya of Bengal. Do not confuse with Brahmānanda's Tārārahasya.

title: Guhyasamājamaṇḍalavidhi
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Emptiness that is Form...Thomas Freeman Yarnall.txt
note: Root mūla work (~450 verses) by Dīpaṃkarabhadra; Śāntipā's ṭīkā is a distinct work, not part of this file.

title: Ucchiṣṭacāṇḍālinīkalpa
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion..._N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt; secondary:cleaned_Tantra in Practice_David Gordon White, ed, 2000_a.txt
note: Anonymous tantric kalpa of goddess Ucchiṣṭacāṇḍālinī/Mātaṅgī; no traditional named author located.

title: Mahāvyutpatti
primary_author: anonymous (Tibetan imperial translation committee, c. 814 CE)
source: secondary:cleaned_Karmay and Nagano_New Horizons in Bon Studies.txt; secondary:cleaned_Shakabpa_One Hundred Thousand Moons_v1.txt
note: Sanskrit-Tibetan lexicon compiled by a royally appointed committee of translators (~814 CE); no individual author. Committee members (Jinamitra etc.) function as compilers/translators.

title: Kalyāṇakāraka
note: Jain Ayurvedic treatise by Ugrāditya, 9th-c. Jain physician; attribution secure.

title: Siddhilakṣmīkoṭyāhutidinakṛtya
note: Anonymous Newar ritual digest (paddhati genre); no named author. Current attribution (anonymous/traditional, Newar Sarvāmnāya) retained.

title: Kāṇhapādasya dohākoṣa
primary_author: Kāṇha / Kṛṣṇacaryāpāda (dohā root verses); anonymous (surviving Sanskrit commentary)
source: secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Root dohās by Kāṇha; surviving Sanskrit commentary anonymous (named ṭīkās: Amṛtavajra, Mekhalā). Conflates author of verses + commentator.

title: Caryāsaṃgraha
primary_author: multiple mahāsiddhas (caryāgīti anthology); no single author
note: Anthology/compilation of caryā songs by multiple named mahāsiddhas; not single-authored. Empty current_author corrected to multi-author compilation.

title: Śivamahimnastava
note: Traditional ascription to Puṣpadanta via internal colophon (Gandharva legend); historical author unknown. Retains current traditional attribution.

title: Mañjuvajramukhyākhyāna
note: Late (~1185 CE) anonymous Guhyasamāja/Jñānapāda ritual manual; Tanaka is modern editor. Empty current_author set to anonymous.

title: Vijñānabhairava, with commentary Kaumadī (Kaumudī)
current_author: root text anonymous (revealed tantra); commentary Kaumudī by Bhaṭṭa Ānanda (Ānanda Bhaṭṭa)
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Vijñānabhairava tantra); Ānanda Bhaṭṭa (Kaumudī commentary)
note: Anonymous revealed tantra (Rudrayāmala) + Kaumudī commentary by Ānanda Bhaṭṭa; two distinct hands. Matches current attribution.

title: Viṣṇusmṛti
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally revealed by Viṣṇu; single Vaiṣṇava/Kāṭhaka author per Olivelle)
note: Pseudepigraphic-revelatory framing (spoken by Viṣṇu) but text genuinely anonymous; one human author per Olivelle, not multi-author. Traditional ascription to Viṣṇu noted.

title: Śaktisūtra
note: Authorship unverifiable; Gnoli's putative attribution doubted in literature. Treat as anonymous Śākta sūtra-text.

title: Vārāhītantra
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion ... N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Anonymous revealed Śākta tantra (dialogue framing); no individual author. Likely composite/accreted scripture but no distinct named authors to separate.

title: Sarahapādasya dohākoṣa
note: Traditional (mahāsiddha) attribution, secure within tradition. Dohākoṣa = People Dohā; transmitted dohākoṣas often carry later commentary (e.g. Advayavajra), but this item is the root verses by Saraha. DSBC URL points to wrong book.

title: Ānandatantra
note: Anonymous revealed Śākta/Vāma-srotas tantra; cited by Abhinavagupta/Jayaratha. No individual author.

title: Kākacaṇḍeśvarīmata
note: Anonymous revealed Śākta tantra (-mata genre); no individual author traced. Low confidence only on completeness of search, not on anonymity.

title: Mantrakoṣa
note: Title 'Mantrakoṣa' is shared by multiple distinct authors (e.g. a Mantrakoṣa ascribed to Jagannātha Cakravartī); current ascription to Āśāditya Tripāṭhī unverified. Single-author record retained but low confidence.

title: Prajñopāyaviniścayasiddhi
note: Anaṅgavajra, Siddha lineage from Uḍḍiyāna; teacher Mahāpadmavajra. Edited with Jñānasiddhi of Indrabhūti (a separate work/author — not conflated here).

title: Kālāgniyajñavidhi
note: Anonymous ritual manual; no traditional ascription recoverable. Matches current 'anonymous/traditional'.

title: Bhuvaneśvarīmahāstotra
note: Stotra ascribed to Pṛthvīdharācārya by its edition; traditional/editorial attribution, not independently corroborated. Do not conflate with Pṛthvīdhara the dramatist-commentator on the Mṛcchakaṭika.

title: Mahāmāyātantra (Toh 425)
primary_author: anonymous (root tantra, buddhavacana); Ratnākaraśānti (Guṇavatīṭīkā); Jinavara & Gö Lhetsé (Tibetan translation)
note: Distinct roles: anonymous/buddhavacana root tantra vs Ratnākaraśānti's Guṇavatīṭīkā commentary vs the Jinavara/Gö Lhetsé Tibetan translation. current_author was empty; record now separates these contributors.

title: Vajrasattvaniṣpādanasūtra [Vajrasattvasādhana]
current_author: Candrakīrti (the tantric author of the Guhyasamāja Ārya school)
note: Ascribed to the tantric Candrakīrti but treated as pseudepigraphic ('pseudo-Candrakīrti', 'dubious') by Wedemeyer and in the Pramāṇakīrtiḥ volume. Status downgraded from secure attribution to pseudepigraphic.

title: Pramāṇavārttikālaṃkāra
note: Title names only the commentator; root verses are Dharmakīrti's, so the text conflates two distinct authors (mūla vs bhāṣya). Prajñākaragupta early 10th c. (Bhattacharya); Dharmakīrti 7th c.

title: Ratnāvalī (commentary on the Hevajratantra) — Kamalanātha
primary_author: Kamalanātha (Ratnāvalī, commentary); on the anonymous Hevajratantra (mūla)
note: Record was blank; commentary by Kamalanātha vs anonymous root tantra = two roles. Some traditions associate the Ratnāvalī attribution with Mañjuśrī/Kamalanātha.

title: Piṅgalāmata
current_author: anonymous/traditional (a Nepalese manuscript names Śrīkaṇṭhanātha as promulgator)
primary_author: anonymous (Śaiva/Bhairava tantra; traditionally promulgated by Śrīkaṇṭhanātha/Śiva)
note: Traditional ascription to Śiva/Śrīkaṇṭhanātha is revelatory (promulgator), not human authorship; retained anonymous.

title: Netratantra
primary_author: anonymous (Netratantra mūla); commentary Uddyota by Kṣemarāja
note: Root tantra anonymous; commentator Kṣemarāja is distinct and named for completeness. Edition is a separate matter; root remains anonymous.

title: Ratnāvalī (commentary on the Hevajratantra) — Kamalanātha
primary_author: Kamalanātha (Ratnāvalī, commentary); on the anonymous Hevajratantra (mūla)
note: Record blank; same work/authors as the K12 entry. Commentary (Kamalanātha) vs anonymous root tantra = two roles.

title: Viṣṇusmṛti
current_author: anonymous/traditional (revealed by Viṣṇu in the frame)
primary_author: anonymous (Vaiṣṇava-Dharmaśāstra mūla); commentary Keśavavaijayantī by Nandapaṇḍita
note: Mūla anonymous (legendary frame: revealed by Viṣṇu to the Earth-goddess). Nandapaṇḍita is the commentator (Keśavavaijayantī), a distinct named role.

title: Mañjuśrīmūlakalpa [Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa]
primary_author: anonymous (composite/multi-author Buddhist scripture; framed as Buddhavacana via Mañjuśrī)
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Composite anonymous tantra/kalpa accreted over centuries; distinct chapters (esp. ~750 CE Prophecy of Kings) by different anonymous authors/redactors. No single author; traditional ascription is Buddhavacana via Mañjuśrī.

title: Sitātapatrā
primary_author: anonymous (dhāraṇī; framed as Buddhavacana, issuing from the Buddha's uṣṇīṣa)
note: Anonymous uṣṇīṣa protective dhāraṇī/vidyā; traditionally Buddhavacana. No human attribution.

title: Mañjuśrīmūlakalpa (Āryamañjuśrīmūlakalpa)
primary_author: anonymous (composite/multi-author Buddhist scripture; framed as Buddhavacana via Mañjuśrī)
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt
note: Duplicate of the MMK; multi-stratum composite by several anonymous authors/redactors. conflation_flag=true.

title: Sattvārādhanagāthā
current_author: attributed to Nāgārjuna (āryanāgārjunakṛtā), almost certainly pseudepigraphic
source: dsbc:header | secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt | web:https://maitripa.org/library/subject-guides/madhyamaka/source-treatises-of-madhyamaka/nagarjuna/
note: Colophon ascribes to Nāgārjuna but also calls it Buddha-spoken; almost certainly not the 2nd-c. Madhyamaka Nāgārjuna. Pseudepigraphic; traditional ascription retained with low confidence.

title: Unmattabhairava
note: Anonymous revealed Bhairavatantra of the Mantrapīṭha; traditionally divine revelation (Śiva/Bhairava), no human author. Cited as a canonical tantra title by Sanderson.

title: Skanda-Purāṇa: Revākhaṇḍa
note: Anonymous Purāṇic māhātmya of the Narmadā (Revā); standard Purāṇic ascription to Vyāsa is traditional/legendary, not historical. Identity vs. the misattributed Vāyupurāṇa text clarified by Neuss.

title: Gaṇeśastotra
note: Edition compiler J.S. Pandey is editor of the anthology, not the hymn's author. No traditional named ascription; treat as anonymous devotional stotra.

title: Pañcakrama
primary_author: Ārya Nāgārjuna (tantric) — chs. 1,3,4,5; Śākyamitra — ch. 2 (Sarvaśuddhiviśuddhikrama / Anuttarasaṃdhi)
note: ONE TITLE CONFLATES TWO AUTHORS: ch.2 by Śākyamitra, rest by (tantric) Nāgārjuna. Also: tantric Nāgārjuna ≠ Madhyamaka Nāgārjuna (pseudepigraphic identification). Tripathi 2001 / Mimaki-Tomabechi 1994 critical eds.

title: Piṇḍīkrama
source: gretil-header + secondary:cleaned_Yoga of the Guhyasamajatantra_Wayman + cleaned_Willson and Brauen + cleaned_Cantwell and Mayer
note: Single tantric author. The 'Nāgārjuna' here is the Guhyasamāja/Ārya tantric master, traditionally—but not securely—equated with the Madhyamaka Nāgārjuna; status best called traditional.

title: Yogacūḍāmaṇyupaniṣat (Yogacūḍāmaṇi Upaniṣad)
note: Upaniṣad. Commentary printed in the Adyar edition is by Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin (commentator, NOT author of the Upaniṣad)—not part of this root-text item. Traditionally affiliated to the Sāmaveda; no personal ascription.

title: Nityākaulatantra (Nityākaula)
note: Revealed scripture; Kiss & Sanderson are editors not authors. Distinct from the Śākta 'Nityātantra' quoted in compendia (Prāṇatoṣiṇī etc.); avoid conflating with that.

title: Namaskāraikaviṃśatistotra [Namastāre-ekaviṃśatistotraguṇasahita / Tārā-ekaviṃśatistotra]
current_author: anonymous/traditional (revealed; ascribed to the Buddha/Tārā cycle)
primary_author: anonymous (canonically ascribed to the Buddha — buddhavacana)
source: secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Insight_Wayman + cleaned_In Praise of Tara_Willson + edition-metadata (de Blonay 1895)
note: Root stotra: anonymous, canonically buddhavacana (status pseudepigraphic). Do NOT attribute to Sūryagupta—he wrote the commentary cycle, not the praise. Tibetan tr. by Nyän (11th c.), rev. Drakpa Gyaltsen—translators, not authors.

title: Kāvyamīmāṃsā
note: Colophon name rājaśekharakṛta; early-10th-c. court poet/theorist. No attribution dispute; only Adhikaraṇa 1 survives.

title: Lokeśvaraśataka
note: Vajradatta, 9th c., poet at court of Devapāla (Pāla); identified with Vajradeva in some Tibskrit lists. Tradition: stotra said to have cured leprosy.

title: Dhyānaratnāvali
note: Colophon attributes to Trilocanaśivācārya; previously listed anonymous/traditional. Confidence medium because attribution rests on MS colophon, not a critical edition; distinct from his Siddhāntasārāvali and Prāyaścittasamuccaya.

title: Tantrābhidhāna
current_author: anonymous/traditional (title also used for a modern reference lexicon)
source: secondary:cleaned_explaining mantras and the efficacy of ritual.txt
note: Anonymous tantric lexicon/nighaṇṭu; editor (not author) = Pañcānana Bhaṭṭācārya / Avalon. No personal classical authorship; do not confuse with the modern Tantrābhidhāna reference dictionary.

title: Vimalāvatītantra
current_author: attributed to Vimalaśiva (per project hint; uncertain)
note: Genre = āgamic/revealed tantra → anonymous. Traditional ascription to Vimalaśiva is uncertain and unverified; the inscriptional Vimalaśiva is a guru/donor, not an author. No critical edition.

title: Garuḍa-Purāṇa
note: Anonymous composite Purāṇa; traditional ascription to Vyāsa (low confidence, conventional). Frame: narrated by Viṣṇu to Garuḍa. Not a single-author text but a stratified compilation rather than a conflation of named authors.

title: Maṇḍalabrāhmaṇa Upaniṣad
primary_author: anonymous (root text); Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin (commentary in this edition)
note: Root text genuinely anonymous (traditional dialogue speaker Sūrya/Nārāyaṇa to Yājñavalkya); but the edition conflates it with the named commentary of Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin — separate role flagged.

title: Vajrasattvaniṣpādanasūtra (Vajrasattvasādhana)
note: Tantric Candrakīrti (Guhyasamāja Ārya school), to be distinguished from the Prāsaṅgika-Madhyamaka Candrakīrti; the two are conflated in tradition. Title was empty in workitem — supplied.

title: Samājasādhanavyavastholi
note: Colophon secures Nāgabuddhi (=Nāgabodhi); Guhyasamāja Ārya-school exegete. Attribution traditional rather than independently 'secure'.

title: Vīṇāśikhatantra
primary_author: anonymous (Śaiva Vāma tantra; revealed by Śiva to Devī)
note: Genuinely anonymous Śaiva scripture of the Left Current; traditional 'speaker' is Śiva. No human author.

title: Jayākhyasaṃhitā
note: Anonymous Pāñcarātra (Vaiṣṇava tantric) saṃhitā; traditionally divine revelation. Editor Krishnamacharya is editorial role only, not author.

title: Śivadharma (Śivadharmaśāstra)
note: Anonymous early-medieval lay-Śaiva śāstra of the Śivadharma corpus; transmitted as dialogue, no human author. New critical eds in progress (Shivadharma Project).

title: Guhyasamājatantrapradīpoddyotanaṭīkā-ṣaṭkoṭivyākhyā
note: current_author empty; assigned to the tantric Candrakīrti. NB the name conflates two distinct historical figures in tradition (tantric vs Madhyamaka Candrakīrti); the ṣaṭkoṭi-vyākhyā = exposition of the six hermeneutic positions.
