note: current_author empty; this is a scholarly edition spanning a lineage. Core authorship = Saroruhavajra (=Padmavajra/Saroruha); additional surviving works by later lineage authors edited alongside. Gerloff is modern editor, not author.

title: Bhāgavata-Purāṇa 10, 29–33
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally Vyāsa; narrated by Śuka) — root text; Śrīdhara Svāmin (Bhāvārthadīpikā commentary)
note: Current attribution (anonymous/Vyāsa) covers only the mūla. The e-text CONFLATES the anonymous/traditional Purāṇa text with Śrīdhara Svāmin's distinct commentary (Bhāvārthadīpikā) per colophon. Rāsapañcādhyāyī chapters.

title: Pauṣkara-Saṃhitā
primary_author: anonymous (Pāñcarātra Āgama; framed as Bhagavān to Pauṣkara)
source: gretil-header | secondary:History of the Tantric Religion (Bhattacharyya) | secondary:Hinduism and Buddhism (Eliot)
note: No human author; revealed Āgama. Traditional ascription to divine revelation only. Confirms current 'anonymous/traditional'.

title: Manubhāṣya
note: Commentary securely by Medhātithi (~9th c.). Title bundles Manubhāṣya with the underlying Manusmṛti root text (different/anonymous authorship) — root mūla and bhāṣya are by distinct hands, hence conflation_flag.

title: Mahāmāyātantra
source: gretil-edition | web:https://tibetanbuddhistencyclopedia.com (Mahamaya-tantra) | web:researchgate Ratnakarasanti Maya and Mahamaya
note: Root tantra is anonymous revealed scripture (matches current). Edition/e-text CONFLATES it with Ratnākaraśānti's distinct Guṇavatī commentary; roles must be separated.

title: Kulacūḍāmaṇi Nigama
source: secondary:Encyclopedia of Tantra (Santideva) | secondary:Gendun Chopel (Lopez) | tradition
note: Revealed Kaula Nigama; no human author. Confirms current 'anonymous/traditional'. (Nigama = Devī instructs Śiva, inverse of Āgama.)

title: Saṃnyāsa Upaniṣads
source: secondary:cleaned_Two Traditions of Meditation in Ancient India_Johannes Bronkhorst, 1993.txt; secondary:cleaned_Early Buddhists and Urban Centers...txt (Olivelle 1992)
note: Anonymous corpus of renunciatory Upaniṣads; the edition conflates the anonymous root texts with Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin's commentary (Advaita ascetic, fl. c. 1700-1750).

title: Vāmakeśvarīmata (Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇava)
primary_author: anonymous (revealed tantra); commentaries by Jayaratha (Vivaraṇa) and Bhāskararāya (Setubandha)
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; secondary:cleaned_Essais sur le rituel.txt
note: Anonymous Śrīvidyā tantra (āgama, treated as revelation). Editions conflate the root with named commentators (Jayaratha; Bhāskararāya's Setubandha).

title: Āścaryayogamālā
note: Pseudepigraphic; 'Nāgārjuna' here is the legendary alchemist/Siddha, not the Madhyamaka master. Single (legendary) attributed author, so no conflation.

title: Mantroddhāra
note: Anonymous Buddhist tantric mantroddhāra text; title denotes a mantra-extraction genre rather than a named composition.

title: Stavacintāmaṇi
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; web:https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/j.1748-0922.2012.01612_12.x
note: Root devotional stava by Bhaṭṭanārāyaṇa (Kashmir Śaiva); Kṣemarāja (pupil of Abhinavagupta, 11th c.) wrote the vivṛti included in the same e-text. Distinct from the playwright Bhaṭṭanārāyaṇa of the Veṇīsaṃhāra.

title: Bhāmatī
note: Bhāmatī = Vācaspati Miśra (9th-10th c.), ṭīkā on Śaṅkara's Brahmasūtrabhāṣya; founds the Bhāmatī-prasthāna. Author of the Bhāmatī proper is single and secure; conflation is only at the edition-bundle level (Kalpataru/Parimala = other authors), not within this text item.

title: Nyāyabhūṣaṇa
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 2 - Potter 1977.txt
note: Auto-commentary on Nyāyasāra; same author as root. Securely Bhāsarvajña.

title: Amṛtanādopaniṣad
note: Anonymous śruti (Yoga Upaniṣad). Commentary by Upaniṣad-brahmayogin is a separate, later layer not part of this text item.

title: Somānanda II: Parātriśikātātparyadīpikā and Śāktavijñāna
note: Two separate works conflated: a lost Parātriṃśikā commentary (Somānanda, attested only via Abhinavagupta) + the Śāktavijñāna (ascribed to Somānanda, not securely). Attribution to 'Somānanda II' is uncertain/disputed.

title: Śivadṛṣṭi
note: Mūla/kārikā by Somānanda(nātha); vṛtti by his pupil Utpaladeva. Two distinct authors in one edition.

title: Pārānandasūtra
note: Eponymous traditional ascription to Pārānanda Muni; no independent confirmation, no conflation.

title: Kulālaśāstra
note: No author determinable; anonymous revealed Tantric śāstra. No conflation.

title: Guhyāvalīvivṛti
note: Two distinct roles in one title: mūla verses (Guhyāvalī) by Dauḍīpāda, the vivṛti commentary by Ghanadeva. Siddha ascription is traditional; both names from the edition's own framing.

title: Prāṇābharaṇa
note: Single author; auto-commentary (ṭippaṇī) by the poet himself, so no conflation. Colophon explicit.

title: Bhagavadgītābhāṣya
note: Title conflates mūla (Gītā, traditionally Vyāsa) and bhāṣya (Śaṅkara). The Gītābhāṣya is one of Śaṅkara's securely authentic works per scholarly consensus; the verses themselves are not his.

title: Agni-Purāṇa
primary_author: anonymous (composite; traditionally narrated by Agni to Vasiṣṭha)
note: Composite encyclopedic Purāṇa, no historical author; traditional ascription is the divine narration frame (Agni→Vasiṣṭha). Multiple anonymous redactors over time but not distinct named authors, so conflation_flag false.

title: Cakrasaṃvarastuti
note: Genuinely anonymous Cakrasaṃvara hymn; no colophon names an author.

title: Catuḥṣaṣṭisaṃvarastotra
note: Genuinely anonymous Saṃvara (64-deity) hymn; no colophon authorship.

title: Śāktavijñāna
note: Ascribed to Somānanda (Somānandanātha), author of the Śivadṛṣṭi; traditional single-author Kashmir Śaiva attribution, no commentary layer conflated here.

title: Saundaryalaharī
primary_author: traditionally ascribed to Śaṅkarācārya (disputed; likely not by him)
note: Pan-Indian Śākta hymn; persistently ascribed to Ādi Śaṅkara but rejected by scholarship (Bharati). Two-section structure (Ānandalaharī / Saundaryalaharī) noted but no separately named author, hence disputed not conflated.

title: Vajrayoginyāḥ piṇḍārthastutiḥ
note: Short Vajrayāna stuti; no secure authorial ascription found. Do not conflate with the Virūpa-ascribed stutipraṇidhānam. Remains anonymous/traditional.

title: Mahāmantrānusāriṇī
primary_author: anonymous (canonical Buddhist sūtra, attributed to the Buddha by tradition)
note: Canonical protective sūtra (mahāsūtra); traditionally buddhavacana, no historical author. Editor Skilling is editor, not author.

title: Caryāgītikoṣa
current_author: multiple mahāsiddhas (Kāṇha, Lui-pā, Śabara, Bhusuku and others)
primary_author: multiple Buddhist Siddhācāryas/mahāsiddhas (Kāṇha/Kṛṣṇacaryā, Luipā, Śabara, Bhusuku, Saraha, et al.)
source: secondary:cleaned_Schaeffer_Saraha dissertation.txt; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; web:http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/833/1416
note: Anthology of ~50 caryā songs each ascribed to a different mahāsiddha (Apabhraṃśa originals with Skt. chāyā); genuine multi-author conflation. Current_author already lists the siddhas correctly.

title: Upadeśasāhasrī
primary_author: Śaṅkara (Ādi Śaṅkara) — padya (verse) and gadya (prose) parts
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Mimamsa and Vedanta. Interaction and Continuity_Johannes Bronkhorst, ed., 2007.txt
note: Verse (padya/Padyabandha) and prose (gadya/Gadyabandha) sections are both Śaṅkara's; one author, two genres — not a multi-author conflation. Authenticity defended by Mayeda; the most securely genuine of Śaṅkara's prakaraṇa works.

title: Matsyendranātha: Jñānakārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_YOGA, Immortality & Freedom_Mircea Eliade; secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Studies in Honour of Hammalava Saddhatissa
note: Traditional ascription to the legendary siddha Matsyendranātha; in practice a text of his school/lineage, not a verified individual author.

title: Kriyāsāratattvaprakāśinīvyākhyā
current_author: uncertain (a commentary on the Kriyāsāra of Nīlakaṇṭhaśivācārya)
source: secondary:cleaned_Understanding Mantras_Harvey P. Alper; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy
note: Two distinct roles conflated under one title: root Kriyāsāra = Nīlakaṇṭhaśivācārya; commentary (vyākhyā) author unknown. Commentary author remains anonymous/uncertain.

title: Mīmāṃsāślokavārttikakāśikā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy...Tarkabhasa_Kajiyama; secondary:cleaned_Problem of Knowledge in Yogacara Buddhism_Tripathi
note: current_author lists only the commentator. Title conflates root verses (Kumārila) and commentary (Sucaritamiśra); both attributions secure.

title: Yogaśikhopaniṣat (Yogaśikhā Upaniṣad)
source: secondary:cleaned_Roots of Yoga_Mallinson & Singleton; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol.1_Potter
note: Base Upaniṣad anonymous; the edition's commentary is by Upaniṣad-brahmayogin (recorded as a distinct role). Not a conflation of the work itself.

title: kālikārahasya
note: Genuinely unknown author; anonymous tantric Śākta text. No traditional individual ascription identified.

title: Spandakārikā, with Vivṛti by Rāmakaṇṭha
current_author: Spandakārikā by Kallaṭa Bhaṭṭa (or Vasugupta); Vivṛti commentary by Rāmakaṇṭha
primary_author: Spandakārikā: Vasugupta or Kallaṭa Bhaṭṭa (disputed); Vivṛti: Rāmakaṇṭha (Rājānaka Rāma)
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_Śaivism and the tantric traditions; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy
note: Two-author conflation plus a dispute: root kārikās attributed to Vasugupta OR Kallaṭa Bhaṭṭa (unresolved); commentary (Vivṛti/Vivaraṇa) securely by Rāmakaṇṭha (Rājānaka Rāma, 10th c.).

title: Tantrasadbhāva (selected chapters)
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (Goudriaan 1992); secondary:cleaned_Yoga Powers (Jacobsen 2012); web
note: Anonymous revealed Śaiva Tantra (Trika Vidyāpīṭha); cited by Kṣemarāja; current_author already empty.

title: Dharmottarapradīpa
primary_author: Durveka Miśra (Dharmottarapradīpa); subcommentary on Dharmottara's Nyāyabinduṭīkā on Dharmakīrti's Nyāyabindu
note: Subcommentary securely by Durveka Miśra (c.1100); embeds Dharmottara's ṭīkā and Dharmakīrti's mūla — distinct authors per layer.

title: Triśikhibrāhmaṇopaniṣat (Triśikhibrāhmaṇa Upaniṣad)
source: secondary:cleaned_White_Yoga in Practice; secondary:cleaned_YOGA Immortality & Freedom (Eliade); edition
note: Upaniṣad itself anonymous (late Yoga Upaniṣad); commentary by Upaniṣad-Brahma-Yogin — distinct named commentator.

title: Rasahṛdayatantra
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion (Bhattacharyya); secondary:cleaned_Maas_Rasayana
note: Mūla by Govindabhagavatpāda (Govinda Bhikṣu, alchemist; identity vs Śaṅkara's guru uncertain); commentary Mugdhāvabodhinī by Caturbhujamiśra — distinct authors.

title: Jñānasiddhyāgama
note: Anonymous revealed Śaiva āgama (Sanderson); distinct from Buddhist Jñānasiddhi of Indrabhūti. No author.

title: Gaṇakārikā 1 (with Ratnaṭīkā)
current_author: kārikās attributed to Haradattācārya, with the Ratnaṭīkā of Bhāsarvajña
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol.2 (Potter 1977); secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit and Indian Studies (Ingalls Fs); web
note: Disputed: kārikās traditionally ascribed to Haradattācārya but credited to Bhāsarvajña by Potter/edition; Ratnaṭīkā commentary securely by Bhāsarvajña (10th c.). Roles separated.

title: Ghaṭakarparakāvya
current_author: Ghaṭakarpara (traditionally ascribed to Kālidāsa); NOTE: the project hint 'Abhinavagupta' refers to the commentator, not the poet
primary_author: Anonymous poet, traditionally ascribed to Kālidāsa (kāvya); Abhinavagupta (vivṛti/commentary)
note: Two distinct persons conflated by edition title: the (pseudo-/traditionally-Kālidāsa) poet of the kāvya vs Abhinavagupta the commentator. Kāvya attribution to Kālidāsa is spurious/traditional; sometimes the poet 'Ghaṭakarpara' is named eponymously from the last verse.

title: Dīkṣottara
note: Anonymous revealed Śaiva tantra; presented as Śiva's teaching. Traditionally part of the Kālottara/Niśvāsa scriptural corpus; no individual ascription.

title: Vakroktijīvita
note: Verses and auto-vṛtti both by Kuntaka, so NOT a multi-author conflation despite kārikā/vṛtti structure. Attribution secure.

title: Tattvajñānasaṁsiddhi-mahāsukharakāśikā-ṭīkā
primary_author: Bhikṣu Śrī Dhyānacandra (ṭīkā Mahāsukhaprakāśikā); Śūnyasamādhivajra (root Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi)
note: Two distinct authors: root verses by Śūnyasamādhivajra(pāda) vs the ṭīkā Mahāsukhaprakāśikā by Dhyānacandra. (Note: a separate Marmakalikāpañjikā on this root is by Vīryaśrīmitra per Pandey's Sarnath 2000 ed. — different commentary, not this one.)

title: Vajra(mahā)bhairavatantra
primary_author: Anonymous (revealed tantra; traditionally revealed to Lalitavajra)
source: secondary:cleaned_Buddhism between Tibet and China_Matthew Kapstein, ed. 2009.txt + web:https://ora.ox.ac.uk/objects/uuid:3e18e7fb-22de-407d-a2c8-3b3e43c7b7aa
note: Anonymous revealed Buddhist tantra. Lalitavajra is the traditional revealer/discoverer, not the author. Wenta is the modern editor, not the author.

title: Bhāgavata-Purāṇa 1
note: Traditional ascription to Vyāsa recorded but not historical; composite/anonymous Purāṇa. Single composite work, so no multi-author conflation flag despite redactional layering.

title: Sāṃkhyatattvakaumudī
source: secondary:Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy; secondary:Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol.2 - Potter
note: Kaumudī securely by Vācaspatimiśra (~10th c.); it is a commentary on a separate root text, the Sāṃkhyakārikā of Īśvarakṛṣṇa. Roles listed for completeness but no genuine conflation within this commentary.

title: Saundaryalaharī
note: Tantric Śākta hymn; traditional ascription to Ādi Śaṅkara is widely rejected by scholars. Tradition sometimes splits it: first 41 verses (Ānandalaharī) said to be revealed/by a separate hand vs. the rest — but no secure named author. Kept as disputed/effectively anonymous.

title: Mahānayaprakāśa (anonymous)
current_author: anonymous (one of several Krama works of this title; sometimes ascribed to Arṇasiṃha)
primary_author: anonymous (Krama work; sometimes ascribed to Arṇasiṃha)
note: Title shared by several distinct authors (Śitikaṇṭha, Arṇasiṃha, and anonymous); THIS item (TSS 130) is the anonymous one. A library-catalogue ascription of TSS 130 to Abhinavagupta is a confusion across the homonymous titles. Conflation is across works sharing the title, not within this single work, so conflation_flag kept false; no secure author.

title: Satyaśāsanaparīkṣā
source: secondary:Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol.1 - Potter; web:https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10781-011-9147-7
note: Single author, secure. Transmitted incomplete (planned chapters lost) but no authorship dispute or multi-author structure.

title: Śivastotrāvalī
note: Verses securely by Utpaladeva. A separate commentary by Kṣemarāja exists but is NOT part of this GRETIL e-text (verses only), so no conflation in this item.

title: Śrīkaṇṭha: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Ratnatrayaparīkṣā
source: secondary:Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism (Goudriaan ed.); secondary:Vac_Padoux 1990
note: This item is the Ratnatraya(parīkṣā) kārikā by Śrīkaṇṭha alone. The Aṣṭaprakaraṇa is a multi-author anthology (other tracts — Tattvaprakāśa, Bhogakārikā, Mokṣakārikā etc. — are by Sadyojyoti, and a Tattvasaṅgraha by Sadyojyoti), and commentaries on the Ratnatraya are by Rāmakaṇṭha/Aghoraśiva; but the Ratnatraya text itself is single-author Śrīkaṇṭha, so conflation_flag is for the collection, not this work.

title: Utpaladeva: Īśvarasiddhi
note: Part of Utpaladeva's Siddhitrayī (Ajaḍapramātṛ-, Īśvara-, Sambandha-siddhi); securely his.

title: Subodhamañjarī
note: Colophon ascribes to Vāmanadatta; identity overlaps with Dvayasampatti/Bodhavilāsa author cluster (Vāmananātha/Hrasvanātha). current_author was blank.

title: Mokṣopāya
note: Anonymous Kashmirian work; later vulgate redaction = Yogavāsiṣṭha pseudepigraphically ascribed to Vālmīki. Traditional ascription to Vālmīki noted; not historical.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvṛtti
note: Auto-commentary: both kārikā and vṛtti by Utpaladeva (son of Udayākara). Same author for both layers — not a conflation.

title: Bhagavadgītā
primary_author: Bhagavadgītā: anonymous (trad. Vyāsa); Sarvatobhadra commentary: Rājānaka Rāmakaṇṭha
note: Title conflates the anonymous/Vyāsa-ascribed Gītā with Rāmakaṇṭha's Sarvatobhadra ṭīkā — distinct authors/roles separated.

title: Ajaḍapramātṛsiddhi
note: Securely Utpaladeva; first of the three Siddhi treatises. Colophon-era note (Śaka 1841) is the editor's, not original authorship.

title: Mokṣopāya
note: Genuinely anonymous root text. Traditional/pseudepigraphic ascription to Vālmīki (sage Vasiṣṭha as speaker) belongs to the later Yogavāsiṣṭha recension, not this Mokṣopāya edition.

title: Sambandhasiddhi
source: tradition; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Securely Utpaladeva (c. 925-975). The Siddhitrayī carried Utpaladeva's autocommentary (vṛtti); this work-item targets the kārikā text itself.

title: Śivastotrāvalī
note: Two distinct authors: root hymns by Utpaladeva vs. Vivṛti by Kṣemarāja. Current_author lists only Utpaladeva; the commentary author was omitted.

title: Mṛgendrapaddhatiṭīkā
current_author: Naṭeśaguru (alias Vaktraśambhu); on the Mṛgendrapaddhati attributed to Aghoraśivācārya
primary_author: Naṭeśaguru alias Vaktraśambhu (ṭīkā); on Aghoraśiva's Mṛgendrapaddhati
note: The ṭīkā itself is securely Naṭeśaguru/Vaktraśambhu; it comments on a distinct author's work (Aghoraśiva's Mṛgendrapaddhati). Two distinct persons bound in one title -> conflation_flag true. Current_author already captured both.

title: Siddhitraya: Part 3: Saṃvitsiddhi
note: Root verses securely Yāmuna (c. 10th c. Śrīvaiṣṇava). The GRETIL etext bundles a 'Siddhāñjana' commentary by a different/unnamed (likely modern editorial) hand -> conflation. Commentator identity uncertain; flagged low confidence.

title: Lalitā Sahasranāma
current_author: anonymous/traditional (framed as revealed by the Vāgdevatās; dialogue of Hayagrīva and Agastya)
note: Anonymous/traditional stotra root vs. named commentator Bhāskararāya. The work-item title is the stotra but the edition (Saubhāgyabhāskara) conflates it with Bhāskararāya's commentary -> conflation_flag true.

title: Timirodghāṭa(tantra)
note: Anonymous revealed tantra ('Dispelling of Darkness'); no human author named in any source. Current 'anonymous/traditional' confirmed.

title: Sarvamatasaṃgraha
note: Anonymous Vedānta-leaning doxographical compendium; not to be conflated with the Sarvadarśanasaṃgraha. Attribution genuinely uncertain; current label confirmed.

title: Vivekacūḍāmaṇi
current_author: Traditionally attributed to Śaṃkara (Ādi Śaṅkara); authorship disputed by modern scholarship
note: Colophon names Śaṅkarācārya; modern scholarship divided (Ingalls/Hacker/Grimes). Single (disputed) author, not multi-author. Current 'disputed' label confirmed.

title: Ratnatrayaparīkṣā
note: Authorial colophon names Śrīkaṇṭhasūri; work follows Rāmakaṇṭha's vṛtti (influence, not co-authorship). Secure single author. Current attribution confirmed.

title: Svabodhodayamañjarī
note: Colophon names Vāmanadatta son of Harṣadatta; Sanderson distinguishes him from another Vāmanadatta. Secure single author. Current attribution confirmed.

title: Aṣṭāṅganighaṇṭu
note: Traditional ascription to Vāhaṭa/Vāgbhaṭa; which of the homonymous Vāgbhaṭas is unsettled. Not multi-author. Current attribution confirmed as traditional, not secure.

title: Pāśupatabrahma Upaniṣad
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt; tradition
note: Root Upaniṣad anonymous (late, of the Atharvaveda corpus); the edition's commentary is by Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin (Rāmacandrendra Sarasvatī), 18th c. — distinct role conflated under one title.

title: Rasādhyāya
note: Rasaśāstra/alchemy text; author self-identifies only as a pupil of Kaṅkālaya (Kaṅkālanātha), name unstated -> anonymous, traditional teacher-ascription noted. ṭīkā is a distinct (anonymous) layer printed with the text.

title: Laghuyogavāsiṣṭha, with commentary Vāsiṣṭhacandrikā
current_author: abridgement traditionally ascribed to Abhinanda (of Kashmir); commentary Vāsiṣṭhacandrikā by Ātmasukha (Mummaḍideva)
primary_author: Abhinanda (abridgement); Ātmasukha (Vāsiṣṭhacandrikā, prakaraṇas 1–3); Mummaḍi Devarāya (Saṃsārataraṇī, prakaraṇas 4–6)
note: Current_author wrongly equates Ātmasukha with Mummaḍideva; in fact they are two different commentators — Ātmasukha (Vāsiṣṭhacandrikā, prak. 1–3) and Mummaḍi Devarāya (Saṃsārataraṇī, prak. 4–6). Plus Abhinanda as abridger. Three distinct hands.

title: Nārada-Purāṇa 2 [Nāradīya-Purāṇa 2]
note: Anonymous Mahāpurāṇa (Uttarabhāga); colophon style 'śrībṛhannāradīyapurāṇa'. Traditional ascription to Vyāsa only — no individual author identifiable.

title: hayaśīrṣapañcarātra
note: Revealed Vaiṣṇava Āgama (Pāñcarātra), framed as Viṣṇu's revelation in his horse-headed (Hayaśīrṣa) form -> anonymous by genre; no individual author.

title: Nārada-Purāṇa 1 [Nāradīya-Purāṇa 1]
note: Anonymous Mahāpurāṇa (Pūrvabhāga); colophon 'śrībṛhannāradīyapurāṇe pūrvabhāge'. Traditional Vyāsa ascription only.

title: Hetutattvopadeśa
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Minor Buddhist Texts. Part I _Giuseppe Tucci, 1956.txt
note: Colophon names Paṇḍitarāja Jitāripāda (Jitāri, c. 940-1000, teacher of Atiśa). Secure single-author work.

title: Sāmānyadūṣaṇadikprasāritā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Problem of Knowledge in Yogacara Buddhism_Chhote Lal Tripathi, 1972.txt
note: Buddhist nyāya tract refuting universals (sāmānya). Author Paṇḍitāśoka secure; dating debated (verse shared with Jñānaśrīmitra's Apohaprakaraṇa, per skt-en-mono).

title: Paraśambhumahimnastava (Paraśambhumahimnaḥstava)
current_author: attributed to one styled 'Krodhabhaṭṭāraka' (alias; the epithet is traditionally associated with the sage Durvāsas in the Krama tradition)
primary_author: ascribed to Durvāsas (under the alias Krodhabhaṭṭāraka)
note: Devotional Krama stava. Attribution to Durvāsas/Krodhabhaṭṭāraka is legendary (the sage is a mythic lineage-founder); actual human author unknown. Pseudepigraphic.

title: Hetutattvopadeśa
note: Duplicate edition of the Jitāri text (Chattopadhyaya Skt-reconstruction from Tibetan). Author Jitāri secure; Tibetan translators are a separate role, not authorial.

title: Jātinirākṛti
current_author: anonymous/traditional (attributed in some editions to Dharmakīrti or his school)
source: secondary:cleaned_Minor Buddhist Texts. Part I _Giuseppe Tucci, 1956.txt; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: CHANGED: current_author claims anonymous/Dharmakīrti, but colophon and three independent scholarly sources (Tucci, Bühnemann, Aris) securely attribute to Jitāri. 'jitāripādānām' = honorific plural for Jitāri, not a school.

title: Mokṣasopāna
note: Yoga/mokṣa text. Author known only by the cryptic epithet Kāṇḍadvayātītayogī (likely a self-designation); real identity and date undetermined. Treated as uncertain, not anonymous, since the epithet is the text's own claim.

title: Rasādhyāya
note: Anonymous alchemical Rasādhyāya plus an anonymous ṭīkā printed together (two unnamed hands). Do not confuse with the Nāṭyaśāstra Rasādhyāya (Bharata, with Abhinavagupta's Abhinavabhāratī).

title: Tarkarahasya
note: Anonymous late Buddhist pramāṇa work; occasional/unconfirmed ascription to Jitāri rejected by EAST. Distinct from Guṇaratna's Tarkarahasyadīpikā.

title: Rāmakaṇṭha: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Nādakārikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Edition conflates root kārikā (Rāmakaṇṭha) with Aghoraśiva's commentary; current_author records only the kārikā author.

title: Svabodhasiddhi
note: Single-author Kashmirian Śaiva work, secure per Hanneder 2017.

title: Sāmbapañcāśikā
note: Pseudepigraphic hymn ascribed to legendary Sāmba; printed edition conflates the stotra with Kṣemarāja's commentary (the one historically identifiable hand).

title: Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha: Vyomavyāpistava
current_author: Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha (II) (commentator); the Vyomavyāpin/Vyomavyāpistava hymn itself is a Śaiva mantra-stotra
source: secondary:cleaned_Self's Awareness of Itself Bhatta Ramakantha's Arguments Against the Buddhist Doctrine of No-Self_Alex Watson, 2006.txt
note: Vyomavyāpistava is Rāmakaṇṭha's own composition (a stava on the Vyomavyāpin mantra), not a commentary; single author. Current_author's 'commentator' framing corrected to author.

title: Dviśatikālottara
note: Revealed Śaiva Siddhānta scripture; one of several Kālottara recensions by verse-count (200 = dviśati). No human author; commentaries are by others (Rāmakaṇṭha, etc.) and not part of this root text.

title: Gītārthasaṅgraha
note: Yāmunācārya (c. 10th c., teacher/grandfather-figure of the Śrīvaiṣṇava lineage before Rāmānuja). The verse-summary of the Gītā; not to be conflated with Vedānta Deśika's later -rakṣā commentary.

title: Īśvarasiddhi
note: Second of the three siddhi-treatises; survives incomplete/fragmentary like the rest of the Siddhitraya. Secure to Yāmunācārya.

title: Laghustuti, with commentary by Rāghavānanda
current_author: Laghubhaṭṭāraka (also a name used in the Krama/Śākta tradition); commentary by Rāghavānanda
note: Single title conflates two distinct authors/roles: root stuti (traditionally Laghubhaṭṭāraka, attribution medium-confidence/uncertain in scholarship) vs. the vṛtti by Rāghavānanda. Roles separated.

title: Siddhitraya: Part 1: Ātmasiddhi
note: First of the three siddhis. Traditionally/securely Yāmunācārya; text survives only in fragmentary form. 'Traditionally considered part of...' (Bronkhorst) but attribution to Yāmuna itself is uncontested.

title: Ahirbudhnyasaṃhitā, Volume 1
source: web:tradition (Pāñcarātra āgama) + secondary:cleaned_Book of the Goddess Past and Present_Carl Olson, 2002
note: Revealed Pāñcarātra scripture; no human author. Editors Ramanujacharya (ed.) & Schrader (supervisor) listed as compiler/redactor roles only, not attributed authorship. Traditional dialogic frame Ahirbudhnya->Nārada.

title: Laghutantraṭīkā
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci’s Collection. Part I_Francesco Sferra, ed, 2008.txt; web:https://www.academia.edu/45511596/
note: Author is the commentator; the root Cakrasaṃvaratantra it glosses is a distinct anonymous/revealed scripture and is not the work being attributed here, so no conflation. 'Bodhisattva Vajrapāṇi' is the honorific used in the tradition/edition title.

title: Kālasaṃkarṣiṇīmatatantraṭippaṇī
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_Śaivism and the tantric traditions.txt; tradition
note: Glossator unidentified; the underlying Kālasaṃkarṣiṇīmata is a traditionally revealed (anonymous) Krama tantra. No author can be assigned without inventing one.

title: Bodhicaryāvatārapañjikā
note: Pañjikā by Prajñākaramati (c. 10th-11th c.); root Bodhicaryāvatāra by Śāntideva (c. 8th c.). The single title/file conflates root verses + commentary by different authors, hence conflation_flag true; primary commentary attribution (Prajñākaramati) is unchanged.

title: Kramastotra
note: Self-named author in colophon and final verse; the famous Kashmiri Krama/Pratyabhijñā master Abhinavagupta. Note: an older Krama Kramastotra/Kramastava tradition exists, but this text explicitly names Abhinavagupta.

title: Kauśikapaddhati
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_From Material to Deity. Indian Rituals of Consecrations_Einoo & Takashima, ed., 2005.txt
note: Keśava (c. 11th c.), author of this paddhati on the Kauśikasūtra of the Atharvaveda. Attribution secure and undisputed.

title: Kalāvilāsa
note: Kṣemendra, 11th-c. Kashmiri satirist/poet; attribution secure via internal colophons. This didactic-satirical kāvya is part of his well-documented corpus.

title: vāruṇapaddhativyākhyāna
note: Base root-paddhati = Varuṇaśivācārya; the vyākhyāna/commentary (this title) is itself anonymous. Distinct from the root work — title conflates root author and unnamed commentator.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 10. Gadāparvan
note: Colophon 'kṣemendraviracitāyāṃ bhāratamañjaryāṃ'; secure 11th-c. Kashmiri author. Matches current_author.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 13. Śāntiparvan
note: Parvan-section of Kṣemendra's single-author Bhāratamañjarī; secure. Matches current_author.

title: Dhvanyāloka [Sahṛdayāloka]
primary_author: Ānandavardhana (vṛtti); kārikās disputed (possibly an earlier Dhvanikāra)
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit and Indian Studies. Essays in Honour of Daniel H.H. Ingalls...; web:wisdomlib.org
note: Genuine multi-author dispute: kārikās possibly by an anonymous Dhvanikāra, vṛtti by Ānandavardhana. The Locana (separate commentary by Abhinavagupta) is NOT in this e-text. Refines current single-author attribution.

title: Āgamaprāmāṇya
source: gretil-header (muktabodha e-text); secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Indian Philosophy_Christopher Bartley
note: Single author Yāmuna, Śrīvaiṣṇava ācārya. Only the floruit dating is contested, not the attribution. Matches current_author.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 18. Svargaparvan
note: Colophon 'śrīkṣemendraviracitāyāṃ bhāratamañjaryāṃ svargaparva'; secure. Matches current_author.

title: Mukundamālā
note: Colophon names King Kulaśekhara; traditionally identified with the Tamil Āḻvār. No conflicting attribution in the edition.

title: Daśarūpaka
note: Root kārikā work by Dhanañjaya. Edition title mentions Avaloka by Dhanika, but this e-text omits the commentary; thus single author. (Dhanika is generally held to be Dhanañjaya's brother/associate.)

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 12. Strīparvan
note: Colophon explicitly names Kṣemendra. Sansknet/GRETIL only as modern digitizers.

title: Ṣaṭsāhasrikā Hevajratantrapiṇḍārthaṭīkā, Ch. 1 (ed. Sferra 2009)
note: current_author empty; assigned Vajragarbha (traditional ascription, well attested in Tibetan tradition). Sferra is modern editor. NB: source_url 44704162 is Sferra's related 'Elucidation of True Reality' Kālacakra-commentary piece; the 2009 'Laud of the Chosen Deity' is the ch.1 edition cited. Authorship of later chapters of the whole ṭīkā is doubted, but not relevant to ch.1; not a conflation within this item.

title: Dhvanyāloka [Sahṛdayāloka], Uddyota 4
note: Conflation: kārikā-layer (Dhvanikāra/'Sahṛdaya', per Abhinavagupta) vs vṛtti (Ānandavardhana). Disputed; some scholars give both to Ānandavardhana. Uddyota 4 colophon names Ānandavardhana as vṛtti author.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 9. Śalyaparvan
note: Colophon names Kṣemendra. Sansknet/GRETIL only modern digitizers.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 19. Harivaṃśa
note: Single-author kāvya abridgment; section division follows the MBh parvans but authorship is uniform (11th c. Kashmiri Kṣemendra).

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 6. Bhīṣmaparvan
note: Editorial parvan division; one author throughout.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 17. Mahāprasthānikaparvan
note: Editorial parvan division; one author throughout.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 8. Karṇaparvan
note: Editorial parvan division; one author throughout.

title: Bodhisattvāvadānakalpalatā
current_author: Kṣemendra (with the final avadāna added by his son Somendra)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Kaul_Buddhism in Kashmir.txt; secondary:cleaned_Lin_Adapting the Buddha's Biographies...txt
note: Conflation: father Kṣemendra wrote pallavas 1-107; son Somendra wrote the prefatory matter and the final (108th) avadāna and added the colophon. Current_author already noted Somendra's final avadāna; refined to separate roles and add Somendra's introduction.

title: Darpadalana
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue...; secondary:cleaned_Lin_Adapting the Buddha's Biographies...txt
note: Self-attribution in the text itself; single author.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 7. Droṇaparvan
note: Single-author kāvya epitome; colophon explicitly names Kṣemendra as composer (viracita). 11th-c. Kashmir.

title: Sumāgadhāvadāna [Sumāgadhāvadānasūtra]
source: secondary:cleaned_Introduction to the Buddhist Tantric Systems_Lessing & Wayman, 1968.txt
note: Part of Avadānakalpalatā (collection conflates Kṣemendra 1-107 + Somendra ch.108 + Somendra's intro verses), but THIS pallava no.93 is securely Kṣemendra's own. Whole collection is multi-author; this individual avadāna is not.

title: Vimalaprabhāṭīkā on Śrīlaghukālacakratantrarāja (ed. Upādhyāya 1986, Vol.1)
note: current_author empty; volume conflates root tantra (Mañjuśrīyaśas) and Vimalaprabhā commentary (Puṇḍarīka). Both ascriptions are traditional/legendary (Kalkin kings of Śambhala).

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 11. Sauptikaparvan
note: Single-author kāvya; secure attribution to Kṣemendra (11th-c. Kashmir).

title: Nyāyakusumāñjali: Stavaka 2
primary_author: Udayana (Udayanācārya) [kārikās disputed: possibly earlier Nyāya author]
note: GRETIL file is the kārikā (verse) layer — the very part Ruzsa argues is by an earlier author, not Udayana. Traditional attribution still Udayana; verses' authorship disputed. Prose auto-commentary (Udayana) not in this file; four named commentaries (Bodhanī etc.) in print edition also absent from GRETIL text.

title: Nyāyakusumāñjali: Stavaka 1
primary_author: Udayana (Udayanācārya) [kārikās disputed: possibly earlier Nyāya author]
note: GRETIL file = kārikā layer (the disputed verses). Traditional/secure for most scholarship = Udayana; Ruzsa 2022 disputes verse authorship and posits verse-vs-prose conflation. Print edition's four commentaries (Bodhanī/Prakāśa/Prakāśikā/Makaranda by separate authors) not present in this text file.

title: Kubjikāmatatantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Kaula scripture; tradition: Śiva/Kubjikā revelation)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Anonymous revealed Kaula tantra; editors Goudriaan & Schoterman are modern editors, not authors. Traditional ascription is divine revelation, not a historical person.

title: Garbhāvakrāntyavadāna
note: Pallava 10, securely Kṣemendra; only pallava 108 is by son Somendra, so no conflation for this chapter. Somendra added short framing verses to each pallava but the avadāna proper is Kṣemendra's.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 14. Āśvamedhikaparvan
note: Secure single-author work; parvan division is internal structure, not separate authorship.

title: Mukundamālā
primary_author: Kulaśekhara (traditionally the Āḻvār / Cera king; attribution disputed)
note: Traditional ascription to Kulaśekhara Āḻvār is disputed; possibly a later Kulaśekhara. Single author either way (no conflation), but status downgraded from secure to disputed.

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 15. Āśramavāsikaparvan
note: Secure single-author work; parvan is internal structure only.

title: Dhvanyāloka [Sahṛdayāloka], Uddyota 3
primary_author: Ānandavardhana (vṛtti); kārikās possibly by an earlier 'dhvanikāra' (disputed)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit and Indian Studies. Essays in Honour of Daniel H.H. Ingalls_Bimal Krishna Matilal, M. Nagatomi, J. M. Masson, E. C. Dimock Jr.,1980_a.txt
note: Genuine kārikā-vs-vṛtti authorship controversy (parallel to MSA). Vṛtti securely Ānandavardhana; kārikās possibly by an earlier anonymous 'dhvanikāra'. Flagged as conflated/disputed. Note: this work = the integral Dhvanyāloka including its kārikās; Uddyota 3 is a chapter division.

title: Pradīpoddyotanaṭīkā (commentary on the Guhyasamājatantra)
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Wedemeyer diss.txt
note: Attributed to Candrakīrti but the tantric Candrakīrti (author of this Guhyasamāja commentary, ~9th c., Ārya tradition) is distinct from the Madhyamaka Candrakīrti; identity disputed. Commentary on the Guhyasamājatantra root tantra (root tantra anonymous, not by Candrakīrti).

title: Bhāratamañjarī: 16. Mausalaparvan
note: Secure single authorship; 11th-c. Kashmiri polymath Kṣemendra. No multi-author structure (a verse epitome, not a root+commentary).

title: Vṛttaratnākara (with Sulhaṇa's Sukavihṛdayānandinī commentary)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Hahn_A brief introduction into the Indian metrical system...txt; secondary:cleaned_Untersuchungen zur Buddhistischen Literatur...1997.txt
note: This GRETIL file conflates two authors: the mūla metrics treatise by Kedārabhaṭṭa and the Sukavihṛdayānandinī commentary by Sulhaṇa. current_author lists only Kedārabhaṭṭa.

title: Paramārthasāra
note: The mūla verses are Abhinavagupta's (itself an adaptation of the Ādhāra/Śeṣa Paramārthasāra); the included vivṛti is by Yogarāja. Root attribution to Abhinavagupta secure; file conflates root + commentary author. current_author omits Yogarāja.

title: Dhvanyāloka [Sahṛdayāloka], Uddyota 1
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit and Indian Studies. Essays in Honour of Daniel H.H. Ingalls...1980_a.txt; secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Philosophy of the Middle...Ruegg, 2010_a.txt
note: Classic kārikā-vs-vṛtti authorship dispute: kārikās possibly by a separate older Dhvanikāra, vṛtti securely Ānandavardhana's. Uddyota 1 of the root work (kārikā+vṛtti); Abhinavagupta's Locana NOT included. Flagged as conflation per the disputed two-author structure.

title: Śivajñānasiddhi
current_author: uncertain (a Śivajñānasiddhi is associated with Arulnandi Śivācārya; Tamil tradition)
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; web:https://muktabodha.org/digital-library/
note: Traditionally ascribed to Aruḷnandi (Aruṇanti) Śivācārya; original is Tamil (Civañāṉacittiyār, Cupakkam + Parapakkam), this being a Sanskrit rendering/version. Distinct from Bhojadeva's homonymous works; attribution traditional, not text-critically secured for the Sanskrit recension.

title: Antarvyāptisamarthana
note: Secure single-author logic tract. Note antarvyāpti is also championed by Ratnakīrti, but this specific Samarthana is Ratnākaraśānti's.

title: Bodhapañcadaśikā
note: Internal colophon names Abhinavagupta; short fifteen-verse Pratyabhijñā/Trika digest.

title: Vṛttaratnākara
note: Root verses by Kedārabhaṭṭa; the included commentary Sukavihṛdayānandinī is by Sulhaṇa (a distinct, later author) — title conflates the two roles.

title: Vyomavatī
source: secondary:Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 2 - Potter 1977
note: Vyomavatī itself is wholly Vyomaśiva's commentary; Praśastapāda listed only as author of the commented base text (not conflated within this work).

title: Nyāyakusumāñjali: Stavaka 3
note: Third stabaka of Udayana's theistic Nyāya work; e-text is root only, so no commentary conflation here despite the multi-commentary edition.

title: Tantrasāra
note: Self-abridgement of the Tantrāloka; do not confuse with Mādhva or other Tantrasāra works.

title: Mālinīślokavārttika: Kāṇḍa 1
note: Authorial vārttika by Abhinavagupta; root Mālinīvijayottaratantra is a distinct (anonymous/scriptural) work and is not bundled here, so no conflation.

title: Nyāyakusumāñjali: Stavaka 5
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 2 - Potter 1977.txt
note: Root text of Udayana alone (5th stavaka); commentaries (e.g. Varadarāja, Vardhamāna, Haridāsa) not present in this e-text, so no conflation.

title: Paryantapañcāśikā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Colophon names Abhinavagupta; single-author stotra/philosophical poem of ~50 verses.

title: Hevajratantra with Muktāvalī Pañjikā of Ratnākaraśānti
source: secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism_ A Social History of the Tantric Movement.txt; tradition
note: Root tantra anonymous/Buddha-vacana; commentary (Muktāvalī Pañjikā) by Ratnākaraśānti (~early 11th c.). Title/author fields were empty; this title conflates root scripture + commentator.

title: Nyāyakusumāñjali: Stavaka 4
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 2 - Potter 1977.txt
note: Root text of Udayana alone (4th stavaka); no commentary bundled, so no conflation.

title: Anuttarāṣṭikā
note: Minor 8-verse stotra; traditional ascription to Abhinavagupta accepted, but for short stotras the attribution is conventional rather than independently secured.

title: Tantrasāra
note: Secure. Abhinavagupta's own prose abridgement of his Tantrāloka.

title: Nibandhāvalī
note: Collection of multiple WORKS but a single author; not an author-conflation. SARIT XML returned 502 at fetch time; confirmed via Thakur edition descriptions.

title: Parātriṃśikāvivaraṇa
note: Abhinavagupta is author of the vivaraṇa. The brief root verses (Parātriṃśikā) are anonymous/āgamic but are not the unit catalogued here.

title: Tantrāloka
note: Root by Abhinavagupta (c. 1000 CE); commentary -Viveka- by Rājānaka Jayaratha (~12th-13th c.). Current_author records only Abhinavagupta; the bundled Jayaratha commentary makes this an author-conflation.

title: Bhagavadgītā
current_author: anonymous/traditional (ascribed to Vyāsa; embedded in the Mahābhārata)
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Two distinct hands bundled: anonymous/Vyāsa Gītā + Abhinavagupta's Gītārthasaṃgraha. Current_author records only the Gītā mūla; commentator omitted.

title: Bhāvacūḍāmaṇi
note: Author secure = Vidyākaṇṭha. CAUTION: edition metadata wrongly links it to the Mṛgendrāgama/Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha; the Bhāvacūḍāmaṇi is actually on the Mayasaṃgraha. Father Vidyākaṇṭha vs son Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha are distinct.

title: Tattvaratnāvaloka [Tattvaratnāloka]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Tibetan Renaissance. Tantric Buddhism in the Rebirth of Tibetan Culture_Ronald M. Davidson, 2005.txt
note: Single-author tantric work; colophon names Vāgīśvarakīrti. No conflation.

title: Parātrīśikālaghuvṛtti
current_author: Abhinavagupta (the catalogue 'unknown' author hint is incorrect)
source: secondary:cleaned_skt-en-mono.txt; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt
note: Title conflates the anonymous Parātrīśikā scripture (Rudrayāmala) with Abhinavagupta's Laghuvṛtti commentary. Vṛtti securely Abhinavagupta; catalogue 'unknown' hint refers only to the scriptural verses.

title: Tantrāloka, chapters 15-38 (āhnikas)
note: Mūla (āhnikas 15-38) securely by Abhinavagupta. Jayaratha's Viveka is a separate later commentator bundled in the edition; listed for role-clarity but the item is Abhinava's text, so not flagged as conflation.

title: parātriṃśikāvivaraṇa
note: Title conflates the anonymous Parātriṃśikā scripture with Abhinavagupta's Vivaraṇa. Commentary securely Abhinavagupta; root verses anonymous/revealed.

title: Abhinavagupta: Bodhapañcadaśikā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt
note: Short single-author Śaiva primer; self-naming colophon. No conflation.

title: Dhvanyāloka [Sahṛdayāloka], Uddyota 2
primary_author: Ānandavardhana (vṛtti); kārikās ascribed to the 'Dhvanikāra' (possibly Ānandavardhana himself or an earlier author)
note: Kārikā vs vṛtti authorship historically disputed (Dhvanikāra hypothesis). E-text item = Dhvanyāloka mūla+vṛtti, Uddyota 2; vṛtti securely Ānandavardhana, kārikās' separate authorship unresolved. Locana (Abhinavagupta) not included here.

title: Mālinīvijayavārttika
source: secondary:cleaned_Vac. The Concept of the Word in Selected Hindu Tantras_Andre Padoux, 1990.txt; secondary:cleaned_Tournier_Eltschinger_Sernesi_Archaeologies of the Written.txt
note: Single-author Kashmir Śaiva vārttika by Abhinavagupta; edition by Madhusūdan Kaul Shāstrī, KSTS 31, 1921.

title: Bhagavadgītā
primary_author: anonymous/traditional (mūla ascribed to Vyāsa); Vīraśaiva bhāṣya-kāra unidentified
note: Two distinct strata: anonymous/Vyāsa-ascribed mūla + a separate, unidentified Vīraśaiva bhāṣya. Bhāṣya author left anonymous rather than guessing Śrīpati.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 6, with commentary Tātparyaprakāśa
current_author: attributed to Vālmīki (text); commentary Tātparyaprakāśa by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī
primary_author: anonymous (mūla = Kashmirian Mokṣopāya, pseudepigraphically ascribed to Vālmīki); Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī (Tātparyaprakāśa)
source: web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mokshopaya; secondary:cleaned_Mimamsa and Vedanta. Interaction and Continuity_Johannes Bronkhorst, ed., 2007.txt; secondary:cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue...British museum.txt
note: Two distinct authors: anonymous Mokṣopāya mūla (Vālmīki ascription pseudepigraphic) + ṭīkā by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī. Part 6 of the saṃpūrṇa.

title: Tantrāloka, chapters 1-14 (āhnikas)
source: secondary:cleaned_Davidson_Indian Esoteric Buddhism.txt; secondary:cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse.txt; secondary:cleaned_Pramāṇakīrtiḥ-Steinkellner 70th_v2.txt
note: Mūla securely Abhinavagupta; conflation_flag true because edition/item includes Jayaratha's viveka commentary (a distinct author).

title: Navasāhasāṅkacarita
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Images, Miracles, and Authority in Asian Religious Traditions_Richard H. Davis, ed., 1998.txt
note: Colophon: Padmagupta alias Parimala, son of Mṛgāṅkagupta. Single author; matches current.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 1, with the commentary Tātparyaprakāśa
current_author: attributed to Vālmīki (anonymous/composite); commentary by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī
primary_author: anonymous (mūla = Kashmirian Mokṣopāya, pseudepigraphically ascribed to Vālmīki); Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī (Tātparyaprakāśa)
source: web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mokshopaya; secondary:cleaned_Mimamsa and Vedanta. Interaction and Continuity_Johannes Bronkhorst, ed., 2007.txt
note: Vairāgyaprakaraṇa = Part 1. Two distinct authors: anonymous mūla (Vālmīki ascription pseudepigraphic) + ṭīkā by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 5, with commentary Tātparyaprakāśa
current_author: attributed to Vālmīki (text); commentary Tātparyaprakāśa by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī
primary_author: Vālmīki (traditional, pseudepigraphic — actually anonymous, orig. Mokṣopāya); commentary Tātparyaprakāśa by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī
note: Mūla pseudepigraphically ascribed to Vālmīki; genuinely anonymous (Mokṣopāya layer). Commentary Tātparyaprakāśa securely by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī — distinct author/role, hence conflation of text + commentary.

title: Abhinavagupta: Paramārthasāra
primary_author: Abhinavagupta (reworked kārikās, after Ādiśeṣa's older Paramārthasāra); Yogarāja (vivṛti)
note: Verses = Abhinavagupta's reworking of Ādiśeṣa's earlier Paramārthasāra; commentary (vivṛti) = Yogarāja. Three distinct contributors across roles → conflation; current_author lists only Abhinavagupta.

title: Stotraratna
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Indian Philosophy_Christopher Bartley, 2011.txt
note: Devotional hymn securely by Yāmuna (c. 11th c.), grandson of Nāthamuni. No commentary in this e-text → no conflation.

title: Saṃvitsiddhi
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Mimamsa and Vedanta. Interaction and Continuity_Johannes Bronkhorst, ed., 2007.txt; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Indian Philosophy_Christopher Bartley, 2011.txt
note: Third tract of Yāmuna's Siddhitrayī; securely Yāmuna. Text is fragmentary (Mesquita reconstruction) but authorship undisputed; single author.

title: Nyāyavārttikatātparyapariśuddhi: Adhyāya 1.2
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Philosophy in Classical India. The Proper Work of Reason_Jonardon Ganeri, 2001.txt
note: Udayana's sub-comm. on Vācaspati's Tātparyaṭīkā (chain: Nyāyasūtra → Vātsyāyana Bhāṣya → Uddyotakara Vārttika → Vācaspati Tātparyaṭīkā → Udayana Pariśuddhi). E-text is Udayana's text only → single author, not conflated.

title: Parātriśikāvivṛti (Parātrīśikāvivaraṇa)
primary_author: Abhinavagupta (vivaraṇa); root Parātriśikā = anonymous Tantric āgama
source: gretil-header N/A (muktabodha url); secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; web:tradition
note: Commentary (vivaraṇa) securely Abhinavagupta; root verses = anonymous Tantric scripture (Parātriśikā). Distinct author roles → conflation; current_author records only Abhinavagupta the commentator.

title: Śiva-Purāṇa 7 [Vāyavīya-Saṃhitā]
note: Traditional ascription to Vyāsa only; no historical author. Composite anonymous Purāṇic saṃhitā.

title: Saṃvitsiddhi
note: Single Viśiṣṭādvaita author; text survives in fragments, critically reconstructed by Mesquita. Part of the Siddhitraya.

title: Paramārthasāra
note: Distinct from the OLDER Paramārthasāra by Ādiśeṣa (a Vaiṣṇava Ādhāra-kārikā work) which Abhinavagupta recast/adapted; title is shared by two authors but this e-text is Abhinavagupta's recension. Yogarāja's Vivṛti is a separate commentary not in this file.

title: Bhairavastava
note: Colophon explicitly names Abhinavagupta as composer; minor stotra (nine verses).

title: Abhinavagupta: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvivṛtivimarśinī, Volume 1
note: Layered text: root kārikās by Utpaladeva, the sub-commentary (IPVV) by Abhinavagupta. The work itself (IPVV) is securely Abhinavagupta's; Utpaladeva listed as kārikā author for the underlying base. Not a conflation of a single title's authorship.

title: Mālinīślokavārttika: Kāṇḍa 2
note: Colophon names Abhinavagupta; the Mālinīślokavārttika (=Mālinīvijayavārttika) is his vārttika on the Mālinīvijayottaratantra. Single author.

title: Nyāyavārttikatātparyapariśuddhi: Adhyāya 1.1
note: Secure Nyāya attribution. The work is the topmost layer of the NS commentary tower (Vātsyāyana Bhāṣya > Uddyotakara Vārttika > Vācaspati Tātparyaṭīkā > Udayana Pariśuddhi) but the Pariśuddhi proper is single-authored by Udayana.

title: Abhinavagupta: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvivṛtivimarśinī, Volume 3
note: Current record lists only Abhinavagupta. The title-complex conflates Utpaladeva (kārikā + the 'Aṣṭasāhasrī' vivṛti, mostly lost) with Abhinavagupta (vivṛtivimarśinī). Distinct from Abhinavagupta's separate Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinī (on the kārikā alone).

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 4, with the commentary Tātparyaprakāśa
current_author: attributed to Vālmīki (anonymous/composite); commentary by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Root text: pseudo-Vālmīki, anonymous/composite (orig. Kashmirian Mokṣopāya). Commentary: Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī. Two distinct authorial layers — conflation flagged.

title: Bījanighaṇṭu
primary_author: anonymous (framed as teaching of Śiva, transmitted via Bhairava)
note: No historical author. Traditional/divine ascription to Śiva (via Bhairava) noted but not a real authorship claim; remains anonymous.

title: Mṛtyuvañcanopadeśa
note: Secure: author named in repeated colophons (viracita). Vāgīśvarakīrti = Vikramaśīla scholar, c. 11th c.

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha, Part 2, with the commentary Tātparyaprakāśa
current_author: attributed to Vālmīki (anonymous/composite); commentary by Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Root text pseudo-Vālmīki (anonymous/composite); commentary Ānandabodhendra Sarasvatī. Mumukṣuvyavahāra is the 2nd prakaraṇa. Two authorial layers — conflation flagged.

title: Parātrīśikāvivṛti (Parātrīśikā-laghuvṛtti)
primary_author: Abhinavagupta (laghuvṛtti/vivṛti); Parātrīśikā root verses anonymous (Trika tantra)
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt; secondary:cleaned_Understanding Mantras_Harvey P. Alper, 1989.txt
note: Title conflates: anonymous scriptural Parātrīśikā root verses (concluding portion of a Trika/Rudrayāmala tantra) vs Abhinavagupta's laghuvṛtti commentary. Only the vṛtti is his.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinī
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Kārikā by Utpaladeva; vimarśinī (the work itself) by Abhinavagupta, disciple-lineage of Utpaladeva via Lakṣmaṇagupta. Edition adds Bhāskara's Bhāskarī sub-commentary.

title: Abhinavagupta: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinī
source: gretil-header (parallel abhiprvu); secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Kārikā by Utpaladeva; commentary (vimarśinī = the named work) by Abhinavagupta. Muktabodha/KSTS no. 22 & 33.

title: Abhinavagupta: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvivṛtivimarśinī, Volume 2
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism ... Padoux 1992.txt; secondary:cleaned_skt-en-mono.txt
note: Three-layer text: Utpaladeva's kārikā, Utpaladeva's (now largely lost) vivṛti, and Abhinavagupta's vivṛtivimarśinī (the named work, KSTS 62). The work itself is Abhinavagupta's.

title: Mṛtyuvañcanopadeśatārāsādhana
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_YOSHIZAKI_Newar Buddhism.txt; secondary:cleaned_Emptiness that is Form ... Yarnall.txt
note: Single-author Buddhist death-cheating Tārā sādhana. Attribution to Vāgīśvarakīrti secure per critical edition.

title: Siddhitraya: Part 2: Īśvarasiddhi
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Mimamsa and Vedanta ... Bronkhorst 2007.txt; secondary:cleaned_skt-en-mono.txt
note: Root Īśvarasiddhi by Yāmuna (secure); the three siddhis survive only as fragments. Edition/file also carries Kṛṣṇamācārya's Siddhāñjana commentary — distinct author, not conflated into the root attribution.

title: Ṣaṭtriṃśattattvasandoha
current_author: Amṛtānanda(nātha) (attributed; sometimes given as Rājānaka Ananda)
source: secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Mūla (Trika/Kashmir Śaiva 36-tattva compendium) by Amṛtānandanātha; vivaraṇa by Rājānaka Ānanda — two distinct persons. Edition (KSTS, ed. Mukunda Rāma Shāstrī) prints both.

title: Sāratamā [āryāṣṭasāhasrikāyāḥ prajñāpāramitāyāḥ sāratamākhyā pañjikā]
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci's Collection. Part I_Francesco Sferra.txt; secondary:cleaned_Drewes_Oral_Texts_in_Indian_Mahayana.txt
note: Single-author pañjikā commenting on the Aṣṭasāhasrikā (root sūtra is anonymous Mahāyāna scripture, not co-author of this work). Variant titles Sāratarā/Sārottamā noted by Drewes.

title: Chandoratnākara
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanskrit Texts from Giuseppe Tucci's Collection. Part I_Francesco Sferra.txt; secondary:cleaned_Epperson_Kālidāsa in Tibet_Messenger Poetry in Translation.txt
note: Metrics treatise with svopajña (auto-)commentary; verses + vṛtti both Ratnākaraśānti's.

title: Vṛttamālāstuti
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Epperson_Kālidāsa in Tibet_Messenger Poetry in Translation.txt
note: Colophon names Jñānaśrīmitra. The e-text is the stuti (verses) only; Śākyarakṣita's Vṛttamālāvivṛti commentary is separate and not conflated here.

title: Hetubinduṭīkāloka
primary_author: Durveka Miśra (Āloka); Bhaṭṭa Arcaṭa (Hetubinduṭīkā); Dharmakīrti (Hetubindu mūla)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Indian Buddhism. A Survey With Bibliographical Notes_Hajime Nakamura, 1980.txt; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy...Tarkabhasa of Moksakaragupta_Kajiyama, 1966.txt
note: Title is the Āloka by Durveka Miśra, but the e-text/edition embeds three authors' strata: Dharmakīrti's Hetubindu (mūla), Arcaṭa's ṭīkā, Durveka's Āloka sub-commentary. current_author captures only Durveka.

title: Vṛttamālāstuti
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Epperson_Kālidāsa in Tibet_Messenger Poetry in Translation.txt
note: Duplicate work-item of the Vṛttamālāstuti (same file). Single author Jñānaśrīmitra; Śākyarakṣita's separate vivṛti not in this e-text.

title: Daśākuśalakarmapathadeśanā
note: Composed by Atiśa; colophon also names monk Jayaśīla as transmitter/editor (anūditaḥ sampāditaśca), a redactional role, not a co-author.

title: Śaraṇagamanadeśanā
note: Secure single-author work of Atiśa; no commentary layer.

title: Garbhasaṅgraha
source: secondary:cleaned_Atisa Dipamkara  Illuminator of the Awakened Mind_James B  Apple, 2019.txt
note: current_author records a traditional Nāgārjuna ascription, but the edition's colophon and modern scholarship (Negi 1992; Apple 2019) assign it to Atiśa/Dīpaṅkaraśrījñāna. Attribution is therefore disputed; best display author = Atiśa.

title: Kubjikātantra
note: Anonymous āgamic/tantric revelation; traditionally ascribed to Śiva. No individual author; sources do not single out a redactor by name.

title: Bodhisattvādikarmikamārgāvatāradeśanā
note: Authored by Atiśa; colophon adds Jayaśīla as transmitter/editor (anūditā), a redactional role only.

title: Suprabhedāgama
note: Anonymous revealed āgama; revelation ascribed to Śiva. No human author; composite/redacted over time but no named layers in sources.

title: Vimalaprabhāṭīkā on Śrī-Laghukālacakratantra (Vol. 2)
note: Two distinct (legendary) authors conflated under one volume: mūla tantra by Mañjuśrīyaśas, ṭīkā (Vimalaprabhā) by Kalki Śrīpuṇḍarīka. Ascriptions are Shambhala-lineage/pseudepigraphic; actual composition Indian, c. 11th c.

title: Sūkṣmāgama
note: Revealed Śaivasiddhānta āgama; traditionally Śiva's teaching, no human author. Sambandhaśivācārya/Ganesan are modern editors, not authors. Remains anonymous.

title: Tejobindu Upaniṣad
source: secondary:cleaned_White_Yoga in Practice.txt | secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Mūla Upaniṣad anonymous; commentary by Upaniṣad-Brahmayogin (distinct, datable person). Edition conflates anonymous root with named commentator, but root text stays anonymous.

title: Ṣaṭsāhasrikā Hevajraṭīkā
note: Commentary (ṭīkā) on the Hevajratantra, not the tantra itself. Ascribed to Vajragarbha (the tantra's own interlocutor) — traditional ascription; editor flags doubt over later-chapter authorship.

title: Laghutantraṭīkā
note: Commentary on Cakrasaṃvaratantra ch.1 by Vajrapāṇi (a human author using the bodhisattva name/persona). Not a root tantra. Single author.

title: Vimalaprabhāṭīkā on Śrī-Laghukālacakratantra (Vol. 3)
note: Conflates two (legendary) authors: mūla tantra by Mañjuśrīyaśas, ṭīkā (Vimalaprabhā) by Kalki Śrīpuṇḍarīka. Pseudepigraphic Shambhala ascriptions; vol. 3 of same edition as the vol. 2 item.

title: Sekoddeśaṭīkā (Paramārthasaṃgraha)
primary_author: Nāḍapāda / Nāropa (ṭīkā); root Sekoddeśa = anonymous Kālacakra tantra (traditionally Buddha-spoken)
source: secondary:cleaned_Newman_Brief History Kalachakra.txt; secondary:cleaned_Buddhist Thought_Williams Tribe Wynne 2000.txt; edition (Carelli 1941)
note: Two layers: root Sekoddeśa (anonymous tantric root, traditionally Bhagavān/Sucandra) commented on by Nāropa in the ṭīkā (Paramārthasaṃgraha). current_author empty; set Nāropa as ṭīkā author and flag conflation with anonymous root.

title: Spandasandoha
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion_Bhattacharyya 2006.txt; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies Vol.1 - Potter.txt
note: Secure: Kṣemarāja (pupil of Abhinavagupta), early 11th c. Pratyabhijñā/Spanda lineage. Single author.

title: Kṣaṇabhaṅgasiddhi
note: Colophon-attested to Ratnakīrti (11th c., Vikramaśīla, pupil of Jñānaśrīmitra). Single author, no commentary layer in this file.

title: Apohasiddhi
note: Colophon-attested to Ratnakīrti (11th c.). Scribe Trailokyadata is copyist, not author. Single author.

title: Mṛtyuvañcanasitatārāsādhana
current_author: anonymous/traditional (within the circle of Vāgīśvarakīrti's Mṛtyuvañcana cycle)
primary_author: Vāgīśvarakīrti (source teaching); sādhana itself anonymous/redactional within his Mṛtyuvañcana cycle
note: Text self-references Vāgīśvarakīrti as the source authority; sādhana (SM 103) is a derivative ritual text within his Mṛtyuvañcana cycle. Promotes from 'anonymous/traditional' to traditional ascription to Vāgīśvarakīrti (medium confidence — sādhana may be redactional rather than autograph).

title: Sekoddeśa [Sekoddeśaṭīkā]
primary_author: anonymous (root Sekoddeśa, Kālacakra root tantra, traditionally Buddha-spoken); Nāropa only for the ṭīkā
note: Title conflates the anonymous root Sekoddeśa with Nāropa's ṭīkā. current_author 'Nāropa' is wrong for the root verses present in this file (which are the Kālacakra root section). Flag conflation; root = anonymous, ṭīkā = Nāropa.

title: Yaśodharācarita
note: Modern named author; not the medieval Yaśodhara legend versions (Vādirāja Sūri, Puṣpadanta's Jasaharacariu) mentioned in Winternitz.

title: Caryāsaṅgrahapradīpa
primary_author: Dīpaṅkaraśrījñāna (Atiśa) per colophon; attributed to (tantric) Āryadeva in Tibetan tradition
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Ontology of Consciousness. Percipient Action_Helmut Wautischer, ed., 2008.txt
note: Sanskrit text per colophon is Atiśa's; but the title is widely cited as Āryadeva's (tantric Āryadeva of the Guhyasamāja Ārya school). Same title, competing authorial ascriptions; not a single secure attribution.

title: Bodhavilāsa
primary_author: Attributed to Kṣemarāja in transmitted colophon; scholarship reassigns to Vāmanadatta (= Dvayasampatti)
note: Title transmitted under Kṣemarāja's name (Pensa's ed.) but scholarly identification with the Dvayasampatti points to Vāmanadatta; attribution to Kṣemarāja is doubtful.

title: Kṣaṇabhaṅgasiddhi vyatirekātmikā
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Introduction to Buddhist Philosophy...Tarkabhasa of Moksakaragupta_Kajiyama 1966.txt
note: Author secure (Ratnakīrti, 11th c.); the work is a digest based on Jñānaśrīmitra's Kṣaṇabhaṅgādhyāya, but authorship of this redaction is Ratnakīrti's own.

title: Bhairavānukaraṇastotra
note: Stotra securely ascribed to the Kashmir Śaiva Kṣemarāja (pupil of Abhinavagupta), per colophon and editor.

title: Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism...Padoux_Goudriaan 1992.txt
note: Single author Kṣemarāja for both sūtras and auto-commentary; a summary of Pratyabhijñā doctrine drawing on Abhinavagupta, but not a multi-author conflation.

title: Sūtrārthasamuccayopadeśa
note: Colophon names Atiśa as composer; Jayaśīla as scholarly reviser/finalizer (saṃśodhaka), a redactional role only.

title: Pratyabhijñāhṛdaya (text version)
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism. Studies in Honor of André Padoux_Teun Goudriaan, ed, 1992.txt
note: Single author; the work is a summary of Pratyabhijñā doctrine by Kṣemarāja with his own auto-vṛtti (not transmitted in this text-only version).

title: Spandanirṇaya
primary_author: Kṣemarāja (Spandanirṇaya commentary); Vasugupta or Kallaṭa (Spandakārikā root verses, disputed)
source: secondary:cleaned_Sanderson_Śaivism and the tantric traditions.txt; gretil-header
note: Two distinct authorial layers: root Spandakārikā (Vasugupta vs Kallaṭa, disputed) + Spandanirṇaya commentary (Kṣemarāja). Title/current_author conflate the commentator with the unnamed kārikā author.

title: Śivasūtravimarśinī (text version) [commentary on the Śivasūtra revealed to Vasugupta]
current_author: Śivasūtra revealed to/by Vasugupta; Vimarśinī commentary by Kṣemarāja
primary_author: Kṣemarāja (Vimarśinī commentary); Śivasūtra traditionally revealed (ascribed to Śiva, 'discovered' by Vasugupta)
note: Śivasūtra is traditionally revealed (counted as Āgama, ascribed to Śiva via Vasugupta) — not a securely human-authored root; Vimarśinī commentary securely by Kṣemarāja. Root listed as anonymous with traditional ascription noted.

title: Bodhipathapradīpa
note: Single author Atiśa; secure. Sanskrit original lost, reconstructed/preserved via Tibetan (Eimer ed.).

title: Satyadvayāvatāranāma
note: Atiśa is composer (viracita); Vīryasiṃha is translator/reviser (anūdya saṃśodhya nirṇītaḥ), not co-author. Sanskrit largely lost.

title: parāprāveśikā
note: Single-author Trika/Pratyabhijñā prose primer; securely Kṣemarāja, matches current_author.

title: Stūpalakṣaṇakārikāvivecana
note: current_author empty -> Bhadravyūha per colophon. Roth = modern editor, not author. Title pairs verses (kārikā) and analysis (vivecana) but both ascribed to one author.

title: Aṣṭāvakragītā
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally ascribed to the sage Aṣṭāvakra)
note: Legendary/traditional ascription to Aṣṭāvakra is to the dialogue character, not a historical author; genuinely anonymous. Matches current_author intent.

title: Garbhasaṃgraha
source: secondary:cleaned_Atisa Dipamkara  Illuminator of the Awakened Mind_James B  Apple, 2019.txt
note: Traditional ascription to Atiśa upheld; flag homonymous Kāṇha Garbhasaṃgraha as a separate work to avoid misidentification. Survives only in Tibetan translation.

title: Vimalaprabhāṭīkā on the Laghukālacakratantra (Upadhyaya ed. 1986)
primary_author: Kalki Śrī Puṇḍarīka (Vimalaprabhā ṭīkā); Mañjuśrīyaśas (root Laghukālacakratantra)
note: Multi-author title: root tantra (Mañjuśrīyaśas) + ṭīkā (Puṇḍarīka) by different persons in the Kalkin lineage. current_author empty -> separated roles.

title: Cittotpādasaṃvaravidhikrama
note: Colophon names Atiśa as author plus two reviewers (redactors); matches current_author. Reviewers recorded as secondary roles, not a conflation of distinct authors.

title: Gurukriyākrama
source: secondary:cleaned_Introduction to the Buddhist Tantric Systems_Lessing & Wayman, 1968.txt; secondary:cleaned_Vetturini_Kadam School of TBism_vol2.txt
note: Toh. 3977; short ritual work, single author Atiśa. Confirmed by Tibetan colophon ascription 'jo bos mdzad pa'.

title: Bhojadeva: Aṣṭaprakaraṇa — Tattvaprakāśa
current_author: Bhojadeva (King Bhoja of Dhārā, Paramāra dynasty)
source: secondary:cleaned_Ritual and Speculation in Early Tantrism..._Goudriaan, 1992.txt; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; secondary:cleaned_gavin-flood.txt
note: Root mūla (kārikā) text by Bhoja. The edition's commentaries (Tātparyadīpikā=Śrīkumāra, Vṛtti=Aghoraśiva) are separate works; the title here denotes the root, so no conflation for this item. Root authorship secure.

title: Vajrayoginyāḥ stutipraṇidhānam
note: = Trikāyavajrayoginīstutipraṇidhāna / -stotra (GSS27). Part of anonymous GSS collection; remains anonymous. No traditional single ascription confirmed.

title: Marmakālikā
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; web:WebSearch Tattvajnanasamsiddhi/Viryasrimitra
note: Title 'Marmakālikā' = the Marmakalikāpañjikā commentary by Vīryaśrīmitra; conflates root (mūla) Tattvajñānasaṃsiddhi of Śūnyasamādhipāda with the pañjikā of Vīryaśrīmitra. Was 'anonymous'; commentator now identified.

title: Vimalaratnalekha

title: Subhāṣitaratnakoṣa
note: Duplicate work-record, corrected/revised e-text version of the Subhāṣitaratnakoṣa. Same compiler. Per-verse poets cited internally.

title: Advayavajrasaṃgraha
note: Title field was empty; supplied 'Advayavajrasaṃgraha'. A single-author NAMED corpus (Advayavajra's collected short works), not a conflation of distinct authors; however attribution of some individual pieces within the 21 is uncertain (medium confidence). Advayavajra ≡ Maitrīpa ≡ Maitreyanātha (siddha).

title: Laghustava

title: Ṣaṭsāhasrikā Hevajrapiṇḍārthaṭīkā (Hevajratantrapiṇḍārthaṭīkā), ch. 10 (Tattvapaṭala)

Author Vajragarbha is the traditional commentator but his identity is flagged as uncertain in Kongtrul. The base tantra and its commentator are distinct, but this work-record is the COMMENTARY only -> single attributed role, no conflation.

title: Revākhaṇḍa of the Vāyupurāṇa

title: Tattvaratnāvalī

Maitrīpa), NOT the Yogācāra Maitreya(nātha) of the Five Treatises; the current_author string risks conflating these homonyms — they are one and the same person here, so no conflation flag, but the 'Maitreyanātha' label is potentially misleading.

title: Phetkāriṇītantra
note: Anonymous revealed Tantra; quoted by later digests so pre-dates them. No reliable individual authorship; traditional ascription only as divine/āgamic revelation.

note: Colophon-confirmed single author; a Buddhist Nyāya/logic primer (sopāna).

title: Nibandhāvalī
note: Nibandhāvalī = 'collection of essays', all by the one author Ratnakīrti — an anthology of his works, not multiple authors, so no conflation.

title: Gītagovinda

title: Kṣaṇabhaṅgasiddhi Vyatirekātmikā

title: Sumāgadhāvadāna

title: Gītagovinda-kāvya

title: Yoginīhṛdaya
primary_author: anonymous (root tantra); Dīpikā by Amṛtānanda; Setubandha by Bhāskararāya
note: Root tantra genuinely anonymous (traditionally framed as Bhairava's revelation, part of/sequel to Vāmakeśvarīmata). Edition conflates mūla + Amṛtānanda's Dīpikā + Bhāskararāya's Setubandha — distinct authors/roles.

title: Śaivāgamādimāhātmya (Śivāgamādimāhātmyasaṃgraha)
note: Securely authored/compiled by Jñānaprakāśa of Śālivāṭi (Jaffna). Strictly a saṃgraha (anthology of cited scripture) with his own commentary, so role is compiler+commentator.

title: Paraśurāmakalpasūtra
current_author: traditionally ascribed to Paraśurāma (compiled by Sumedha); historically anonymous
primary_author: anonymous (traditionally ascribed to Paraśurāma; compilation attributed to Sumedha); commentary Saubhāgyodaya by Rāmeśvara
note: Mūla pseudepigraphically ascribed to Paraśurāma / compiled by Sumedha (both legendary); historically anonymous. Edition conflates anonymous sūtra-mūla with Rāmeśvara's Saubhāgyodaya commentary — distinct authors.

title: Caryāgītikoṣa (Caryāgītikoṣavṛtti)
primary_author: Songs (caryās) by various Buddhist siddhas (Saraha, Kṛṣṇacārya/Kāṇha, Lui, etc.); commentary (vṛtti) by Munidatta
note: Gold-standard conflation: a single title bundles songs by many distinct mahāsiddhas (each caryā ascribed individually in the MS) PLUS a separate Sanskrit commentary (vṛtti) by Munidatta. Roots vs commentary by different authors; root songs themselves multi-author. Item had blank current_author.

title: Dvāviṃśatyavadānakathā
note: Genuinely anonymous late/medieval Nepalese avadāna compilation (22 stories on dāna). No author or traditional ascription; modern ed. by Mamiko Okada. Compiler unnamed.

title: Kathāsaritsāgara
note: Single secure author; the Durgāprasād-Parab 1915 edition title itself names Somadevabhaṭṭa. Based ultimately on the (lost) Bṛhatkathā tradition but Somadeva is the composer of this recension.

title: Carcāstava
current_author: anonymous/traditional (one of the five hymns of the Pañcastavī)
primary_author: anonymous (Kashmirian Śākta hymn; one of the five stavas of the Pañcastavī)

title: Yogavāsiṣṭha
current_author: anonymous/traditional (ascribed to Vālmīki; core text = Mokṣopāya, of unknown Kashmiri authorship)
primary_author: anonymous Kashmirian author (Mokṣopāya root, pseudo-Vālmīki); Bhāskarakaṇṭha (Mokṣopāya-Ṭīkā)

title: Kaulopaniṣad

title: Kāvyaprakāśa

title: Kāvyaprakāśa

note: Bindu is itself a ṭīkā on Śaṅkara's Daśaślokī, but the GRETIL file is the Madhusūdana bindu-text alone; not conflated in this edition. Distinct sub-commentators (Brahmānanda, Nārāyaṇa Tīrtha) belong to separate works.

title: Gopālacampū: Pūrvacampū, 12-22
note: Pūrvacampū, cantos 12-22; single-author, no bundled commentary.

title: Śivārcanacandrikā
note: Śaiva pūjā/ritual paddhati by the 16th c. polymath Appayya Dīkṣita; secure, no dispute.

title: Nāṭakacandrikā
note: Dramaturgical treatise by the Gauḍīya theorist Rūpa Gosvāmin; colophon attribution unambiguous.

title: Vairāgyaśataka
note: Title is shared with Bhartṛhari's celebrated Vairāgyaśataka, but this is Appayya Dīkṣita's own distinct devotional/vairāgya stotra; attribution to Appayya is explicit in the edition's colophon. No conflation.

title: Yoginītantra
primary_author: anonymous (Śiva–Pārvatī dialogue; no human author named)
note: Anonymous Śākta scripture of Kāmarūpa (Assam); traditionally framed as Śiva's teaching, no historical author. Dated ~16th/17th c. Distinct from Buddhist yoginītantra class.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 5. Bhaktisandarbha
primary_author: Jīva Gosvāmī (author/expander), based on preliminary notes of Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt | web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sat_Sandarbhas
note: Securely Jīva's work but rests on Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī's antecedent notes (Jīva states this himself); two roles -> conflation_flag true (Gopāla Bhaṭṭa source/compiler, Jīva author-redactor).

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 6. Prītisandarbha
primary_author: Jīva Gosvāmī (author/expander), based on preliminary notes of Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī
source: gretil-header | secondary:cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt | web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sat_Sandarbhas
note: Final/sixth Sandarbha. Jīva is author-redactor; built on Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī's antecedent notes. Two roles -> conflation_flag true.

title: Rādhākṛṣṇārcanadīpikā
note: Independent ritual manual self-attributed by Jīva in the colophon; not part of the Sandarbha/Gopāla Bhaṭṭa complex, so no conflation. The accompanying Brahmasaṃhitā-ṭīkā is also Jīva's.

title: Śaktisaṃgamatantra, Kālīkhaṇḍa
primary_author: anonymous (Śākta compendium; Kālīkhaṇḍa is khaṇḍa 1 of 4)
source: web:https://www.wisdomlib.org/definition/shaktisangamatantra | secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Anonymous revealed Śākta tantra; Kālīkhaṇḍa is one (the first) of four khaṇḍas of a large composite compendium (likely late, c. 16th–18th c.). No named author. Composite of khaṇḍas but all anonymous, so no author-conflation flagged.

title: Vallālacarita
note: Single author Ānanda Bhaṭṭa (c. 1510 CE). Note: value as a historical source on the 12th-c. Sena king Vallālasena is debated, but the attribution to Ānandabhaṭṭa is secure; not pseudepigraphic.

title: Śaktisaṃgamatantra: Tārākhaṇḍa
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion An Historical, Ritualistic, and Philosophical Study_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt
note: Anonymous Tantric digest, 16th-17th c.; Tārākhaṇḍa = book 2 of 4. No author named; remains anonymous/traditional.

title: Bhāvaprakāśa
note: Standard Ayurvedic nighaṇṭu/compendium securely by Bhāvamiśra, 16th c. Not to be confused with Śāradātanaya's Bhāvaprakāśana.

title: Ṣaṭcakranirṇaya (Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa)
note: Resolves the conditional in current_author: securely by Pūrṇānanda; the Ṣaṭcakranirūpaṇa = ch. 6 of his Śrītattvacintāmaṇi (16th c.).

title: Rasaprakāśasudhākara
note: Securely Yaśodhara (Bhaṭṭa); rasaśāstra work on mercury/metals.

title: Kulavṛtti
source: secondary:cleaned_Origins of Yoga and Tantra. Indic Religions to the Thirteenth Century_Geoffrey Samuel, 2008 (2010).txt
note: Root vs commentary distinction: Kulacūḍāmaṇitantra (anonymous, 9th-11th c.) carries the Kulavṛtti commentary by Pūrṇānanda(giri). current_author names only the commentator; flagged conflation for the two layers.

title: Dīkṣādarśa
note: Śaivasiddhānta dīkṣā manual; securely by Vedajñānaguru II, latter half 16th c. (Sanderson).

title: Vīramitrodaya: Samayaprakāśa
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion_N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt; secondary:cleaned_Education In Ancient India_Scharfe 2002.txt
note: Colophon names Mitramiśra; Vīramitrodaya is a large dharmanibandha, Samayaprakāśa = the kāṇḍa on calendrical/ritual time. Single author, secure.

title: Yogasārasaṃgraha
source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt; web: edition Ganganatha Jha 1894
note: A short prose epitome of Yoga doctrine by Vijñānabhikṣu; secure single authorship.

title: Śaivasiddhāntaparibhāṣāmañjarī

note: Proper title is Śaivāgama-paribhāṣāmañjarī (workitem 'Śaivasiddhānta-' is a variant). Author = Vedajñānaguru II / Nigamajñāna II of Cidambaram, 16th c. Secure.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 1. Tattvasandarbha
primary_author: Jīva Gosvāmī (redactor/author); based on Gopāla Bhaṭṭa's original notes

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 4. Kṛṣṇasandarbha
primary_author: Jīva Gosvāmī (redactor/author); based on Gopāla Bhaṭṭa's original notes
source: web:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sat_Sandarbhas; secondary:cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse.txt; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Same conflation pattern as Tattvasandarbha: Gopāla Bhaṭṭa's foundational notes vs Jīva Gosvāmī's authorial redaction/composition. Jīva is the substantive author of this volume.

title: Tattvacintāmaṇi (Śrītattvacintāmaṇi), chapters 16 to end
