title: Tantrarājatantra
primary_author: anonymous (revealed Āgama; Manoramā commentary by Subhagānandanātha)
note: Root tantra anonymous (traditionally a divinely-revealed Āgama); named human author belongs to the Manoramā commentary, not the work item (root text).

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 2. Bhāgavatasandarbha
primary_author: Jīva Gosvāmī (author/redactor of final form, based on preliminary notes of Gopāla Bhaṭṭa)
source: secondary:cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Single title conflates two persons: Gopāla Bhaṭṭa (original/preliminary compilation) and Jīva Gosvāmī (final author-redactor). Jīva is the dominant author but the work explicitly builds on Gopāla Bhaṭṭa's material.

title: Ṣaṭsandarbha: 3. Paramātmasandarbha
primary_author: Jīva Gosvāmī (author/redactor of final form, based on preliminary notes of Gopāla Bhaṭṭa)
source: secondary:cleaned_Freschi and Maas_Adaptive Reuse_Aspects of Creativity in South Asian Cultural History.txt
note: Conflation as for part 2: Gopāla Bhaṭṭa (preliminary compilation) + Jīva Gosvāmī (final author-redactor). A commentarial tradition (Gopālatoṣaṇī etc.) is later and not part of this work item.

title: Tattvacintāmaṇi (Śrītattvacintāmaṇi), chapters 11-15
note: Secure. The editor's modern commentary/notes (Cintāmaṇi Bhaṭṭācārya, Bhuvanmohan Sāṅkhyatīrtha) are editorial apparatus, not co-authorship of the root text. Not the homonymous Nyāya work by Gaṅgeśa.

title: Sāṅkhyasāra
note: Secure single authorship; colophon repeatedly names Vijñānabhikṣu. Two-part structure (pūrva-/uttara-bhāga) is internal, not multi-author.

title: Gopālacampū: Uttaracampū, 5-6
note: Secure. Single-author campū kāvya by Jīva Gosvāmin (same author as the Sandarbhas, but here without the Gopāla Bhaṭṭa preliminary-notes layer, so no conflation).

title: Gopālacampū: Uttaracampū, 1-3
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Tantra. 5 volumes_Sadhu Santideva, 1999.txt
note: 16th-c. Gauḍīya. Single-author prose-verse campū; no commentary layer in this file.

title: Gopālacampū: Uttaracampū, 29
note: Chapter 29 (bhāvi-kathā-pramāṇa-prathanam) of Jīva's Uttaracampū; single author.

title: Saṅkalpakalpadruma
note: Title is shared by two distinct Gauḍīya authors (Jīva Gosvāmin and Viśvanātha Cakravartī); not a single conflated work but a homonym. This file follows the Jīva attribution; alternate Viśvanātha attribution noted.

title: Tarkasaṃgraha
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Philosophy in Classical India. The Proper Work of Reason_Jonardon Ganeri, 2001.txt
note: Root text only in this file; ca. 17th c. Nyāya-Vaiśeṣika primer. No commentary conflated here.

title: Tarkasaṃgraha
primary_author: Annambhaṭṭa (mūla + Dīpikā); Nīlakaṇṭha (Dīpikāprakāśikā); N.S. Rāmānuja Tātācārya (Bālapriyā); Kṛṣṇa Tātācārya (Prasāraṇā)
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt

title: Liṅgadhāraṇacandrikā

title: Jñānaprakāśācārya: Pauṣkaravṛtti
note: vṛtti is by Jñānaprakāśa; the commented Pauṣkarāgama scripture itself is anonymous/revealed and not counted here since the item is the commentary. Crit. ed. Ganesan (IFP).

title: Gūḍhārthadīpikā
note: Confirms Kuśalamiśra as commentary author (resolves 'uncertain' hint). Distinct base kāvya traditionally ascribed to Kālidāsa; scribe Rājānāndarāma named in colophon.

title: Rādhākṛṣṇāṣṭakālīyalīlāsmaraṇakramapaddhati

title: Laghusiddhāntakaumudī [Laghukaumudī]
note: Varadarāja the abridger; source work by his teacher Bhaṭṭoji Dīkṣita (listed as source-author, not a conflation).

title: Sādhanādīpikā
note: Genuine attribution dispute: GRETIL colophon and most scholarship support Rādhākṛṣṇadāsa Gosvāmī; some Vraja editions ascribe it to Nārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭa. Not a multi-author conflation, but rival single-author ascriptions.

title: tārārahasyavṛtti 2

title: Sudhālaharī
note: Colophon explicitly names Paṇḍitarāja Jagannātha as composer; matches current attribution.

title: Kaliviḍambana

source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt

title: Rasagaṅgādhara
source: gretil-header; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 5 - Potter 1990.txt; secondary:cleaned_Bendall_Catalogue...txt

title: Gheraṇḍa-Saṃhitā

title: Devīnāmavilāsa
source: secondary:cleaned_Tantric Tradition_Agehananda Bharati, 1965.txt; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt
note: Attribution to Sāhib Kaul confirmed by secondary literature (also editor Madhusudan Kaul Shastri, KSTS 63). Bharati flags that 'Sāhib Kaul' may be a high tantric title rather than strictly a proper name, but it is the consistently used author designation.

title: Muktivāda
source: secondary:cleaned_skt-en-mono.txt; secondary:cleaned_Encyclopedia of Indian Philosophies, Vol. 1 - Potter.txt

title: Śrīvidyārṇavatantra
note: Author name 'Vidyāraṇya' is retained but the WHICH-Vidyāraṇya identification is disputed: likely NOT the celebrated Śṛṅgerī Advaitin commonly assumed, but a distinct (probably northern) tantric Vidyāraṇya Yati. Status downgraded from implied-secure to disputed; flagged as changed for this nuance.

title: Merutantra
primary_author: anonymous (late Tantra, no historical author named)
source: secondary:cleaned_History of the Tantric Religion ... N. N. Bhattacharyya 2006.txt

title: Mokṣopāyaṭīkā

title: Jānakī Pariṇaya
note: Title is the nāṭaka by Rāmabhadra Dīkṣita; distinct from the Jānakīpariṇaya campū of Cakrakavi (separate work, not a co-authored conflation). NB: the GK19 source_url is a generic GRETIL index and the snippet content corresponds to Yogavāsiṣṭha verses, not the nāṭaka — attribution here follows the item title (Rāmabhadra Dīkṣita's nāṭaka).

title: Narasiṃhaṭhakkura: Tārābhaktisudhārṇava
note: Single author Nṛsiṃha/Narasiṃha Ṭhakkura, c. 1688. Editors (Pañcānana Tarkaratna under Arthur Avalon/Woodroffe) are modern, not authors. Confirms current attribution.

title: Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinīvyākhyā

source: secondary:cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Resolves the 'uncertain' status: the vyākhyā is the Bhāskarī of Bhāskarakaṇṭha (early 18th c. Kashmir). The base text it comments on (Īśvarapratyabhijñāvimarśinī) is by Abhinavagupta, which itself comments on Utpaladeva's kārikās — but this item is specifically the sub-commentary (Bhāskarī), authored by Bhāskarakaṇṭha alone.

title: Tantracintāmaṇi
note: Compendium (40 prakāśas) of Tantra excerpts compiled by Navamīsiṃha/Navasiṃha alias Ādyānanda, minister of Bhūpālendra Malla of Kathmandu (c. 1689-1694). Confirms current attribution.

title: Kulamuktikallolinī
note: Navamīsiṃha and Ādyānanda are the same author (proper name vs initiatory name); not a conflation. Floruit late 17th c. Nepal/Kathmandu.

title: Paribhāṣenduśekhara
source: gretil-header + secondary:cleaned_Tradition and Argument in Classical Indian Linguistics ... Bronkhorst 2003.txt
note: Author of the mūla secure. Kielhorn/ Kāśī editions bundle commentaries by other hands, but this GRETIL e-text is the Nāgeśa mūla only.

title: Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇavatantra with the commentary called Setubandha
current_author: Commentary (Setubandha) by Bhāskararāya Makhin; mūla tantra anonymous/traditional
primary_author: Bhāskararāya Makhin (Setubandha commentary); mūla Nityāṣoḍaśikārṇava anonymous (part of Vāmakeśvaratantra)
note: Two distinct authorial layers: anonymous root tantra (Vāmakeśvara) vs Bhāskararāya's Setubandha ṭīkā. Current_author already separates roles; conflation flagged because one title combines them.

title: Bhāskararāya: Mahāyāgakrama
note: Muktabodha source_url 404; attribution rests on title and well-attested Bhāskararāya corpus. Initiatory name Bhāsuranandanātha.

title: Varivasyārahasya
source: secondary:cleaned_skt-en-mono.txt + secondary:cleaned_Traverses on Less Trodden Path ... Yajneshwara Shastri.txt + edition
note: Both the verses (varivasyārahasya) and the auto-commentary Prakāśa are by Bhāskararāya; single author, two roles (kārikā + auto-vṛtti). Not a multi-author conflation.

title: Gurustutiratnāvalī
note: Muktabodha source_url 404; attribution confirmed by recent scholarship (Brill IIJ 2021). 18th-c. Kashmiri Śaiva/Śākta author.

title: Bodhasāra
primary_author: Narahari (mūla); Divākara (commentary Dīpti, not in e-text)
source: gretil-header; secondary:reference-literature/cleaned_Literature about the Philosophical Systems and Indian Philosophy.txt
note: Root Vedānta treatise by Narahari (secure). The printed edition pairs it with the pupil Divākara's commentary (Dīpti/Arthadīpa) — distinct author; the GRETIL e-text contains only the mūla, no commentary.

title: Mahānirvāṇa Tantra
primary_author: anonymous (tantra); Hariharānanda Bhāratī (commentary, in printed ed.)
note: Tantra is anonymous (no historical author; traditionally revelation). Edition conflates the anonymous mūla with Hariharānanda Bhāratī's commentary — distinct, datable person; possible 18th-c. redaction by the commentarial milieu.

title: Puraścaryārṇava, volume 1
current_author: Pratāpasiṃha Śāha Deva (Pratap Singh Shah, King of Nepal)
primary_author: Pratāpasiṃha Śāhadeva (royal author/patron of the digest)
note: Royal-author/patron attribution (viracita = composed by King Pratāpasiṃha). Genre is a tantric digest, so much material is compiled from earlier tantras; authorship is single-named and traditional rather than a multi-author conflation.

title: Puraścaryārṇava, volume 3
current_author: Pratāpasiṃha Śāha Deva (Pratap Singh Shah, King of Nepal)
primary_author: Pratāpasiṃha Śāhadeva (royal author/patron of the digest)
note: Volume 3 of the same digest; same single traditional royal attribution. Compendium genre (quotes many sources) but conventionally a one-author work.

title: Puraścaryārṇava, volume 2
current_author: Pratāpasiṃha Śāha Deva (Pratap Singh Shah, King of Nepal)
primary_author: Pratāpasiṃha Śāhadeva (royal author/patron of the digest)
note: Volume 2 of the same digest; same single traditional royal attribution.

title: Prāṇatoṣiṇī (Kāmyakāṇḍa)
note: Tantric digest (nibandha) securely compiled by Rāmatoṣaṇa; it quotes many tantras but the compiler is a single named person (self-attested), so not an author-conflation.

title: Rāmatoṣaṇa (compiler): Prāṇatoṣiṇī, part 6
note: Compilation/digest of many tantras; named person is the compiler/redactor, not author of quoted source tantras. As a nibandha its individual quotations are by various (mostly anonymous) tantric authors, but the work itself is securely Rāmatoṣaṇa's compilation; not treated as multi-author conflation since it is explicitly a digest.

title: Prāṇatoṣiṇī (Dharmakāṇḍa)
note: One kāṇḍa of the Prāṇatoṣiṇī digest; quoted tantra material is by various original authors but the work is securely Rāmatoṣaṇa's compilation.

title: Prāṇatoṣiṇī (Arthakāṇḍa)
note: One kāṇḍa of the Prāṇatoṣiṇī digest; compiled tantra quotations by various original authors, work securely Rāmatoṣaṇa's compilation.

title: Rāmatoṣaṇa (compiler): Prāṇatoṣiṇī, Bhaktikāṇḍa
note: One kāṇḍa of the Prāṇatoṣiṇī digest; compiled quotations by various original authors, work securely Rāmatoṣaṇa's compilation.

title: Prāṇatoṣiṇī (Sargakāṇḍa)
note: One kāṇḍa of the Prāṇatoṣiṇī digest; compiled quotations by various original authors, work securely Rāmatoṣaṇa's compilation.

title: Śrītattvanidhi
current_author: Kṛṣṇarāja Wodeyar III (Mummaḍi Kṛṣṇarāja Woḍeyar) of Mysore
primary_author: Kṛṣṇarāja Wodeyar III of Mysore (compiler/patron, traditional)
source: web:https://www.wisdomlib.org/definition/shritattvanidhi | secondary:cleaned_From entertainment to enlightenment..._May-Ying Mary Ngai, 2010.txt
note: Royal/nominal authorship: like King Bhoja's corpus, court-produced compendium compiled under (and ascribed to) the Maharaja; draws on earlier source texts. Status downgraded from secure to traditional and role refined to compiler; no distinct named co-author found so not flagged as conflation.

title: Bhāvanāsārasaṃgraha
note: Devotional anthology/compilation; Kṛṣṇadāsa functions as compiler drawing on earlier līlā works. Attribution traditional within Gauḍīya tradition; not critically verified outside the edition.

title: Devanandanasiṃha: Śāktapramoda
note: Not an original single-author work: it is a compilation/digest of sixteen distinct (largely anonymous, traditionally Śākta/Tantric) ritual manuals. Deva Nandan Singh is the compiler/redactor, not the author of the constituent Tantras. Name also given as Rājanandana/Rajanandan Siṃha.

title: Harināmacintāmaṇi
note: Single, securely attributed modern (late 19th c.) Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava author. No conflation; no dispute.

title: Śvānastavakāvya
source: secondary:cleaned_Indian and Tibetan Studies (Collectanea Marpurgensia Indologica et Tibetica)_Dragomir Dimitrov, et.al. ed., 2002.txt
note: Contemporary (20th c.) named Sri Lankan Sanskrit poet; secure single authorship, edited/translated by Bhikkhu Pāsādika (editor, not author).
